subject: Ancient History
question_text: The prehistoric period in the early development of human beings is commonly known as the ________ [SSC MTS 02/05/2023]
solution:
(A) Old stone age
Old stone age or Palaeolithic age (5 lakh BC - 10,000 BC). People were hunters and food gatherers. Tools - choppers, clevers, hand-axes based on flake technology. Palaeolithic sites - Sohan (Punjab), Belan Valley (UP), Didwana (Raj.), Hungsi (Karnataka), Patne, Inamgaon, Nevada (Maharashtra), Bhimbetka (MP) etc.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: In India, the practice of erecting megaliths began about ________ ago. [SSC MTS 15/05/2023]
solution:
(A) 3000 years
Megaliths - large stones used to construct monuments or graves. Cairn Circle - megaliths around the grave in circular shape. Dolmens - large flat stones placed horizontally upright on burial sites. Menhirs - erect standing stones.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Iron age is so named because during this time iron mostly replaced ________ in implements and weapons, beginning in the Middle East and South-eastern Europe. [SSC CGL 27/07/2023]
solution:
(C) bronze
Bronze age (2500 - 1750 BC) also known as Indus valley civilization. Iron Age (1000 - 500 BC) during the Later Vedic period. Iron mostly replaced bronze in implements and weapons. Bronze is an alloy of copper and tin.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Pottery was first traceable to which period of ancient Indian history? [SSC PHASE XI 28/06/2023]
solution:
(C) Neolithic
Neolithic Age (7000 - 1000 BC) or New stone age. Food producers and practiced agriculture. Black and red colour pottery. People owned property. Neolithic Sites- Mehrgarh (Baluchistan) evidence of wheat, barley, cotton; it was the oldest site. Burzahom (pit dwellings and burial with dog) and Gufkral (cave of potters) in Kashmir. Koldihwa (UP) rice evidence, Garo Hills in Meghalaya, Chirand (bone tools) in Bihar.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Bhimbetka, a noted site of the Palaeolithic period, is located in which state of India? [SSC CGL TIER-II 02/03/2023]
solution:
(D) Madhya Pradesh
Bhimbetka rock paintings were found by V.S. Wakankar in 1957. It is a UNESCO site (2003). Located in Vindhya ranges in MP. These paintings span Palaeolithic, Mesolithic, Neolithic and historical periods.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: During which of the following period Ostriches were found in India? [SSC CGL 02/12/2022]
solution:
(D) Palaeolithic
Ostrich evidence (egg shells) first found in Patne, Maharashtra; a lower Palaeolithic site.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: In which of the following sites of jadeite stone was found? [SSC CGL 03/12/2022]
solution:
(D) Daojali Hading
Daojali Hading is a Neolithic site in Dima Hasao district of Assam. Even pestles, mortars and stone tools were also found here along with jadeite Stone. Mahagara (UP) and Hallur (Karnataka) are Neolithic sites for millet cultivation) and Mehrgarh (Baluchistan) are Neolithic sites.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: In which among the following periods Catal Huyuk was one of the most famous sites? [SSC CGL 05/12/2022]
solution:
(A) Neolithic
Catal Huyuk was the first human proto city, located in Turkey. It is a neolithic site, where houses were built of mud bricks and wood.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The duration of the Mesolithic period is from about ________ years ago to ________ years ago. [SSC CGL 12/12/2022]
solution:
(C) 12000, 10000
Mesolithic Period - also known as Microlithic period. Warmer climate and increased flora and fauna. People were hunters and herders. Mesolithic sites - Langhnaj, near Mehsana (Gujarat); Bhimbetka near Bhopal and Adamgarh (MP), Chopani Mando near Belan Valley (Allahabad, UP), Bagor (Raj.) earliest evidence of domestication of animals. Sanganakallu (Karnataka), Tuticorin (Tamil Nadu).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: A celt is ________ from the Neolithic period. [SSC CGL 20/04/2022]
solution:
(C) a tool
Celt is a pre-historic tool; a long, thin, stone or bronze tool similar to hoe or axe.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: 'Microliths' were ________ used by early humans. [SSC MTS 06/10/2021]
solution:
(B) stone tools
Microliths were stone tools of small size made up of flint or chert, used to make tools like saws, sickles and arrow heads. These are found in the Mesolithic age.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following is a Palaeolithic site of archaeological importance in India? [SSC MTS 06/10/2021]
solution:
(C) Hunasagi
Burzahom, Mehrgarh and Chirand are Neolithic sites. Hunasagi near Yadgiri district (Karnataka) is a Palaeolithic site.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The Iron Age site named Tekwada is located in ________. [SSC MTS 17/10/2024]
solution:
(D) Maharashtra
Tekwada is located in Jalgaon, Maharashtra.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following is NOT associated with the Iron Age in India? [SSC MTS 08/10/2024]
solution:
(A) Mehrauli iron pillar
Iron age is dated around 1000 BC; the Mehrauli Iron Pillar is dated to the Gupta period (375-415 AD). Smelting is a process of extraction of metal from its ore by heating and melting. Northern black polished ware and painted grey ware pottery is associated with Iron age.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following periods in India is also associated with the Iron Age? [SSC MTS 13/11/2024]
solution:
(C) Later Vedic Age
Palaeolithic and neolithic age is stone age. Harappan age associated with bronze. The Aryans of Later Vedic period had knowledge of iron. In their texts it is mentioned as "Lohit Ayas" and "Krishna (Shyama) Ayas"; means black metal "Iron".

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The Megalithic site ________ is a site on the river Ghod, a tributary of the Bhima. [SSC MTS 14/10/2024]
solution:
(A) Inamgaon
It is a post-Harappan Chalcolithic site of Maharashtra. It belongs to Jorwe culture. Chalcolithic Age (3500 - 1000 BC): Copper, it was the first metal discovered by humans. Copper mines - Khetri mines and Ganeshwar in Rajasthan, Malanjkhand in MP. Chalcolithic sites - Ahar and Gilund (Raj.) near Banas Valley, Chirand (Bihar), Malwa, Kayatha (Kalisindh) and Eran in MP. Jorwe in Maharashtra. Under Jorwe - Newasa, Daimabad, Chandoli, Inamgaon, Nashik, Navadatoli.

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Which of the following statements is/are true about the Iron Age? [SSC MTS 23/10/2024]
I. The establishment of large cities took place on the basis of sizeable surplus.
II. The ceramic associated with this age was Painted Grey Ware.
III. The Indus Valley Civilization flourished during the Iron Age.
solution:
(B) Only 1 and 2
Indus Valley Civilisation belongs to the Bronze Age.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following is the correct chronological sequence of the pre-historic period of human activities and civilization? [RRB NTPC 11/02/2021]

options.a: Neolithic Period, Mesolithic Period, Palaeolithic Period
options.b: Palaeolithic Period, Mesolithic Period, Neolithic Period
options.c: Mesolithic Period, Neolithic Period, Palaeolithic Period
options.d: Metal Age Period, Mesolithic Period, Palaeolithic Period

solution:
(B) Palaeolithic Period, Mesolithic Period, Neolithic Period
Palaeolithic Age (5 lakh - 10000 BC), Mesolithic (12000 - 10000 BC), Neolithic (7000 - 1000 BC)

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following metals was first used by man? [RRB NTPC 19/03/2021]
solution:
(D) Copper
[ref Q-16]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which one among the following statements with reference to the 'Neolithic Period' is not correct? [CAPF 2023]

options.a: The characteristic features of the period included ground and polished stone tools, agriculture, animal domestication and pottery.
options.b: This period is also known as the 'New Stone Age'.
options.c: The characteristic features appeared almost at the same time in various parts of the subcontinent.
options.d: Earliest evidence of agriculture is found from Mehrgarh around 8000 BCE.

solution:
(C) The characteristic features appeared almost at the same time in various parts of the subcontinent.
The Neolithic period or New Stone Age included polished stone tools, agriculture (earliest evidence Mehrgarh around 8000 BC) and domestication. These features did not appear simultaneously across the subcontinent. It appeared very late in some regions of the subcontinent.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which one of the following is not a pottery type? [CAPF 2023]
solution:
(B) CCE
Types of pottery - Ochre Coloured Pottery (OCP) 2000 - 1200 BC. Black and Red Ware (BRW) 1200 - 1000 BC. Painted Grey Ware (PGW) 1000 - 600 BC. Northern Black Polished Ware (NBPW) 600 - 300 BC.

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Consider the following statements with reference to Paleolithic tools: [CDS II 2023]
1. Isampur was a well-known center of stone tool manufacture located in Gulbarga district of Karnataka and was situated along a small seasonal stream known as Kamta Halla.
2. A Paleolithic blade is a flake tool, the length of which is more than twice its width.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
solution: (C) Both 1 and 2

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Consider the following statements about the Jorwe culture of the Deccan: [CDS I 2024]
1. It covers, practically, the whole of modern Maharashtra except the coastal Konkan district.
2. The Pravara Godavari valleys seem to have been the nuclear zone.
3. The large sites of this culture yield evidence of shifting agriculture.
Which of the following statements given above is/are correct?
solution:
(B) 1 and 2 only
The large sites of Jorwe culture do not provide evidence of shifting agriculture. Instead, they indicate a more settled agricultural lifestyle with evidence of permanent settlements.

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
With reference to the Mesolithic art in India, consider the following statements: [CDS II 2024]
1. Alexander Burns discovered the first rock paintings in India at Sohagighat in Uttar Pradesh.
2. Bhimbetka rock paintings were discovered when archaeologist V.S. Wakankar noticed the rocks out of a train window while travelling from Bhopal to Itarsi and got off at the nearest railway station to explore the site.
3. The paintings and engravings do not exist in the same shelter in the rock art of Orissa.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
solution:
(B) 2 Only
Alexander Burns did not discovered the first rock paintings in India. The first rock paintings in India were the Bhimbetka rock paintings in MP discovered by V.S. Wakankar.
The paintings and engravings often coexist in the same shelter in the rock art of Orissa.

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Consider the following pairs:
Historical Place | Well-known for
1. Burzahom | Rock-cut shrines
2. Chandraketugarh | Terracotta art
3. Ganeshwar | Copper artefacts
Which of the pairs given above is/are correctly matched? [IAS 2021]
solution:
(D) 2 and 3
Chandraketugarh can be found close to Bidyadhari river in West Bengal. It is famous for Terracotta art.
Ganeshwar is located near copper mines of Sikar-Jhunjhunu area in Rajasthan. It is a Chalcolithic site and known for excavations revealing numerous copper artifacts.
Burzahom is a Neolithic site in Kashmir. Skeleton evidence of burial of dog with their masters. People lived in lake side pits. It is not known for Rock-cut shrines.

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Consider the following Information: [IAS 2024]
Archaeological Site | State | Description
1. Chandraketugarh | Odisha | Trading Port town
2. Inamgaon | Maharashtra | Chalcolithic
3. Mangadu | Kerala | Megalithic site
4. Salihundam | Andhra Pradesh | Rock-cut cave shrines
In which of the above rows is the given information correctly matched?
solution:
(B) 2 and 3
Inamgaon is a Chalcolithic site in Maharashtra. Mangadu in Kollam, Kerala known for Megaliths representing burial monuments.
Salihundam is located in Andhra Pradesh is famous for Buddhist stupas, viharas and other Buddhist remains and not Rock cut shrines. Chandraketugarh is located in West Bengal.

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Consider the following archaeological sites:
1. Burzahom
2. Sarai Khola
3. Kurnool
4. Attirampakkam
Which one of the following represents the locations of the above sites starting from north to south? [CBI 2023]
solution:
(B) 1-2-3-4
Burzahom (Kashmir), Sarai Khola (Punjab, Pak), Kurnool (Andhra Pradesh) and Attirampakkam (Tamil Nadu).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The word India came from the Indus, called ________ in Sanskrit. [SSC CGL 12/12/2022]
solution:
(D) Sindhu
Rigveda mentions 'Sapta Sindhu'. Indus (Sindhu), Jhelum (Vitasta), Chenab (Asikni), Ravi (Parushini), Beas (Bipasha), Sutlej (Shutudri) and Saraswati.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following architectural elements is excluded from the construction sites of the Indus Valley Civilisation? [SSC MTS 30/09/2024]
solution:
(C) Iron Slabs
Indus Valley Civilization (Bronze Age) (2600 BC-1700 BC) near Indus river. Name- John Marshal (director of Archaeological Survey of India). Bronze (alloy of copper and tin) tools were found. Urban civilization. Gypsum Mortar, baked bricks found in Mohenjodaro. Sun dried bricks - Dholavira.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following Indus Valley sites is now located in Pakistan? [SSC MTS 11/09/2023]
solution:
(A) Harappa
Harappa (River- Ravi) first IVC site excavated by Daya Ram Sahni in 1921. Findings - 6 granaries in a row (circular brick platform), Virgin goddess seals, Coffin burial, stone symbols of Lingam, Wheat and Barley etc.
Lothal (Gujarat)(river - Bhogava) is an ancient port, Dockyard, terracotta ship, fire altar, joint burial etc. Alamgirpur (UP)- eastern most site of IVC. Kalibangan (Raj)- Black bangles, 7 fire altars, ploughed fields, houses with wells, etc.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: According to the archaeologists, in Harappan cities the part to the west was smaller and higher, was known as________. [SSC CGL 06/12/2022]
solution:
(A) Citadel
Citadel - Higher town (Western side) also known as Acropolis. City without Citadel - Chanhudaro (Indus), City divided in 3 parts - Dholavira (Gujarat) giant water reservoirs found here.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Mohenjodaro belongs to which of the following civilizations? [SSC MTS 13/06/2023]
solution:
(B) Indus Valley Civilization.
Mohenjo-Daro (Mound of the Dead) (Indus) is excavated by R.D. Banerjee in 1922. It is located in Larkana district in Sindh (Pak). Largest city of IVC. Evidences - Great Bath, Great Granary (largest building), Pashupati seals, Dancing girl statue (Bronze) in Tribhanga mudra, Bearded man (steatite), Unicorn seals, Mother goddess (clay), etc. UNESCO site 1980.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Mohenjodaro site of Harappan civilization is situated on the bank of which river? [SSC CPO 04/10/2023]
solution:
(D) Indus River
Ghaghara river - Kalibanga, Banawali (barley evidence), Rakhigarhi (Haryana) (largest IVC site in India, terracotta wheel and toys).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: What was the shape of the Great Bath of Mohenjo Daro? [SSC MTS 15/07/2022]
solution:
(A) Rectangular
Great Bath - Ritual bathing and religious purposes, made of burnt bricks, located in Citadel, largest in Mohenjodaro.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following statements is true about the Great Bath of the Indus Valley Civilisation? [SSC MTS 08/09/2023]

options.a: It was used for grain storage.
options.c: It was used as the water tank of the city.
options.d: It was probably used for religious purpose.

solution: (D) It was probably used for religious purpose.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The Great Granary was one of the largest granaries in the ________. [SSC MTS 11/11/2024]
solution:
(B) Harappan Civilization
Great Granary found in Mohenjodaro is the largest building of IVC. It is of rectangular shape and used to store grains.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following sites of Indus valley civilization is not on the bank of river Indus? [SSC CHSL 20/10/2020]
solution:
(C) Ropar
Ropar (Punjab-Ind) is situated on Satluj River. Evidences - Burial with dog, oval burial pits, copper tools, Vina Vadini (lady playing vina), etc.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following Harappan sites is located in Afghanistan? [SSC MTS 12/10/2021]
solution:
(A) Shortughai
Shortughai and Mundigaq are Indus sites found in Afghanistan. Shortughai was a trading colony. Evidence - Traces of canals, bull carts made of clay and valuable blue stone called Lapis Lazuli.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Harappa, one of the sites of the Harappan culture, was found in which of the following states of pre-independent India? [SSC MTS 30/11/2024]
solution:
(A) Punjab
Harappa is located near Sahiwal district in Punjab, Pakistan on the banks of Ravi river. [refer Q-3]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Most of the Harappan sites are primarily divided into ________ parts. [SSC MTS 30/10/2024]
solution:
(A) Two Parts
Citadel (higher town) on the western side, also known as Acropolis and Lower town. City without a citadel is Chanhudaro.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which bonding material was mainly used to join the stones or bricks of 'The Great Bath' of the Harappan Civilisation? [SSC MTS 29/10/2024]
solution: (B) Gypsum Mortar

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The remains of Great Bath have been found from the excavation at which of the following Harappan sites? [SSC MTS 08/10/2024]
solution:
(D) Mohenjodaro
[ref Q-5]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Fire altars were found in which of the following groups of Harappan cities? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 13/10/2023]
solution:
(A) Kalibangan and Lothal
Fire altars were found at the houses of Kalibangan and Lothal. These were used for religious purposes (worship Agni). [ref Q-3]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The ancient city of Dholavira, is located in which state? [SSC MTS 10/05/2023]
solution:
(D) Gujarat
Dholavira is located in Kutch (Gujarat). UNESCO World Heritage site in 2021. Giant water reservoir is found in Dholavira and is divided into 3 parts. It is located on the Tropic of Cancer.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Most Harappan seals made of ________. [SSC MTS 21/07/2022]
solution:
(C) Steatite
Other materials - terracotta, gold, agate, chert, ivory, etc. Pashupati (proto shiva), Buffalo, Elephant, Tiger Rhino (Unicorn), Deer, boat etc. were found in seals.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following Harappan cities was found without a citadel? [SSC MTS 04/11/2024]
solution:
(C) Chanhudaro
It was discovered in 1931 by NG Majumdar. Only city without citadel. Located on Indus river in Sindh (Pak). It was a manufacturing site - craft production, metal equipments, bead making, seal making etc.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The Citadel area has a huge mud________ platform, which is 6 m high and underlies all the construction. [SSC MTS 13/11/2024]
solution: (A) Brick

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following were NOT a part of a citadel in Harappan architecture? [SSC CPO 10/11/2022]
solution:
(D) Residential buildings for masses.
These buildings were made in the Lower town.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following types of house remains were found in Mehrgarh site of Harappan civilization? [SSC CGL 09/12/2022]
solution:
(C) Square or Rectangular
Mehrgarh (Baluchistan) is a Neolithic site in Pakistan. The earliest evidence of houses and agriculture (wheat, barley, cotton, dates).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The Harappan brought Gold from present-day________ in India. [SSC CGL 08/12/2022]
solution:
(C) Karnataka
Metals such as copper, bronze, gold, silver, lead etc were known except Iron. Silver from Afghanistan, Iraq, Iran, Lead from Kashmir and Rajasthan, Copper from Rajasthan.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following metals was used to make weapons and tools in Harappan cities? [SSC CGL 06/12/2022]
solution:
(C) Copper
Copper and Bronze. Tools - axes, knives, fish hooks, arrows, saws, spearheads etc.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: In which of the following Indian states, Harappan cities have NOT been found? [SSC CGL 01/12/2022]
solution:
(A) Uttarakhand
India- Punjab, Haryana, Rajasthan, Western UP and Gujarat. Pakistan- Punjab, Sindh and Baluchistan.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following Harappan town was located on Khadir Beyt in the Runn of Kutch and was divided into three parts? [SSC CHSL 14/03/2023]
solution:
(D) Dholavira
[ref Q-17]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: In 1948, Ernest Mackay mentioned that in the Harappan city of Lothal, the drains for the drainage system were made of________. [SSC PHASE XI 28/06/2023]
solution: (B) Burnt

subject: Ancient History
question_text: To study the buildings and structures of the Harappan civilisation, a teacher should take his students on a field trip to which of the following states of India? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 10/12/2024]
solution: (A) Gujarat and Rajasthan

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Drains exiting from Mohenjo-Daro had________ doors that were probably closed at night to prevent vagrants or negative elements from entering the city through that access. [SSC MTS 11/11/2024]
solution:
(D) Wooden
[ref Q-5]

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Which of the following statements is/are FALSE for the Harappan town planning? [SSC MTS 01/10/2024]
1. Most of the towns have citadel in harappan civilization.
2. Every town had a temple in Harappan civilization.
3. Every building was made of sand stone in Harappan civilization.
solution:
(C) Only 2 and 3 only
Citadel - all towns except Chanhudaro. Most buildings were made up of bricks, mud, wood, and gypsum.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following statements is correct? [SSC MTS 04/11/2024]

options.a: Granaries in the Indus Valley Civilization were found in Harappa only.
options.b: The script of the Harappan culture has been deciphered by historians.
options.c: At burials in Harappan sites the dead were generally laid in pits.
options.d: Harappan culture spread only to Sindh and Punjab.

solution:
(C) At burials in Harappan sites the dead were generally laid in pits.
The script of Harappan Civilization was Pictographic and Boustrophedon (left to right in one line and right to left in next line); it is still undeciphered.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: In Harappan civilisation, which material was NOT used in the construction of buildings? [SSC MTS 13/11/2024]
solution:
(B) Iron
Iron was not discovered during IVC, it was discovered in later vedic period (1000 BC - 500 BC).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following was NOT found in the Harappan excavation sites? [SSC MTS 16/10/2024]
solution: (B) Public Hospital

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Select the correct alternative on the basis of the given statements on the Great Bath of Harappan civilisation. [SSC STENOGRAPHER 13/10/2023]
Statement I: The Great Bath was found in Mohenjo-Daro site of Harappan civilization.
Statement II: The Great Bath was lined with bricks, coated with plaster and made water-tight with a layer of natural tar.

options.a: Neither Statement I nor Statement II is true.
options.b: Both Statement I and Statement II are true.

solution:
(B) Both Statement I and Statement II are true.
[ref Q-5]

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Select the correct alternative on the basis of the given statements on the cities of Harappan civilisation. [SSC STENOGRAPHER 12/10/2023]
Statement I: Most of the Harappan cities were divided into two parts: Higher Town and Lower Town.
Statement II: The archaeologists describe the lower part of the towns as Citadel.

options.a: Both Statement I and Statement II are true.
options.d: Neither Statement I nor Statement II is true.

solution:
(C) Only Statement I is true.
Towns were divided into two parts - Higher town (Citadel) also known as acropolis and Lower town.

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Select the correct alternative of the basis of the given statements on the drainage system of the Harappan civilisation. [SSC STENOGRAPHER 12/10/2023]
Statement I: In most of the Harappan cities, the houses had open drainage systems outside.
Statement II: In the Harappan civilisation, water and garbage used to drain out from the drains located outside the houses and go into the main underground drain.

options.b: Both Statement I and Statement II are true.
options.d: Neither Statement I nor Statement II is true.

solution:
(C) Only II is true.
In Harappan Civilization the houses had underground or covered drainage system and not open drainage system.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The location of the ancient city of Taxila (Takshshila), mentioned in ancient Indian texts, was identified by [CDS II 2021]
solution:
(A) Alexander Cunningham
Also known as "father of Indian Archaeology" re-discovered Taxila (local site - Sarai Kala). It was added to UNESCO list in 1980. Taxila is located in northwestern Pakistan between Indus and Jhelum River.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Most of the mature Harappan sites in the region of Cholistan had been abandoned during [CAPF 2021]
solution:
(C) 1800 BC
Cholistan is the area in present day Pakistan. After 1800 BC Harappan civilization started to decline.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: In the citadel and lower-town areas of which one of the following Harappan cities, have several large cisterns and reservoirs been found? [CDS II 2022]
solution:
(C) Dholavira
[ref Q-17 level-1]

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Match List-I with List-II and select the correct answer using the code given below the lists: [CAPF 2023]
List-I (Harappan Site) | List-II (Location)
A. Nageshwar | 1. Uttar Pradesh
B. Alamgirpur | 2. Rajasthan
C. Kalibangan | 3. Saurashtra
D. Rakhigarhi | 4. Haryana
solution:
(A) A3 B1 C2 D4
Nageshwar (Saurashtra) is famous for major shell working. Alamgirpur (UP) is the easternmost site of IVC. Kalibangan (Raj) is on Ghaggar river, 7 fire altars, ploughed fields, black bangles found here. Rakhigarhi (Haryana) is the largest IVC site in India, terracotta wheels and toys discovered here.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which among the following was/were the shell-based production centre's of the Indus Valley Civilization? [CISF AC 2021]
solution:
(C) Balakot and Nageshwar
Balakot and Nageshwar were specialized centres for making shell objects like bangles, ladles etc. these are coastal settlements.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: In the Mesopotamian records, the Indus Valley region is addressed as [CISF AC 2021]
solution:
(A) Meluha
Dilmun and Magan were the intermediate stations between Harappa (Meluha) and Mesopotamia(Iraq)(sumerian).

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Which of the following do/does not represent a break between the Early Harappan cultures and the Mature Harappan civilization?
1. Large-scale burning at some sites
2. Abandonment of certain settlements
3. A shift in the course of the Hakra river
Select the correct answer using the code given below: [CISF AC 2023]
solution:
(D) 3 only
There was a break in the Early Harappan and Mature Harappan civilisation due to the Large scale burning in some sites such as Kot Diji which was destroyed by fire, as evidenced by a layer of burned debris. Another reason of break is abandonment of certain settlements during pre-harappan period. Around 1800 BC, settlements in sites like Cholistan were abandoned and population shifted to new settlements in Gujarat, Haryana, Uttar Pradesh, mostly around the Ganga-Yamuna region.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which one among the following statements with reference to the Harappan burial ritual is not correct? [CISF AC 2024]

options.a: At these burials, the dead were generally laid in pits.
options.b: The burial pits were made in several different ways.
options.c: Ornaments have sometimes been found placed in these burials.
options.d: Harappans did not bury mirrors with the dead.

solution:
(D) Harappans did not bury mirrors with the dead.
Harappans bury some of their dead with copper and bronze mirrors, other items- Bangles, Beads, Pottery, Shell items, Jewellery, etc. The burial pits were of different shapes.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which one of the following ancient towns is well-known for its elaborate system of water harvesting and management by building a series of dams and channelizing water into connected reservoirs? [IAS 2021]
solution:
(A) Dholavira
[ref Q-17 level-1]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which one of the following Harappan sites was a specialised centre for making shell objects? [NDA I 2021]
solution:
(B) Balakot
Kot-Diji was excavated by Fazal Ahmed Khan in 1953 near Indus river in Sindh (Pakistan). Amri by NG Majumdar in 1935 near Indus river in Sindh (Pakistan).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: At which among the following Harappan sites are fire altars found? [NDA II 2022]
solution:
(A) Kalibangan
[ref Q-4]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which one among the following motifs does not appear on Harappan seals? [CDS I 2025]
solution:
(C) Lion
Harappan seals were made up of Steatite and it usually have a line in writing, probably containing the name and title of the owner. Animals seen in seals - Buffalo, Elephant, Tiger, Rhino, Deer, Crocodile. Animals not depicted in seals - Cow, Horse, Camel and Lion.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following is the oldest text of ancient India? [SSC PHASE XI 30/06/2023]
solution:
(C) Rigveda
Rigveda is made up of 10 mandalas, 1028 hymns and 10600 verses. Oldest and Largest. Gayatri Mantra by Vishwamitra is mentioned in 3rd Mandala. Deities were Indra, Agni, Vishnu, Varuna. The hymns were recited by Hotri.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The oldest Veda is the Rigveda, composed about ________ years ago. [SSC GD 23/02/2024]
solution:
(B) 3500
It was composed during Early Vedic Age (1500 BC - 1000 BC). Later Vedic Age (1000 BC - 600 BC) Rig Veda starts with the line "Agnimele Purohitam". Added to UNESCO in 2007.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: India was named 'Bharat' after the name of the Bharat clan. In which of the following Vedas is this clan first mentioned? [SSC PHASE XI 27/06/2023]
solution:
(D) Rig Veda
It mentions the Bharata Clan in 3rd and 7th Mandala.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: How many stages of life did early Hindu philosophy determine? [RRB GROUP D 30/08/2022]
solution:
(A) four
Four stages of life, also known as Ashramas are Brahmacharya, Grahastha, Vanaprastha and Sannyasa.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The Early Vedic economy was primarily based on ________ activity. [SSC MTS 15/10/2024]
solution:
(A) Pastoral
Early Vedic - Pastoral, Later Vedic (Iron Age) - Agricultural.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The language of the Aryan texts was: [SSC PHASE XI 03/08/2022]
solution:
(D) Sanskrit
Language of Indo-Aryans. Vedic texts were written in Sanskrit.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: How many Vedangas are there in total? [SSC PHASE XI 20/10/2020]
solution:
(B) Six
Vedangas (tools to understand vedas) - Shiksha (Phonetics), Kalpa (Practices/ritual), Vyakaran (Grammar), Nirukta (Etymology/words), Jyotisha (astrology), Chhanda (Poetic).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: How many Mandalas are there in the Rigveda? [SSC PHASE XI 02/08/2022]
solution: (D) Ten

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Hymns of Rigveda are also known as ________. [RRB GROUP D 06/09/2022]
solution:
(A) Sukta
1028 Hymns or Suktas are in the form of dialogue.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The main collections of Vedic Hymns are called ________. [SSC CGL 18/08/2021]
solution:
(C) Samhita
Subdivisions - (i) Samhita: collection of Hymns, (ii) Brahmanas: commentaries, sacrifices and rituals, (iii) Aranyakas: Hermits (live in Jungle), (iv) Upanishads: also known as Vedanta are for Knowledge gain.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Sanskrit is a part of a family of languages known as ________. [SSC MTS 19/05/2023]
solution: (A) Indo-European

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Rigveda is the earliest specimen of: [SSC MTS 08/10/2024]
solution: (B) Indo European Language

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The Rigveda is the holiest of the four Vedas, consisting of 10 chapters (called Mandalas) of ________ hymns of 10,600 verses. [RRB GROUP D 26/08/2022]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who among the following was 'Senani' in the Rig Vedic period? [SSC MTS 14/10/2024]
solution:
(D) A war hero
Other terms - Purohit (chief priest), Rajan (Head), Gopati (protector of cows), Gramani (village head), Senani (army chief), Sabha (community of privileged), Samiti (common people community), Kulapa (Head of kula-family), Bhagadugha (revenue officer), Spasas (spies), Suta (Charioteer), Akshavapa (accountant).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Asikni, which was a part of Sapta-Sindhu, is the name of which of the following rivers in present times? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 12/10/2023]
solution:
(C) Chenab
Rigveda mentions 'Sapta Sindhu'. Indus (Sindhu/Singi Khamban), Jhelum (Vitasta/Hydaspes), Chenab (Askini), Ravi (Parushini/Airavati), Beas (Bipasha), Sutlej (Shutudri) and Saraswati.

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Consider the following statements in the context of the Vedas. [SSC MTS 22/10/2024]
1. The Vedas are called Shruti.
2. The Rigveda consists of ten Mandalas.
solution:
(D) Both 1 and 2
[ref Q-1]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following Vedas depicts the life of the early Vedic people of India and has a collection of 1028 hymns divided into 10 mandalas? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 12/10/2023]
solution:
(D) Rig Veda
[ref Q-1]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following Vedas is found in two recensions, black and white, and is full of rituals to be performed publicly or individually? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 13/10/2023]
solution:
(C) Yajurveda
It is a collection of mantras and rituals. Divided into 2 recensions: Shukla (white) contains Shatapatha Brahmana and Krishna (black). Sacrificial rituals are also mentioned in Yajurveda. Upanishads - Brihadaranyaka (oldest Upanishad) and Katha (story of Nachiketa).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following Gods acquires a new epithet 'Lord of the Plough' in the later Vedic period? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 13/10/2023]
solution:
(D) Indra
Agriculture became main profession in later Vedic age. The plough was called as "Sira". Varuna - "God of Water". Indra was also called Purandar (destroyer of forts). Other gods were Prithvi, Agni, Soma, Vayu.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The Vedic Aryans lived in the area called Sapt-Sindhu, which means area drained by seven rivers. One of the rivers among the seven is Jhelum. What was its ancient name? [SSC CGL 14/07/2023]
solution:
(D) Vitasta
[ref Q-15]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: A wealthy person in the early Vedic period was known as ________. [SSC CGL 14/07/2023]
solution:
(C) Gomat
It means possession of Cows. Cow was considered as "wealth decider" and called "Aghanya". Gavishthi - Battle of Cows.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: In which of the following Vedas was Dasarajna war (the war of ten kings) mentioned? [SSC CGL 21/07/2023]
solution:
(C) Rigveda
Dasarajna War mentioned in 7th mandala of Rigveda. Between Bharatas king Sudas against confederation of ten kings. Bharatas emerged victorious. Fought on the banks of Ravi river (Parushni).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following varnas primarily used to do cultivation, animal husbandry and trade during the later Vedic period? [SSC CGL 25/07/2023]
solution:
(D) Vaishyas
Brahmanas - Priest class, Kshatriyas - Warrior class, Vaishyas - Agriculture, trade and merchant class, Shudras - Labour class.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the four Vedas contains a collection of magic spells and charms to fend off evil spirits and diseases? [SSC CGL 26/07/2023]
solution:
(D) Atharva Veda
Veda of magic formulae. It is divided into 20 Khandas. Satyamey Jayate is taken from Mundaka Upanishad and Vasudhaiva Kutumbakam is from Maha Upanishad, both belong to Atharva veda. Total 108 Upanishads.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The Purva Mimansa school of philosophy was founded by ________ in ancient India. [SSC PHASE XI 28/06/2023]
solution:
(D) Jamini
Schools of Philosophy: Samakhya-Kapila, Nyaya-Gautam, Vaisheshika-Kannada(atoms), Yoga-Patanjali, Uttara Mimansa - Badrayana and Purva Mimansa-Jaimini.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The iron implement site of Hastinapur was found in which present day state of India? [SSC MTS 11/07/2022]
solution:
(B) Uttar Pradesh
It was found in Meerut district of Uttar Pradesh.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: As per the Rigvedic or Early Vedic Period (1500-1000 BC), who among the following was a River Goddess? [SSC MTS 25/07/2022]
solution:
(B) Sindhu
Agni - Fire God, Arika- not vedic deity, means "enemy". Usha - Goddess of Dawn (means light or day).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: In the Rig Veda there is a hymn in the form of a dialogue between Sage Vishvamitra and two rivers that were worshipped as goddesses. Which are these rivers? [SSC CGL 13/04/2022]
solution: (D) Beas and Satluj

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following statements about the 'Gotra' practice in ancient India is true? [SSC CGL 18/04/2022]

options.a: Men and women were expected to marry within the same gotra.
options.b: People belonging to the same gotra were regarded as descendants of the person after whom the gotra was named.
options.c: Women retained their father's gotra after marriage.
options.d: Each gotra was named after a famous king.

solution: (B) People belonging to the same gotra were regarded as descendants of the person after whom the gotra was named. Marriages within the gotra are not permitted. Seven Major Gotras are Atri, Bharadwaja, Gautama, Jamadagni, Kashyap, Vashishtha, Vishwamitra. 8th Agastya added later.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following book was written by Aryabhatta? [SSC CGL 12/12/2022]
solution:
(B) Aryabhatiyam
Natya Shastra by Bharata Muni is also called 5th Veda. Dhatupatha - Bhimasena, Romaka Siddhanta - Varahamihira.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The Vedangas are Hindu auxiliary disciplines that originated in ancient times and are linked to the study of the Vedas. These are ________ in number. [SSC CGL 12/09/2024]
solution:
(A) Six
[ref Q-7]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: According to the Varna system that was prevalent in the Vedic period, which of the following was NOT a Varna? [SSC CGL 18/09/2024]
solution:
(C) Samiti
Samiti - community of common people.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: A person's Vedic approach and his relationship with society are determined by the four goals of life. Which of the following is not among these goals? [SSC CGL 17/08/2021]
solution:
(A) Sidhi
Four goals - Artha, Moksha, Dharma, Kama.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: 'Senani', 'Kulapa' and 'Gramani' are some of the functionaries mentioned primarily in which Veda? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 11/12/2024]
solution:
(C) Rigveda
[ref Q-14]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following is NOT associated with Aryabhatta? [SSC CGL 06/12/2022]

options.a: States that day and night were caused by the rotation of the earth on its axis.
options.b: Developed a scientific explanation for eclipses.
options.c: Found a way of calculating the circumference of a circle.

solution:
(D) Spoke about elaborate surgical procedures.
Sushruta (7th or 6th century) also known as "Father of Indian Surgery" or "Father of Plastic Surgery" was the one who spoke about elaborate surgical procedures.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: What is the collection of Vedic hymns or 'mantras' called? [SSC MTS 22/10/2024]
solution: (A) Samhita

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following Veda is also called the "Book of Song", "Veda of Chants" or even "Yoga of Song"? [RRB GROUP D 08/09/2022]
solution:
(A) Sama Veda
Oldest book on music. Consists of chanting melodies. 2 Upanishads - Chandogya and Kena Upanishad.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Dharamsutras contain : [SSC MTS 08/07/2022]
solution:
(D) Norms for ruler
It contains guidelines for social and individual behaviour, law and ethics.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The Atharvaveda is the collection of ________ khandas. [SSC CGL 12/12/2022]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following Vedas dealt with music? [SSC MTS 12/10/2021]
solution: (A) Sama Veda

subject: Ancient History
question_text: In which year were 30 Rig Veda manuscripts dating from 1800 to 1500 BC included in the UNESCO's Memory of the World Register? [SSC MTS 22/10/2021]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: According to the Puranas, Lord Vishnu took the shape of ________ in order to rescue the earth, which had sunk into the water? [SSC CGL 16/08/2021]
solution:
(B) A Boar
The 10 incarnations of Lord Vishnu - Matsya, Kurma, Varaha (Boar), Narasimha, Vamana, Parashurama, Rama, Krishna, Buddha and Kalki.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: According to "The Ramayana", Ratnakar was the other name of ________. [SSC CGL 17/08/2021]
solution:
(D) Valmiki
Sage Valmiki wrote Ramayana (Adi Kavya) which has 24,000 verses and is divided into seven kandas. It is the oldest epic in the world.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The words "Satyameva Jayate" on the state emblem has been taken from which Upanishad? [SSC CHSL 17/03/2020]
solution:
(D) Manduka
Mundaka Upanishad is a part of Atharva Veda.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: At one stage in the Vedic Age, the king was called "gopati" which meant "________"? [SSC CHSL 26/10/2020]
solution:
(D) Lord of Cattle
"Bhupati" - Lord of the land. "Prajapati" - Lord of People. "Rudra" - Lord of Animals.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: With reference to Vedangas, which of the following terms denotes "Ritual"? [SSC CGL 07/03/2020]
solution:
(C) Kalpa
[ref Q-7]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who among the following is supposed to compile both the Puranas and the Mahabharata? [SSC CGL 05/12/2022]
solution:
(C) Sage Vyasa
Mahabharata, the longest epic of the world, consists of 1,00,000 shlokas in 18 Parvans. Bhagavad Gita is extracted from Bhishma Parvan. There are 18 famous Puranas, Matsya Purana is the oldest.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following is false as per Vedic theory? [SSC CPO 16/03/2019]

options.a: The Kshatriya or warrier rulers came from Purusha's arms
options.b: The Vaishyas or the commoners, came from Purusha's thighs
options.c: The Brahmanas or priests, came from Purusha's ears
options.d: The Shudras or laboureres and Servants came from Purusha's feet

solution:
(C) The Brahmans or priests, came from Purusha's ears
Acc. to Purusha Sukta - Brahmans (Mouth), Kshatriya (Arms), Vaishyas (Thighs), Shudras (Feet). It is mentioned in the 10th mandala of Rigveda.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: At the banks of which of the following rivers was the Battle of Ten Kings fought in the Early Vedic Period? [CDS I 2022]
solution:
(C) Parushni
Also known as Ravi river. [ref Q-21 level I]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which one of the following statements about Rig Veda is not correct? [CDS I 2014]

options.a: Deities were worshipped through prayer and sacrificial rituals.
options.b: The Gods are presented as powerful, who could be made to intervene in the world of men via the performance of sacrifices.
options.c: The Gods were supposed to partake of the offerings as they were consumed by the fire.
options.d: The sacrifices were performed in the temples.

solution:
(D) The sacrifices were performed in the temples
The sacrifices were performed during "Yajna" in open spaces. Soma sacrifice mentioned in 9th mandala of Rigveda. Somras is a drink made from plants. Lord Soma (Lord of Plants).

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Statement I: The early Aryans, who were essentially pastoral, did not develop any political structure which could measure up to a State in either ancient or modern sense.
Statement II: Kingship was the same as tribal chiefship; the term Rajan being used for tribal chief who was primarily a military leader and who ruled over his people and not over any specified area. [CDS II 2017]

options.a: Both the statements are individually true and Statement II is the correct explanation of Statement I.
options.b: Both the statements are individually true but Statement II is not the correct explanation of Statement I.
options.c: Statement I is true but Statement II is false.
options.d: Statement I is false but Statement II is true.

solution:
(A) Both the statements are individually true Statement II is the correct explanation of Statement I
Early Aryans were pastoral community and had no political structure or state. Later, they migrated from Central Asia as tribes which were called 'janas' and formed Janapada (jana - people, pada - foot) which led to the formation of Mahajanapadas in later period.

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Which of the following characteristic's describes the nature of religion according to the Rig Veda? [CDS I 2015]
1. Rig Vedic religion can be described as naturalistic polytheism.
2. There are striking similarities between the Rig Vedic religion and the ideas in the Iranian Avesta.
3. Vedic sacrifices were conducted in the house of the priest who was called yajaman.
4. Vedic sacrifices were of two kinds those conducted by the householder and those that required ritual specialists.
solution:
(D) 1, 2 and 4 only
Yajaman is the one who conducts sacrifices and not the priest. Naturalistic polytheism - multiple gods associated with natural forces ex. Indra, Varuna, Rudra, Vayu etc. Rigveda and Iranian Zenda Avesta have many deities which are similar.
Simple sacrifices can be performed by the householder like performing offerings to Agni but some sacrifices require specialized priests to conduct the rituals, for example Ashwamedha (horse sacrifice).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: In the 10th Mandala of the Rigveda, which one of the following hymns reflects upon the marriage ceremonies? [CDS I 2018]
solution:
(B) Purusha Sukta
[ref Q-47 level 1]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which one of the following does not refer to the bow (and arrow) as a weapon of war in ancient India? [CDS I 2025]
solution:
(B) Kunta
Dhanurveda (archery warfare) is an upaveda attached to Yajurveda. It is one among the 4 upavedas along with Ayurveda, Gandharvaveda, Sthapatyaveda. Karmuka, means a bow in Sanskrit, Sharunga also known as Kodanda is bow related to Lord Vishnu and his avatars (Ram). Kunta refers to spear weapon.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which one among the following is the earliest text? [CDS I 2025]
solution:
(C) Ashtadhyayi
Ashtadhyayi, work on Sanskrit Grammar was written by Panini in Sanskrit in 500 BCE. Manusmriti (200 BC - 200 CE), also known as laws of Manu. Charak and Sushruta Samhita, work on medicine was written in 100 CE. Natyashastra of Bharata, a work on dramaturgy is written in 300 CE, it is also considered as 5th Veda.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: In the Rig Veda Samhita, use of the terms ishukrit and isukara referred to the maker of: [CDS I 2025]
solution:
(A) Arrows
Ishu refers to Arrows and Kara or Krit means maker. So, Ishukar or Ishukrit means maker of arrows.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which one of the following statements about the grammarian Katyayana is correct? [CDS I 2025]

options.a: He was a contemporary of Panini.
options.b: He was a contemporary of Patanjali.
options.c: He belonged to a period between Panini and Patanjali.
options.d: He belonged to a period later than both Panini and Patanjali.

solution:
(C) He belonged to a period between Panini and Patanjali
Munitrayas refers to Panini, Katyayana and Patanjali, who are considered the foundational figures in Sanskrit Grammar. They are collectively known as three sages or Trimuni. Panini (4th century BC) wrote Ashtadhyayi. Katyayana (300 BC) wrote Vartikas. Patanjali (200 BC) also known as Father of Yoga, he wrote Yoga Sutras and Mahabhasya.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which one of the following statements about Rig Vedic goddesses is correct? [CDS I 2025]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: According to Jain Philosophy, the term 'jina' means ________. [SSC CGL 07/03/2020]
solution:
(D) The Conqueror
Jina - who has achieved enlightenment and is liberated from the cycle of rebirth. Follower of 'jina' is called "Jain".
Jain Philosophy: Moksha - 3 Principles (Triratnas). Right Knowledge (Samyaka Jnana), Right Faith (Samyaka Darshan) and Right Conduct (Samyaka Charitra).
5 Principles of Living (Vows) - Anuvratas i.e. Ahimsa (non-violence), Satya (truth), Asteya (do not steal), Brahma-charya (Sexually monogamous) and Aparigraha (detachment).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: In which of the following states was Lord Mahavir born? [SSC MTS 14/08/2019]
solution:
(C) Bihar
Vardhaman Mahavira: Born (540 BC) in Kundagram (Vaishali, Bihar). Death (468 BC) in Pavapuri (Biharsharif, Bihar). He was also known as "Nayaputta". Father - Siddhartha (Jnathrika clan) Mother - Trishala (Lichchhavi princess), Wife - Yashoda. Daughter - Anojja Priyadarshani, whose husband Jamali was 1st disciple of Mahavira.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: At the age of ________ Mahavira left home and went to live in a forest. [SSC MTS 21/07/2022]
solution:
(D) 30
Mahavir left home along with Makkhali Gosala (founder of Ajivika Sect). He attained Enlightenment (Kaivalya) at 42 years under a Sal tree at Jrimbhikagrama near Rijupalika river. Gave 1st sermon at Pava.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Where did Lord Mahavir attain salvation? [SSC MTS 13/10/2021]
solution:
(B) Pawapuri
He achieved Moksha at the age of 72 years.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following 'Agam' describes non-violence in Jainism? [RRB GROUP D 01/09/2022]
solution:
(B) Sutrakratang Sutra
Agam - teachings of Lord Mahavira and his disciples. Antah Kradashanga Sutra - internal purification of soul.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The teachings of Mahavira and his followers were written down about 1500 years ago in the form in which they are presently available at which place? [RRB GROUP D 28/09/2022]
solution:
(B) Valabhi in Gujarat
The second Jain council in 512 AD was held at Valabhi. The Jain literature was written in Prakrit language. The first Jain council was held at Pataliputra in 298 BC, patronised by Bindusara and Leader was Sthulbhadra.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The Jain temple at Pattadakal was built by ________. [SSC MTS 02/05/2023]
solution:
(C) Rashtrakutas
Other imp Jain temples - Rock cut cave temples, Hathigumpha, Udayagiri and Khandagiri Caves (Odisha). Dilwara Jain Temple - Mount Abu (Raj.) by Vastupala Brothers. Statue of Gomateshwara / Bahubali (1st Tirthankar's son) in Shravanabelagola (Karnataka)- Mahamastakabhisheka festival is celebrated here.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Name the holy city recognized as the birthplace of the first and fourth Jain Tirthankaras? [SSC CHSL 26/10/2020]
solution:
(C) Ayodhya
1st Tirthankar - Rishabh Dev, Symbol - Bull.
4th Tirthankar - Abhinandanatha, Symbol - Monkey.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The ________ is a Jain text containing the biographies of the Jain Tirthankaras, most notably Parashvanath and Mahavira? [SSC CPO 13/03/2019]
solution:
(D) Kalpa Sutra
23rd Tirthankar- Parshavanath - birth in Varanasi and symbol - Serpent. Kalpa Sutra was written by Bhadrabahu who was the spiritual teacher of Chandragupta Maurya. Bhadrabahu leaded Digambara sect and Sthulbhadra leaded Shvetambara sect.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who was the last tirthankara of the Jainas? [SSC MTS 16/05/2023]
solution:
(A) Vardhamana Mahavira
Jainism: There were 24 Tirthankaras (Great Teachers). Founder - Rishabh Dev (1st Tirthankar), Real Founder - Vardhman Mahavira (24th Tirthankara) - Symbol "Lion". Chandraprabha was 8th and Padmaprabha was 6th Tirthankara. Rishabhadev is also known as Rishabhanatha or Adinatha.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Buddha meditated for days on end under a peepal tree at ________ where he attained enlightenment. [SSC CHSL 17/03/2023]
solution:
(B) Bodh Gaya
Gautama Buddha : Born - (563 BC) Lumbini, Nepal. Death - (483 BC) Kushinagara. Original name - Siddhartha. He belongs to Shakya clan and also known as Shakyamuni, Tathagata. Father - Suddhodana, Mother - Mahamaya (Koliya tribe). Wife - Yashodhara. Son - Rahul. He left home at the age of 29 years.
Enlightenment (Nirvana) - Uruvela (Bodh Gaya), under a bodhi tree for 49 days near Niranjana river.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: What was the pedestrian path for devotees to wave in homage to Stupa called in Buddhist architecture? [SSC MTS 05/07/2022]
solution:
(A) Medhi
Torana - Gateway, Anda - Hemispherical Dome, Chhatra - Central pillar representing royalty and protection, Vedika - Railings, Harmika - square railings on the top of the dome. "Pradakshinapath" - The circular path that surrounds the base of the Stupa.

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Which of the following statements is correct? [SSC MTS 20/07/2022]
I. Gautama Buddha belonged to a small gana known as the Sakya gana and was a Kshatriya.
II. Gautama Buddha passed away at Kusinara.
solution:
(D) Both I and II
[ref Q-11]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: At which of the following places did Lord Buddha give his first sermon on the Four Noble Truths? [SSC CGL 20/08/2021]
solution:
(D) Sarnath
1st Sermon - Sarnath, Varanasi. 4 Noble truths - (i) The Truth of Suffering, (ii) The Truth of the Cause of Suffering, (iii) The Truth of the End of Suffering, (iv) The Truth of the Path leading to the End of Suffering.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The First Buddhist Council is said to have been patronised by: [SSC MTS 13/10/2021]
solution:
(C) Ajatashatru
1st Buddhist Council - In 483 BC in Rajgriha (Bihar) presided over by Mahakashyapa. The council compiled the Vinaya Pitaka and Sutta Pitaka.
2nd Buddhist council - In 383 BC in Vaishali (Bihar), patronised by Kalashoka, Presidency - Sabakami.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: On which city was the third Buddhist council held? [SSC CPO 13/12/2019]
solution:
(C) Pataliputra
3rd Buddhist Council - In 250 BC, Patronised by Ashoka in Pataliputra (Bihar) and Presidency - Moggaliputta Tissa.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which Kushana ruler was famous in history as a great patron of Buddhism and organized the fourth Buddhist Council? [SSC CGL 25/07/2023]
solution:
(C) Kanishka
4th Buddhist Council - In 72 AD, Patronised by Kanishka and Presidency - Vasumitra, in Kundalvana Kashmir. Buddhism was divided into two sects - Hinayana and Mahayana. Hinayana did not believe in idol worship, language - Pali, mostly in Thailand, Sri Lanka, Myanmar etc. and Mahayana did believe in idol worship, language - Sanskrit mostly in China, Japan, South Korea etc.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: ________'s foster mother Mahapajapati Gotami was the first woman to be ordained as a Bhikkhuni. [SSC CHSL 05/08/2021]
solution:
(B) Buddha
Gautam Buddha is considered "Light of Asia".
1st teacher : Alara Kalama , 2nd teacher : Udraka Ramputra.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The earliest Buddhist texts were written in: [SSC MTS 22/10/2021]
solution:
(A) Pali
Majorly written in Pali language. Tripitakas were written in Pali language.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following locations is famous for having a hemispherical dome-structured monument? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 13/10/2023]
solution:
(A) Sanchi
Sanchi Stupa (MP)(oldest stupa) was built by King Ashoka in 3rd century BC and sandstone carving was done by Shunga dynasty. It was first discovered by Henry Taylor in 1818. It is divided into 4 gateways. Chethiyagiri Vihara - A festival celebrated in Sanchi.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following is the largest Stupa in India? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 13/10/2023]
solution:
(A) Kesariya Stupa
Kesariya Stupa (Bihar) discovered by Colin Mackenzie and excavated by K.K. Muhammad. Shanti Stupa (white colour) is in Ladakh.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which is the oldest Stupa of the Buddhist Period? [SSC MTS 04/09/2023]
solution:
(D) Sanchi Stupa
[ref Q-20]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: One of the most prominent Buddhist structures in India, ________ Stupa at Sarnath was constructed by the great Mauryan king, Ashoka? [SSC CGL 10/06/2019]
solution:
(B) Dhamek
Built by Ashoka in Sarnath, Varanasi where Buddha gave 1st sermon. Dhamek Stupa is cylindrical in shape. Dhauli stupa and Lalitgiri Stupa is in Odisha, Bharhut Stupa (MP).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who among the following has written the biography of Buddha, Buddhacharita? [SSC CPO 04/10/2023]
solution:
(A) Ashvaghosha
Buddhacharita and Jataka tales are written in Sanskrit language. Ashvaghosha was a member of the court of Kanishka.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The Shailendra kings who founded their empire in South-east Asia in the 8th Century AD were the followers of ________. [SSC CGL 14/07/2023]
solution:
(A) Buddhism
Shailendra kings constructed the famous Borobudur temple in Java (Indonesia), they were followers of Mahayana Buddhism.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: With reference to Buddhist architecture, what does chaitya mean? [SSC MTS 09/05/2023]
solution:
(C) Prayer Hall
Prayer Hall or Place of worship. Vihara - place of residence. Stupa - place where relics of Buddha have been placed.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following structure is associated with Buddhism? [SSC MTS 10/05/2023]
solution:
(D) Stupa
Pallava style of architecture found in South India. Mandapa is a pillared hall and Shikhara is the top of the building in Hindu temples.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The rules made for the Buddhist "Sangha" were written down in a book called ________. [SSC MTS 12/05/2023]
solution:
(C) Vinaya pitaka
Sutta pitaka - Buddha's teachings, Vinaya pitaka - Monastic courts and rules, Abhidhamma pitaka - Interpretation on Sutta.
Milindpanha - dialogue between King Meander I (Milind) and Nagasena written in Pali.
Triratnas of Buddhism - Buddha, Dhamma (religion), Sangha (community of monks).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: In Buddhist architecture, which among the following are meditation cells and living quarters for the monks? [SSC MTS 19/05/2023]
solution:
(A) Viharas
Pietra dura or Parchin Kari, is a decorative art form. [ref Q-26]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Ramabhar Stupa, which marks the site where Lord Buddha was cremated, is situated in which state of India? [SSC MTS 07/07/2022]
solution:
(D) Uttar Pradesh
Ramabhar Stupa - Kushinara (UP), mentioned in Mahaparinibbana Sutta (Pali Canon), discovered by British archaeologist Carlleyle (1876).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Bharhut Stupa depict the Jataka tales and stories. It is located in which of the following states? [SSC MTS 14/07/2022]
solution:
(B) Madhya Pradesh
Jataka Tales is about previous births of Buddha in both human and animal forms. Bharhut Stupa was built by Ashoka and later expanded by Shunga Dynasty.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following is NOT a part of Buddhist stupas? [SSC MTS 11/07/2022]
solution:
(B) Gopura
Gopura - Entrance tower of Gateway to a Hindu Temple in South India. [ref Q-12]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The Lion Capital was built to commemorate the historical event of ________ of Buddha. [SSC MTS 13/07/2022]
solution:
(D) First Sermon
The Lion Capital of Ashoka was built in Sarnath during 250 BC to commemorate 1st sermon of Buddha. Important symbols of Buddha's life - Lotus/Bull - birth, Horse - House renunciation (Mahabhinishkramana), Bodhi tree - Enlightenment (Nirvana), Wheel - 1st Sermon (Dharmachakrapravartana) and Stupa - Death (Mahaparinirvana).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: 'Chethiyagiri Vihara' is a ________ festival celebrated in Sanchi. [SSC MTS 14/10/2021]
solution:
(D) Buddhist
[ref Q-20]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following Buddhist sites is located in Uttar Pradesh? [SSC CGL 13/08/2021]
solution:
(B) Sarnath
Karle, famous for rock cut caves, is in Maharashtra. Amaravati (Amaravati stupa) is in Andhra Pradesh.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The 'Gandhara' School of Art was influenced by the art from which of the following European countries? [SSC CPO 23/11/2020]
solution:
(D) Greece
The Gandhara School of Art has many similarities with art of Greece and Rome. It flourished during rule of Kanishka.

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
With reference to Ajivikas, consider the following statements.
a) Makkhali Gosala was the prominent teacher of this sect.
b) Ajivikas believed in fatalism.
Which of the above statements is/are correct? [SSC CGL 23/09/2024]
solution:
(B) Both a and b
Ajivika sect founded by Makhal Gosala. Ajivikas believed in extreme fatalism (everything is determined by fate) and Asceticism (living extreme forms of life by abstinence from worldly pleasures).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following Buddhist sages had initiated the Indo-Greek king Menander into Buddhism? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 10/12/2024]
solution:
(A) Nagasen
Their dialogues were recorded in "Milind Panha" (Questions of Milinda).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The location of 'Dhamek Stupa' is: [SSC PHASE XII 24/06/2024]
solution:
(D) Sarnath
[ref Q-23]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which material was largely used to build the stupa at Piprahwa in Uttar Pradesh? [SSC MTS 08/10/2024]
solution:
(A) Bricks
Sandstone - Sanchi stupa and Dhamek Stupa. Amaravati stupa is made up of Amaravati marbles.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Sanchi stupa was rediscovered in the year ________ by General Henry Taylor. [SSC MTS 29/10/2024]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: During which centuries did several Buddhist pilgrims and scholars travel to China on the historic 'silk route'? [SSC MTS 01/10/2024]

options.c: First, second and third centuries AD

solution:
(C) First, second and third centuries AD
Mahayana Buddhism - China. Silk Route - it connects China with Central Asia and Europe through India. Kanishka opened Silk route for Indians.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who discovered the Bharhut stupa? [SSC MTS 18/10/2024]
solution:
(B) Alexander Cunningham
In Madhya Pradesh (1873).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who excavated the Chira Tope stupa at Taxila? [SSC MTS 23/10/2024]
solution:
(C) John Marshall
Chira Tope Stupa (Dharmarajika Stupa) : Taxila (Pakistan). James Mill wrote - "A History of British India" book. James Princep deciphered Ashokan rock edicts.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: In the Great Stupa at Sanchi there are ________ gateways known as 'Toranas' at the cardinal points to the compass. [SSC MTS 08/10/2024]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who among the following was a contemporary of Buddha, but is not considered the first founder of the religion? [SSC MTS 05/11/2024]
solution:
(B) Mahavira Swami
Rishabhadev (1st Tirthankara) and Arishtanemi (22nd Tirthankara) are the only two mentioned in Vedas. [ref Q-2,10]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The Chaitya and Viharas were constructed for the monks of which religion? [SSC MTS 19/08/2019]
solution:
(C) Buddhism
Chaitya - Place of worship (Prayer Hall) and Vihara (Place of residence).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Lomas Rishi Cave, constructed for Buddhist monks, is located in which state? [SSC MTS 22/08/2019]
solution:
(D) Bihar
Lomas Rishi Cave is a rock cut cave in the Barabar Hills of Bihar.

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Consider the following statements with reference to Jainism :
1. Consider the following statements with reference to Jainism :
1. Vardhamana Mahavira attained Kaivalyajnana outside the town of Jrimbhikagrama, on the banks of river Rijupalika and in the field of a householder named Samaga.
2. The state when the Karma particles actually begin to flow towards the Jiva to bind it is known as Asrava.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct? [CAPF 2023]
solution:
(C) Both 1 and 2
In Jain Philosophy, Asrava is the term for the inflow of karma particles into the soul, which prevents it from achieving liberation.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who among the following was not a Jain Acharya? [CDS II 2021]
solution:
(B) Khema
Khema was a beautiful princess who married to King Bimbisara of Magadha, Khema later entered monastic life under the Buddha as Bhikkhuni. Bhadrabahu was the leader of the Digambar sect of Jainism. Acharya Haribhadra Suri was a Shwetambara Jain leader and Siddhasena Divakara was also Jain Acharya and wrote books about Jain Philosophy.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which one of the following statements about the Shvetambara Sect of Jainism is not correct? [CDS II 2023]

options.a: It refers to monks, who wear white clothes.
options.b: It refers to monks, who migrated to the south of Vindhyas in 4th century BCE.
options.c: They recognized the existence of canonical texts including the 12 Angas.
options.d: They believed that women can also attain salvation.

solution:
(B) It refers to the monks, who migrated to the South of Vindhyas in 4th century BCE.
It was Digambar Sect's monks who migrated to the South of Vindhyas in 4th century BCE. Shvetambara Sect - refers to the monks who wear white clothes. They recognize the existence of canonical texts including 12 Angas. They also believed that women can also attain Salvation.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: According to Jaina tradition, who was the first Jain Tirthankara? [CISF AC 2021]
solution:
(A) Rishabhadeva
Rishabhadeva - 1st Tirthankara, born in Ayodhya, Symbol- Bull. Parshvanatha - 23rd Tirthankara, born in Varanasi and symbol - Serpent. Vardhaman Mahavira - 24th Tirthankara, born in Kundagram(Vaishali), symbol - Lion. Neminatha (Arishthanemi) - 22nd Tirthankara, born in Dwarka, symbol - the Conch (shankha).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: In Jainism, which one of the following does not form part of tri-ratna? [CISF AC 2021]
solution:
(A) Samyak panch-mahavrata
The Tri-ratnas - Right Knowledge (Samyaka Jnana), Right Faith (Samyaka Darshan) and Right Conduct (Samyaka Charitra). 5 Anuvratas of Jainism - Ahimsa, Satya, Asteya, Brahma-charya, Aparigraha. [ref Q-1 Level 1]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Early Buddhist sculptors did not show Buddha in human form. Through which of the following symbols was Buddha's presence shown by the early sculptors? [NDA I 2022]

options.d: Empty Seat, Wheel, Tree and Stupa

solution:
(D) Empty Seat, Wheel, Tree and Stupa
Buddha is represented through symbols like - Empty Throne (Empty Seat), Bodhi Tree, A Wheel (Dharmachakra), Footprints, a riderless Horse and a Parasol.

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
With reference to Indian history, consider the following texts: [IAS 2022]
1. Nettipakarana
2. Parishishtaparvan
3. Avadanashataka
4. Trishashtilakshana Mahapurana
Which of the above are Jaina texts?
solution:
(B) 2 and 4 only
Parishishtaparvan was written by Hemachandra about Chandragupta and Jainism. Trishashthilakshana Mahapurana is a major Jain text composed by Acharya Jinasena. Nettipakarana and Avadanashataka are Buddhist texts.

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
With reference to Indian history, consider the following pairs: [IAS 2022]
Historical person - Known as
1. Aryadeva - Jaina scholar
2. Dignaga - Buddhist scholar
3. Nathamuni - Vaishnava scholar
How many pairs given above are correctly matched?
solution:
(C) Only 2 pairs
Aryadeva was a Mahayana Buddhist monk and a disciple of Nagarjuna. Diganaga was an Indian Buddhist monk and founder of Hetu vidya. Sri Ranganathamuni, also known as Sriman Nathamuni, was a Vaishnava Theologian.

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
With reference to ancient India, consider the following statements: [IAS 2023]
1. The concept of Stupa is Buddhist in origin.
2. Stupa was generally a repository of relics.
3. Stupa was a votive and commemorative structure in Buddhist tradition.
How many of the statements given above are correct?
solution:
(B) Only 2
The concept of Stupa is not exclusively Buddhist in origin. Stupa is a Sanskrit word means "heap" or Pile, it existed in pre Buddhist era. Stupa was a repository of relics and also functioned as votive and commemorative structures in Buddhist tradition.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which one of the following statements with regard to historical importance of Kushinagar, is correct? [CISF AC 2022]

options.a: Siddhartha Gautama's 'great departure' took place here and he became an ascetic, a Sramana.
options.b: Siddhartha Gautama, obtained his enlightenment here and he became Buddha.
options.c: Buddha gave his first sermon known as Dharmachakraparivartana sutra.
options.d: Buddha passed away here, which is known as Mahaparinirvana.

solution:
(D) Buddha passed away here, which is known as Mahaparinirvana.
Gautam Buddha left his place in Kapilavastu to become ascetic, this event is known as the Great Departure or Great Renunciation. He attained enlightenment at Bodh Gaya in Bihar near Niranjana river. Buddha gave his first sermon as Dharmachakra Pravartana Sutra at Sarnath, Varanasi. Buddha passed away in Kushinagara, which is known as Mahaparinirvana.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: "Souls are not only the property of animal and plant life, but also of rocks, running water and many other natural objects not looked on as living by other religious sects." The above statement reflects one of the core beliefs of which one of the following religious sects of ancient India? [IAS 2023]
solution:
(B) Jainism
In Jainism, it is believed that souls (Jiva) exist not just in animals and plants but also in rocks, water and other natural objects, this concept is called Animism.

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
With reference to ancient India, Gautama Buddha was generally known by which of the following epithets? [IAS 2024]
1. Nayaputta
2. Shakyamuni
3. Tathagata
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
solution:
(B) 2 and 3 only
Buddha belongs to Shakya clan and after he attained enlightenment he was called Shakyamuni. Tathagata means 'gone and come' which signifies the cycle of rebirth in Buddhism. Nayaputta is used to refer Vardhaman Mahavira.

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Consider the following observations about the largest stupa at Sanchi: [CAPF 2023]
1. Going by information available from the inscriptions on the railings of the stupa, the construction of part of one of its gateways was financed by the Guild of Ivory workers.
2. In its original early form, this stupa was plain except for the stone railings and the gateways, which were richly carved.
3. The panels on the four gateways contain sculptures only in the front and have no sculptures on the rear side.
4. In 1918, when the stupa was discovered, all of its four gates were intact but the mound was in poor condition.
5. Art historians have established clear connections between the sculptures of the stupa with events described in Jataka Tales.
6. War scenes have also been depicted in these sculptures.
Which of the observations given above are correct?
solution:
(D) 1, 2, 5 and 6 only
Sanchi Stupa was discovered by Henry Taylor in 1818. Each horizontal wall at this stupa was decorated with different themes on front as well as at the back side.

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Which of the following statements about sculptures at Sanchi is/are correct? [CDS I 2022 / NDA I 2025]
1. In some sculptures there is representation of Shalabhanjika.
2. The figure of Shalabhanjika is represented by a woman surrounded by lotuses and elephants.
3. The Shalabhanjika motif was not directly inspired by Buddhist ideas.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
solution:
(D) 1 and 3 only
Shalabhanjika is a decorative element commonly found in Indian art, particularly in Hindu and Buddhist art. The figure of Shalabhanjika can be found surrounded by various elements like lotuses, elephants, trees etc. the specific description in statement 2 is not always true. The Shalabhanjika motif is not directly inspired by Buddhist ideas is true as it existed in pre Buddhist period also.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following is one of the 'three gems' (triratna) of Jainism? [CDS I 2025]
solution:
(B) Samyak-darshana
Triratnas (Three Gems) of Jainism: Samyak Jnana (Right Knowledge), Samyaka Darshan (Right Faith) and Samyaka Charita (Right Conduct). 5 Anuvratas of Jainism - Ahimsa (non-violence), Satya (truth), Asteya (do not steal), Brahmacharya (celibacy) and Aparigraha (detachment).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which one of the following statements about the Buddhist Goddess Hariti is correct? [CDS I 2025]

options.a: She was converted by the Buddha from a blood-thirsty yakshi to a benevolent maternal figure.
options.b: She is the feminine personification of wisdom.
options.c: She protects people from eight great fears.
options.d: She is a warrior goddess associated with the dawn.

solution:
(A) She was converted by the Buddha from a blood-thirsty yakshi to a benevolent maternal figure.
Hariti is a significant figure in Buddhism, she was a yakshi known for abducting and eating children. Buddha transformed her to Hariti, a maternal figure and protector of children. Prajnaparamita - feminine personification of wisdom. Goddess Tara - protects people from eight great fears. In Buddhism, Marici - goddess associated with dawn.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The Aryans lived in the land known as 'Sapta Sindhu' (Land of the Seven Rivers). Which of the following was NOT a part of it? [SSC CGL 23/09/2023]
solution:
(D) Shipra
[ref Q-15 Vedic Age Level-1].

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Kosal, Anga, Kashi and Vajji were all: [RRB NTPC 27/01/2021]

options.c: ruled by King Harshavardhana in 6th century AD

solution:
(B) Part of 16 Mahajanapadas
16 Mahajanapadas with their capitals: Anga - Champa, Magadha - Rajagir/Pataliputra, Kashi - Varanasi, Vatsa - Kausambi, Kosala - Sravasti/Ayodhya, Surasena - Mathura, Panchala - Aichchatra and Kampilya, Kuru - Indraprastha, Matsya - Viratnagar, Chedi / Chetiya - Sothivati/Banda, Avanti - Ujjain/Mahismati, Gandhar - Taxila, Kamboj - Pooncha, Asmaka - Paithan/Pratishthan/Potana, Vajji - Vaishali, Malla - Kushinara.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which dynasty defeated the Kanva dynasty? [SSC MTS 05/11/2024]
solution: (D) Satavahana Dynasty

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The capital of Vajji Mahajanapada was ________. [SSC CGL 19/07/2023]
solution:
(C) Vaishali
Vajji - 8 Clans in which the powerful 3 were Jnatrika (Mahavira's Clan), Videha (King Janaka), Lichchhavi.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The first ruler of Magadha from the Haryanka dynasty was ________. [SSC CGL 03/03/2020]
solution:
(A) Bimbisara (Haryanka dynasty)
Bimbisara (544 BC - 492 BC) - Title: Seniya. Also known as "Shronika". Contemporary to Buddha. He conquered Anga. He married to sister of Prasenjit (the Kosala King) and gained Kashi as dowry. He married Chellana (Lichchhavi princess) and Khema (Madra Clan). He sent Jivaka (his physician) to Ujjain to treat Avanti King Pradyota, who was suffering from Jaundice.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following was the capital of Vatsa Mahajanapada? [SSC MTS 11/11/2024]
solution:
(D) Kausambi
Sources of Mahajanapadas - "Ashtadhyayi" by Panini (oldest book on Sanskrit grammar) mentions 40 Janapadas. Buddhist Literature - Anguttara Nikaya mentioned 16 Mahajanapadas and Digha Nikaya mentioned 12 Mahajanapadas (in Pali language). Jain Literature - Bhagavati Sutra.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Kosala Mahajanapada (6th century BCE) is a part of modern day: [SSC CGL 21/07/2023]
solution: (D) Uttar Pradesh

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Rivers such as the Ganga and Son flowed through ________, a mahajanapada in ancient India. [SSC MTS 06/10/2021]
solution:
(C) Magadha
Magadha emerged to the most powerful Mahajanapada. Ruled by Haryanka Dynasty, Shishunaga Dynasty and the Nanda Dynasty. It's capital Rajgriha was surrounded by 5 hills and Pataliputra situated at the confluence of Ganga and Son.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Iron mines in Jharkhand attributed to the rise of which of the following kingdoms in ancient India? [SSC CGL 25/07/2023]
solution:
(B) Magadha
These iron deposits were used for making weapons and implements, which gave advantageous position to Magadha along with large number of elephants.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who among the following was the last ruler of the Nanda dynasty? [SSC CGL 03/03/2020]
solution:
(A) Dhanananda
Alexander invaded India during his reign in 326 BC. Ugrasena, Panduka, Pandugati, Bhutapala, Rashtrapala, Govishanaka, Dashasiddhaka, Kaivarta and Dhana Nanda, were 9 kings who ruled Nanda dynasty after Mahapadmananda as mentioned in Mahavamsa (Buddhist text).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: In ancient times, the area to the south of the Ganga was known as ________. [SSC CGL 07/12/2022]
solution: (A) Magadha

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Rulers of Mahajanapadas in ancient India collected taxes on crops at the rate of ________ of what was produced. [SSC MTS 05/07/2022]
solution:
(C) 1/6th
Peasants had to pay 1/6th of their produce, it was referred to as "Bhaga". Other taxes - Bali (1st voluntarily, later mandatory), Kara (additional tax on special crops) and Hiranya (tax paid in cash - for non agrarian products).

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Which of the following pairs of 'Name of Ruler-Empire' is correctly matched? [SSC MTS 26/07/2022]
I. Mahapadma Nanda - Magadha Empire
II. Ajatasatru - Maurya Empire
solution:
(D) Only I
Mahapadma Nanda - founder of Nanda dynasty (Magadha). Also known as "Sarvakshatrantak" (uprooter of all the Kshatriyas), "Ugrasen" (owner of huge army) and "Ekrat" (the sole Monarch).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Ajatshatru, a ruler of the Haryanka Dynasty, was the son of ________. [SSC CGL 03/03/2020]
solution:
(D) Bimbisara
Ajatshatru (492 BC - 460 BC) - also known as "Kunika". Son of Bimbisara and Chellana. Conquered Lichchhavi. Defeated Kosala. Patronised 1st Buddhist Council. Killed his father Bimbisara. Conquered Vajji (Vaishali) using War engines (catapults) also called "Mahashilakantaka". Udayin (son of Ajatashatru) shifted capital from Rajgriha to Pataliputra. Udayin was succeeded by Anuruddha (weak ruler). Naga-Dasak was the last ruler of Haryanka dynasty defeated by Shishunaga (established Shishunaga dynasty). Shishunaga defeated Avanti and merged it with Magadha.

subject: Ancient History
