question_text: Which of the following Mahajanapadas emerged as the most powerful at the end of a prolonged conflict for supremacy? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 10/12/2024]
solution: (A) Magadha

subject: Ancient History
question_text: With reference to the sixteen Mahajanapadas, which among the following was NOT a capital city? [RRB NTPC CBT-II 14/06/2022]
solution:
(B) Avanti
Avanti was a Mahajanapada. Capital - Ujjayini / Mahishmati.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Tax, known as bhaga, was levied on which of the following during Mahajanapada period of ancient India? [SSC MTS 09/10/2024]
solution:
(C) Tax on cultivators
Bali: voluntary offerings to the King (not compulsory). Toll tax persisted and collected by officers - 'Shaulkika' or 'Shulkadhyaksha'. Shrenis/Guilds - community of traders, Bhojaka - Village headman, Gahapatis - rich peasants.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: From among the following Mahajanapadas, identify the gana/sangha (oligarchy): [CDS I 2022 / NDA I 2025]
solution:
(B) Vajji
Gana/Sangha means Republic - Kuru, Kamboja, Vajji, Malla, Assaka (Asmaka). Other Mahajanapads were Monarchial.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: On the banks of which one of the following rivers was the ancient Mahishmati located? [CDS II 2022]
solution:
(C) Narmada
Avanti capital - Mahishmati / Ujjayini. Mahishmati was ruled by the Haihaya ruler Kartavirya Arjuna also known as Sahasrabahu and Kalachuris of Mahishmati.

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Which among the following was/were the capital's of the Magadha Janapada? [CISF AC 2021]
1. Rajgriha
2. Pataliputra
3. Champa
4. Vaishall
solution:
(B) 1 and 2
Note: In options if Udayin is not given then, Ajatashatru will be preferred.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which one among the following rulers established Pataliputra as the capital of the Magadhan Empire? [NDA I 2023]
solution:
(C) Ajatashatru
Udayin shifted capital from Rajgriha to Pataliputra (official shift). Ajatashatru built a fort at a village called Patali (later Pataliputra). Note: In options if Udayin is not given then, Ajatashatru will be preferred.

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Consider the following statements about the emergence of urban centres in India from circa 6th century BCE: [NDA I 2024]
1. All of them developed away from the capitals of mahajanapadas.
2. Major towns were located along routes of communication.
3. Many were bustling centres of commercial, cultural and political activity.
solution:
(B) 2
Most of the urban centres were developed around the central capitals of Mahajanapads like Pataliputra, Taxila, Varanasi. So, Statement 1 is incorrect.
Major towns were located along routes of communication and were centres of commercial, cultural and political activity as they were connecting India with Central Asia, Middle East. Statements 2 and 3 are correct.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Taxila was the capital of which one among the following Mahajanapadas of ancient India? [NDA II 2024]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following was the first subcontinental empire? [SSC MTS 16/10/2024]
solution:
(B) Mauryan Empire
Mauryan Empire (322 BC - 185 BC) - by "Mauryas". Origin of Mauryas: Acc to Puranas - they belong to shudras. Mudrarakshasa - they belong to Vrishal/kulahina. Junagarh Rock Inscription-Vaishya community.
Sources-Kautilya's:Arthashastra, Vishakhadatta's:Mudrarakshasa (written during Gupta empire). Megasthenes: Indica, Buddhist literature :Jataka tales, Dipavamsa/Mahavamsa, Divyadana.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who was the founder of the Mauryan dynasty? [SSC MTS 30/09/2024]
solution:
(D) Chandragupta Maurya
Founder of Mauryan Empire. Chandragupta defeated Dhanananda (last Nanda ruler) with the help of Kautilya (Chanakya) in 322 BC. He defeated Seleucus Nicator (frontier of Alexander) in 305 BC and gifted him 500 elephants for territories of Kandahar, Baluchistan, Kabul, Herat.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Chandragupta Maurya's capital was: [SSC STENOGRAPHER 10/12/2024]
solution:
(C) Pataliputra
There were two types of courts in Mauryan Empire.
a) Dharmasthiya Nyayalaya - Vyavaharika (personal disputes)
b) Kantakashodhan Nyayalaya - Pradeshta (disputes between state and people)

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which ruler succeeded Chandragupta Maurya in 297 BCE? [SSC MTS 29/10/2024]
solution:
(B) Bindusara
Bindusara (297 BC - 273 BC) - Son of Chandragupta Maurya. Also known as "Amitrochates" by Greeks, in Sanskrit "Amitraghata" (slayer of foes). He patronised Ajivikas. Bindusara asked Antiochus I (Syria) to send wine, figs and philosophers to his Empire. He is known for conquering land between two seas. Deimachus (Greek ambassador) visited during his reign.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The Mauryan pillar capital found at ________ is popularly known as the Lion Capital. [SSC CGL 27/07/2023]
solution:
(D) Sarnath
Lion capital was declared as National Emblem on 26 Jan 1950. Made of polished sandstone. 5 Components - The shaft, inverted Lotus base, a circular abacus (drum) with 4 animals - elephant, oxen (bull), lion and horse, 4 asiatic lions and a large wheel.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who among the following was the first Mauryan ruler who tried to spread his message to the people through inscriptions? [SSC MTS 26/07/2022]
solution:
(B) Ashoka
Ashoka - ascended the throne with the help of Radhagupta. He killed his 99 brothers and spared Tissa (youngest) (according to buddhist writers). Fought Kalinga War in 261 BC after 8 years of coronation. He adopted Buddhism and replaced Berighosha (physical conquest) with Dhammaghosha (cultural conquest). He sent his son Mahendra and daughter Sanghamitra to Ceylon (Sri Lanka) to spread Buddhism.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which was the first empire in ancient India to use elephants in its war on a large scale? [SSC CHSL 05/08/2021]
solution: (D) Magadha

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following was a Ceylonese chronicle that acted as an important literary source of the Mauryan empire? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 12/10/2023]
solution:
(C) Mahavamsa
Mahavamsa (composed by Mahanama) means "great chronicle". It is written in Pali about Kings of Sri Lanka.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Megasthenes was an ambassador who was sent to the court of which Indian ruler by the Greek ruler, Seleucus Nicator? [SSC MTS 30/10/2024]
solution:
(B) Chandragupta
Seleucus Nicator (Seleucid dynasty) ruled in North western parts of Indian subcontinent. He married his daughter Helena with Chandragupta. He sent Megasthenes, who wrote Indica, which includes details of Mauryan Empire.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The author of 'Arthashastra' was a contemporary of which of the following rulers? [SSC MTS 18/10/2024]
solution:
(B) Chandragupta Maurya
Chanakya - author of "Arthashastra", Also known as "Kautilya" or Vishnugupta. Arthashastra describes the administration and state policies during Chandragupta's reign. Chandragupta built a department: Kosthagara (for storage of grains), mentioned in Sohgaura copper plate inscription. Arthashastra Book 2 - grain storage.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: When did Bindusara ascend the Maurya throne? [SSC MTS 29/10/2024]
solution:
(A) 297 BCE
[ref Q-4]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who among the following Mauryan kings turned to Buddhism and its tenet of non-violence after the Kalinga war? [SSC CPO 04/10/2023]
solution:
(A) Ashoka
Spiritual teacher of Ashoka - Upa Gupta.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who among the following Mauryan kings fasted to death and died at Shravanabelagola? [SSC CPO 05/10/2023]
solution:
(D) Chandragupta Maurya
Chandragupta adopted Jainism and went to Chandragiri Hill, Shravanabelagola (Karnataka) with Bhadrabahu and died by performing Sallekhana (fasting till death).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The foundation of Sanchi Stupa was laid by which of the following Kings? [SSC MTS 08/09/2023]
solution: (C) Ashoka

subject: Ancient History
question_text: What was the function of an officer called Samaharta? [SSC CGL 17/07/2023]
solution:
(B) Tax Assessment
Sannidhata - chief treasury officer. Gopa - responsible for account. Nagaraka - city administration, Akshapatalika - Accountant General/Audit officer. Sitadhyaksha - agriculture, Samsthadhyaksha - market. Navaadhyaksha - ships, Ashvaadhyaksha - commanding officer of Cavalry, Shulkadhyaksha - toll tax. Dhamma mahamatta - new post by Ashoka, for propagating Dhamma.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who sent the Greek ambassador Megasthenes to the court of Chandragupta Maurya? [SSC CGL 18/07/2023]
solution:
(A) Seleucus
[ref Q-9]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: During the Mauryan society, Megasthenes divided the Indian society into seven distinct groups. In which distinct group were the 'king's counsellors' placed? [SSC MTS 21/10/2024]
solution:
(C) Seventh
7 distinct groups - 1) Philosophers, 2) Cultivators/farmers, 3) Hunters and Shepherds, 4) Traders, 5) Warrior, 6) Overseer (spy), 7) Councillor.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who among the following was defeated by Mauryan emperor Chandragupta Maurya? [SSC CGL 20/07/2023]
solution:
(B) Seleucus Nicator
Cassander was the king of Macedonian after Alexander. Ptolemy was the king of Egypt. Antigonus was king of Macedon.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Chandragupta Maurya overthrew the Nanda Dynasty of Magadha with the help of ________. [SSC CGL 21/07/2023]
solution:
(C) Vishnugupta
[ref Q-10]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: How many classes of Indian society are mentioned in Megasthenes book Indica? [SSC CGL 24/07/2023]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: According to Kautilya's Arthashastra, the Commanding Officer the cavalry in the Mauryan administration was known as: [SSC CGL 24/07/2023]
solution:
(C) Ashwadhyaksha
Rathadhyaksha - commander for the chariots. Hastyadhyaksha - commander of elephants. Patyadhyaksha - commander of infantry.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who among the following wrote a book called 'Indica' based on his travels and experiences in Mauryan India? [SSC MTS 28/10/2024]
solution:
(C) Megasthenes
Strabo wrote "Geographica", Diodorus wrote "Bibliotheca Historica" about world history. Pliny - "Natural History".

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following texts lays down minute details of the administrative and military organisation during the Mauryan empire? [SSC PHASE XI 27/06/2023]
solution:
(D) Arthashastra
Brihat Samhita was written by Varahamihira in Sanskrit. Nitisara was written by Kamandaka on politics and statecraft. He was a disciple of Chanakya. Shulba sutras (Mathematical knowledge) by Baudhayana, Manava, Apastamba and Katyayana during Vedic period.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Nagarjuni Caves were donated to which of the following sects by Mauryans? [SSC PHASE XI 28/06/2023]
solution:
(A) Ajivika
Nagarjuni caves are located in Nagarjuni hills near Barabar hills, Bihar. It was donated by Dasaratha Maurya (grandson of Ashoka).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: According to the Ashokan Inscription, which of the following was NOT a provincial center in the Magadha Empire? [SSC MTS 02/05/2023]
solution:
(D) Indraprastha
Provincial Centres - Uttarapatha: Taxila (north), Avantiratha: Ujjain (west), Dakshinapatha: Suvarnagiri (south), Prachyapatha: Tosali (east). Prachi - Pataliputra (Central).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: As per the contents mentioned in Asokan Inscriptions, how many major political centres were there in the Mauryan empire? [SSC MTS 16/05/2023]
solution: (A) Five

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which Mauryan ruler was called Amitrochates by the Greeks? [SSC MTS 16/05/2023]
solution:
(C) Bindusara
[ref Q-4]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: There were several cities in the Mauryan empire. Which of the following cities was located on the southernmost side? [SSC MTS 14/06/2023]
solution:
(C) Brahmagiri
Brahmagiri - Karnataka, Girnar - Gujarat, Rupnath - Madhya Pradesh, Topra - Haryana.

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Which of the following statement is correct? [SSC MTS 08/07/2022]
I. Most of Ashoka's inscriptions were written in Brahmi script.
II. Many of Chanakya's ideas were written down in a book called the Arthashastra
solution:
(D) Both I and II
Ashokan inscriptions in both Prakrit and Sanskrit are written in Brahmi script, some are written in Kharosthi script. Many of Chanakya's ideas related to statecraft are written in Arthashastra.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following rulers did NOT belong to the Maurya dynasty? [SSC MTS 20/07/2022]
solution:
(D) Bimbisara
Bimbisara belongs to Haryanka dynasty.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Ashokan Minor Rock Edicts are found in different parts of India. Which of the following is NOT a find spot of Ashokan Minor Rock Edicts in Karnataka? [SSC CGL 21/04/2022]
solution:
(C) Rupnath
Rupnath Inscription is located in Madhya Pradesh.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Name the Sri Lankan Buddhist text that provides details of the Mauryan empire. [SSC MTS 14/11/2024]
solution:
(D) Dipavamsa
Sri Lankan texts that provide valuable insights about Mauryan Empire are Dipavamsa and Mahavamsa. Divyavadana is a Buddhist text in Sanskrit language about historical figures and Ashokavadana tells about reign of Ashoka. Parishishta Parvan is a Jain epic by Hemachandra in Sanskrit.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who among the following generally used to head the provincial administration under the Mauryas? [SSC CGL 19/09/2024]
solution:
(A) Kumara
Mauryan empire was divided into provinces headed by the prince (Kumara) or a member of royal family. Samaharta - collector general of revenue. Prashasti - inscription, Dandapala - Head of police responsible for law and order.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following was NOT one of the basic attributes of Ashokas' Dhamma? [SSC CGL 24/09/2024]
solution:
(C) Celibacy
Ashoka's dhamma - people should obey their parents, Secularism between religions, respect towards Buddhist monks and Brahmanas, show mercy to slaves and servants, Ahimsa (non-violence) should be practices, political unification, prohibition of animal sacrifices, promoting welfare of his subjects etc.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Deimachus who was a Greek ambassador came to India during the reign of the Mauryan king, ________. [SSC CGL 17/09/2024]
solution:
(B) Bindusara
[ref Q-4]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The Mahabodhi Temple was built entirely from ________ by Ashoka. [SSC STENOGRAPHER 10/12/2024]
solution:
(D) Bricks
Original temple - bricks. Current structure - sandstone and granite. UNESCO World Heritage site (2002).

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
With reference to the city administration of Mauryan Empire, consider the following statements. [SSC STENOGRAPHER 10/12/2024]
I. Megasthenes put light on the Mauryan city administration.
II. The city council was divided into six committees.
III. City administration was well planned.
solution:
(A) I, II, III
Megasthenes put light on Mauryan city administration and stated that it was well planned. It was divided into 6 committees - 1) Industrial arts, 2) Foreigners, 3) Registration of births and deaths, 4) Trade and commerce, 5) Supervision of sales and 6) Collection of taxes.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following edicts of King Ashoka describes Kalinga war? [SSC CGL 18/09/2024]
solution:
(B) Rock edict XIII
Rock edict XIII was the largest inscription from the edict. About Ashoka's victory over Kalinga (261 BC) in which around 1 lakh people were killed and 1.5 lakhs were deported.
It also mentions Ashoka's dhamma victory over Greek kings Antiochus of Syria, Ptolemy of Egypt, Antigonus of Macedon, also mentions Cholas, Pandyas, etc.
Rock edict I - Prohibits animal slaughter.
Rock edict III - Generosity to Brahmans, it says that Yuktas (subordinate officers), Pradesikas (district heads), along with Rajukas (rural officers) will spread the dhamma policy of Ashoka.
Rock edict IV - Dhammaghosha (cultural conquest) is better than Berighosha (conquest of war).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Chandragupta Maurya overthrew which of the following dynasties of ancient India to establish the Mauryan empire? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 11/12/2024]
solution: (A) Nanda

subject: Ancient History
question_text: With reference to provinces under Mauryan dynasty, which of the following pairs is correct? [SSC MTS 30/10/2024]
solution:
(A) Prachyapatha (East) - Toshali
[ref Q-25]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Palkigundu Minor Rock Edicts of Ashoka are located in ________. [SSC MTS 11/11/2024]
solution:
(B) Karnataka
The Inscription is written in Prakrit in Brahmi script.

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Match the army officers of the Mauryan empire in column A with the respective salaries given to them in column B. [SSC MTS 09/10/2024]
Column A (Army Officer) - Column B (Salaries)
a. Senapati - i. 12,000 Pana per annum
b. Adhyakshas - ii. 8,000 Pana per annum
c. Mukhvas - iii. 4,000 Pana per annum
d. Nayka - iv. 48,000 Pana per annum
solution:
(D) a-iv, b-iii, c-ii, d-i
Senapati (Commander-in-Chief): 48,000, Nayaka (Regimental officer): 12,000, Mukhya (chiefs): 8000, Adhyaksha (superintendent): 4,000 panas per annum.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: How many Pillar Edicts or Major Pillar Edicts (PEs) of Ashoka are found? [SSC MTS 12/11/2024]
solution:
(A) Seven
The 7 pillar edicts found at - Meerut, Topra (Delhi), Allahabad (Kaushambi), Lauriya Nandangarh, Lauriya Araraj and Rampurva (Champaran) (Bihar), Mehrauli.
Delhi-Topra Pillar edict - only with 7th edict. Use of only one language (Prakrit) and one script (Brahmi).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who among the following deciphered the Brahmi Script of the Mauryan period? [SSC MTS 01/10/2024]
solution: (B) James Prinsep

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Seleucus Nicator was a contemporary of ________. [SSC MTS 14/10/2024]
solution:
(D) Chanakya
Seleucus Nicator was contemporary of Chandragupta Maurya and Chanakya.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which event brought about a profound change in Ashoka's administrative policy? [SSC MTS 07/10/2024]
solution:
(C) The Kalinga War
The Battle of Kalinga (261 BC) was fought on the banks of Daya river near Tosali, Orissa. After Kalinga victory Ashoka adopted Buddhism and pledged the path of non-violence.
Minor rock edict - Maski finds mention towards Buddhist affiliation.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Ashoka appointed ________ to solve the social problems in his region. [SSC CGL 09/12/2022]
solution:
(D) dhamma-mahamatta
It was a new post created by Ashoka, mentioned in rock edict V, to propagate dhamma and solve social problems of his region. Samaharta - revenue collector, Nyayadhish - judge, Amatya - Secretary.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who among the following published a set of Ashokan inscriptions in 1877? [SSC CGL 20/08/2021]
solution: (C) Alexander Cunningham

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Ashoka sent his son Mahendra and daughter Sanghmitra to spread the message of Dhamma to which of the following countries? [SSC CPO 29/06/2024]
solution:
(B) Sri Lanka
[ref Q-6]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Emperor Ashoka conquered Kallinga after how many years of his coronation? [SSC CGL 24/07/2023]
solution:
(B) 8 years
Ashoka was crowned formally in 269 BC and Kalinga war happened in 261 BC.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The name 'Piyadassi' is associated with whom among the following Mauryan kings? [SSC CGL 13/09/2024 + SSC CPO 28/06/2024]
solution:
(C) Ashoka
Maski minor rock edict (Karnataka) links Devanampiya Piyadassi (Beloved of Gods) with Ashoka's name.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Ashoka sent a mission to spread the principle of Dhamma led by his son, Mahendra and daughter, Sanghamitra to ________. [SSC CGL 17/07/2023]
solution:
(B) Ceylon
[ref Q-6]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who among the following was the last Mauryan ruler and was killed by his commander-in-chief? [SSC CPO 03/10/2023]
solution:
(B) Brihadratha
Brihadratha was the last Mauryan ruler, assassinated in 185 BC by his commander-in-chief, Pushyamitra Sunga, who established Sunga dynasty. Satadhanvan was the father of Brihadratha. Dasaratha was the Grandson of Ashoka.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The painted illustration of the moving of the Ashoka Pillar at Topra is found in [CDS II 2021]
solution:
(C) Sirut-i-Firuz Shahi
Topra is a village in Yamunanagar district of Haryana. The Delhi-Topra Ashokan Pillar was moved to Feroz Shah Kotla in Delhi in 1356 by Feroz Shah Tughlaq.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which one of the following was not a part of Kautilya's Saptanga Theory of the State? [CDS I 2022]
solution:
(D) Dhamma
Kautilya's Saptanga theory - Raja (king), Amatya (secretaries), Janapada (territory), Durg (fort), Kosha (treasure), Sena (army), and Mitra (friend).

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Consider the following statements about Ashoka's faith in Buddhism: [CDS I 2022]
1. Rummindei Pillar Inscription and Nigali Sagar Pillar Inscription provide clear evidence of Ashoka's faith in Buddhism.
2. Minor Rock Edict-I gives evidence of a sudden change in Ashoka's faith in Buddhism.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
solution:
(A) 1 only
Minor Rock edict I indicates that Ashoka turned towards Buddhism gradually, after 2.5 years in power. There was no sudden change. Rummindei Pillar inscription or Lumbini Pillar Inscription is in Nepal. It depicts that Ashoka travelled to the birthplace of Buddha, which is the evidence of Ashoka's faith in Buddhism.

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Consider the following statements about the Mauryan State and the forest people: [CDS I 2024]
1. The forest people were subjected to new forms of political and economic dominance and the necessity to subordinate and assimilate them led to a change in the earlier attitude of excluding these people from imperial territory.
2. The State recognised that the forest produce was the sole monopoly of the forest people.
3. The State was concerned with the conservation of forests and to this end the burning of forests was prohibited.
4. The forest people could be harnessed to serve the State and could be used as troops, spies and assassins.
How many of the above statements is/are correct?
solution:
(C) 3
1, 3 and 4 are correct.
1. The forest people were often subject to new forms of control, including taxation by Mauryan Empire.
2. The state often claimed rights over forest resources and controlled them for their economic and security gain, they did not recognize forest produce as the sole monopoly of the forest people.
3. Burning of forests was prohibited which shows the state was concerned about conservation of forests.
4. The forest people sometimes recruited for military and intelligence purposes due to their knowledge of forest and environment.

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Which of the following is/are the correct components of the "Lion Capital" at Sarnath? [CDS II 2024]
1. The shaft (stambha-yashti) or the tall tapering column
2. An elliptical top part the abacus, devoid of any carvings
3. Overflowing inverted petals below the abacus
Select the answer using the code given below:
solution:
(B) 1 and 3 only
The components of Lion capital - The shaft or the tall tapering column, inverted Lotus base (overflowing inverted petals below abacus) and 4 lion sculptures. A circular abacus (drum) with carvings of elephant (east), horse (south), bull (west) and 4 wheels (represents Dharmachakra). So, 2 is incorrect.

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Consider the following pairs of High Offices and concerned Departments in the Mauryan Administration: [CDS II 2024]
1. Samahartri : Chief Collector of Revenue
2. Dauvarika : Chief of Army
3. Akshapatala : Records-cum-Audit Officer
4. Sannidhatri : Treasurer
Which of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
solution:
(B) 1, 3 and 4 only
Dauvarika - System of administration based on gateways. Dauvarika include gatekeepers (Dwarapala) and officials.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The antariya was a garment worn by people during the Mauryan period. It was: [CDS II 2024]

options.a: an unstitched length of cloth draped around the body.
options.b: a sewn garment consisting of a sleeved tunic.
options.c: head covering used exclusively by women.
options.d: made of strips sewn together tied at the waist with a cord.

solution:
(A) an unstitched length of cloth draped around the body
Antariya is made from fine cotton or muslin and worn by both men and women, it is wrapped around the waist. Uttariya is an upper garment that is paired with antariya by women. Kachcha (Kaupina) loincloth or waist-wrap made of stitched or tied fabric strips, secured with a cord (mekhala or kayabandh).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The Mauryan capital of Pataliputra has been associated with the archaeological remains of [CAPF 2021]
solution:
(C) Kumrahar and Bulandibagh
Kumrahar - The remains of ancient city of Pataliputra excavated, including the 80-pillared Hall (80 pillars supporting a wooden roof). Bulandibagh - Known for discoveries like wooden palisade and terracotta figures.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Ashokan inscriptions in Afghanistan are written in which one of the following scripts? [CAPF 2022]
solution:
(D) Greek-Aramaic
Brahmi - Indigenous script, written left to right. Kharosthi (Aramaic derived) - Northwest India, written right to left. Greek - Afghanistan, written left to right. Aramaic - Afghanistan, written right to left.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which one of the following political centers finds a mention in Ashokan inscriptions? [CAPF 2023]
solution:
(C) Suvarnagiri
It was the Dakshinapatha (south) centre of Mauryan administration.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which one of the following activities was not a part of the daily time-table for a King as prescribed in Arthashastra? [CAPF 2023]
solution:
(B) Visit the town incognito
The king was assisted by Mantri Parishad which include: The Yuvaraja (crown prince), The Purohita (the chief priest), The Senapati (commander in chief), and other ministers. In Arthashastra specific time slots for strategic planning and meetings of the King were given. Some important tasks were strategizing political matters, security and foreign relations, consulting the council of ministers, receiving reports on defence, tax collection, attending legal matters, hearing petitions, ensuring economic prosperity, welfare of his people, etc.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which one of the following animals does not come under the category of four noble animals (Maha-Ajaneya Pasu) carved on the round drum (Anda-Phalaka) of the lion capital at Sarnath? [CAPF 2023]
solution:
(A) Deer
4 animals - Lion, Bull, Horse and Elephant.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which one of the following statements is correct? [CAPF 2023]

options.a: Brahmi, used for writing many Ashokan inscriptions, shows local variations.
options.b: The system of hieroglyphic writing was developed in ancient Mesopotamia.
options.c: The system of cuneiform writing developed around 3rd millennium BCE in Egypt.
options.d: An ancient script, Kharoshthi was widely used in the southern part of India.

solution:
(A) Brahmi, used for writing many Ashokan inscriptions, shows local variations
Brahmi was used extensively during Mauryan period, it varied in different regions due to which there are differences in styles of Brahmi script. The hieroglyphic writing system was developed in ancient Egypt, not Mesopotamia. The system of cuneiform writing was not developed in Egypt, it was developed by ancient Sumerians in Mesopotamia. An ancient script, Kharosthi was widely used in north-western parts of India.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: In Kautilya's Arthashastra, the conquerer king is expected to establish, in the conquered territories, a social order based on the [CAPF 2024]
solution:
(B) dharma, varna and ashrama system
Kautilya, in his Arthashastra advocated the integration of dharma, varna and ashrama system to maintain social order in conquered territories. This led to social stability and peace.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Tosali is known in Indian History as the name of: [CISF AC 2023]

options.a: the Jain brother of the Buddhist Upali.
options.b: a provincial centre in Mauryan Empire.
options.d: a sacred river in Andhra Pradesh.

solution:
(B) a provincial centre in the Mauryan Empire
Prachyapatha (eastern province) - Tosali.

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Consider the following statements about the historical evidence of inscriptions in India: [CISF AC 2024]
1. The oldest deciphered inscriptions are in Brahmi and Kharosthi.
2. The earliest inscriptions in the Tamil language are copper plate grants.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
solution:
(A) 1 only
The oldest deciphered inscriptions in India are found in Brahmi and Kharosthi script around 3rd century BC and were deciphered by James Prinsep. Tamil Brahmi cave inscriptions (2nd BCE) were found before copper plates (5-10 BCE).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which one of the following was not a part of the dhamma of King Ashoka? [NDA I 2021]

options.b: Tolerance of religions other than one's
options.d: Promoting the welfare of his subjects

solution:
(A) Honouring the king
[ref Q-34 level 1]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Arthashastra, the classical work of Indian political thought, focuses primarily on [NDA I 2022]
solution:
(C) statecraft
[ref Q-2 level 2]

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Match List-I with List-II and select the correct answer using the code given below the Lists: [NDA II 2022]
List-I (Mahamatta) - List-II (Function)
A. Anta-mahamatta - 1. Women's welfare
B. Ithijhakha-mahamatta - 2. Spread of Dhamma
C. Dhamma-mahamatta - 3. Associated with city administration
D. Nagalaviyohalaka mahamatta - 4. In-charge of frontier areas
solution:
(C) A4 B1 C2 D3
Anta-mahamatta : in-charge of frontier areas, Ithijhakha-mahamatta : Women's welfare, Dhamma-mahamatta : Spread of Dhamma, Nagalaviyohalaka-mahamatta : Associated with city administration.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who among the following was the author of the famous Sanskrit work 'Mrichchhakarika'? [NDA II 2023]
solution:
(D) Shudraka
It was written in 2nd century BC. It is a story of a young man named Charudatta and his love for Vasantasena. Shudraka also wrote Vinavasavadatta and Padmaprabhitaka. Valmiki wrote the epic Ramayana. Bhasa wrote Duta-Vakya and Bala-charita. Kalidasa - Abhijnanashakuntalam, Vikramorvashi, and Malavikagnimitra etc.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which one of the following Ashokan Minor Rock Edicts, contains the personal name of the king Ashoka? [NDA II 2023]
solution:
(A) Maski
Here, Ashoka is mentioned as Devanami Piyadassi Ashoka. Maski is a village in Raichur district in Karnataka. Minor rock edicts of Ashoka - Maski (Karnataka), Brahmagir (Karnataka), Sasaram (Bihar), Rupnath (MP), Bhabru-Bairat (Rajasthan).

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Consider the following statements about ancient Indian inscriptions: [NDA I 2024]
1. The earliest inscriptions are in Sanskrit.
2. Kharosthi script, used in inscriptions in the north-west was deciphered with the help of coins of Indo-Greek kings who ruled over the area.
3. Most of the inscriptions mention grand, unique events, and routine agricultural practices do not find mention.
How many of the above statements is/are correct?
solution:
(A) 1
The earliest inscriptions in India are in Prakrit language and Brahmi script and not in Sanskrit. Kharosthi script, used in the inscriptions of north-western regions of ancient India was deciphered with the help of bilingual coins of Indo-Greek Kings. It was deciphered by James Prinsep during 19th century. Most of the inscriptions mentions grand, unique events such as victories, religious events, about Kings. Many inscriptions also mention about routine agricultural practices like land records, land tax, tax on produce, etc.

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Consider the following pairs: [IAS 2022]
Site of Ashoka's major rock edicts - Location in the State of
1. Dhauli - Odisha
2. Erragudi - Andhra Pradesh
3. Jaugada - Madhya Pradesh
4. Kalsi - Karnataka
How many pairs given above are correctly matched?
solution:
(B) Only 2 pairs
Ashoka's major rock edicts - Kalsi (Uttarakhand), Girnar (Gujarat), Sopara (Maharashtra), Dhauli and Jaugada (Odisha), Yerragudi/Erragudi (Andhra Pradesh), Sannati (Karnataka). Mansehra and Shahabazgarhi in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (Pakistan). Kandahar in Afghanistan.

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
According to Kautilya's Arthashastra, which of the following are correct? [IAS 2022]
1. A person could be a slave as a result of a judicial punishment.
2. If a female slave bore her master a son, she was legally free.
3. If a son born to a female slave was fathered by her master, the son was entitled to the legal status of the master's son.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
solution:
(B) 2 and 3 only
According to Kautilya's Arthashastra a person could become a slave as a result of war or debt, but not as a judicial punishment. By bearing a child for his master the slave woman was released from her bondage and she was legally free. If a child born to a female slave was fathered by her master. The son will have the same rights and status as the son of master's legal wife.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who among the following was the first to decipher the Ashokan inscriptions? [GEOSCIENTIST 2023]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which dynasty defeated the Kanva dynasty? [SSC MTS 05/11/2024]
solution:
(A) Satavahana Dynasty
Satavahana Dynasta (60 BC - 225 AD) - ruled in deccan region, also known as "Andhrabhrityas". Capital - Paithan/Pratishtan. Founder - Simuka Satvahana. Greatest ruler - Gautamiputra Satakarni, title - Ekabrahmana, defeated Kshatrap Nahapana (Saka ruler). Embraced Brahmanism. Matrilineal society. Started lead coins and traded with Romans. Ajanta and Ellora caves were built during their reign.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who was the successor of Vima Kadphises in Kushana dynasty? [SSC MTS 15/10/2024]
solution:
(C) Kanishka
Kushan Dynasty (1st century AD - 3rd century AD). Also known as Yuezhis/Tocharians. Capital - Peshawar (first) and Mathura (second). Called themselves "Devaputra" (son of Gods. 1st dynasty founder: Kujula Kadphises. 2nd ruler - Vima Kadphises, father of Kanishka. Kanishka - Started Saka Era (followed by Government of India) in 78 AD when he ascended the throne. Started purest form of Gold coins. Also known as 2nd Ashoka. Patronised 4th Buddhist council in Kundalvan, Kashmir. Introduced "Satrap system of Governance" in which kingdom divided into provinces called satrapies each ruled by Kshatrapa or a Mahakshatrapa.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Pushyamitra, who was the commander of Brihadratha, the last Mauryan emperor, killed the king and established a new dynasty. Which of the following was his dynasty? [SSC CGL TIER II 06/03/2023]
solution:
(A) Shunga
Shunga Dynasty (185 BC - 73 BC) : Founder - Pushyamitra Sunga. He defeated Brihadratha, last Mauryan ruler. He was a Brahmin commander of Brihadratha and follower of Hinduism and was a devotee of Krishna. Capital - Vidisha (MP). Also patronised Buddhism, Barhut Stupa built during his reign. His son - Agnimitra. Malvikagnimitra, play of Kalidasa. Patanjali was contemporary of Pushyamitra Shunga. Patanjali's book "Mahabhasya" mentions that pushyamitra Shunga performed two Ashwamedha Yagya.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Vasudeva I who issued coins in gold (dinars and quarter dinars) and copper (single denomination) was a ________ king. [SSC MTS 11/09/2024]
solution:
(D) Kushana
Vasudeva I was last great Kushan ruler, he issued gold and copper coins (featuring Shiva and bull Nandi). Books during Kushan period: Literature : Buddhacharita by Ashvaghosha. Mahavastu and Divyadana. Kamasutra : Vatsyayana. Kushans introduced tunics (shorter, looser, and belt-fastened cloths). Science: Charaksamhita by Charaka (father of medicine), Sushruta Samhita by Sushruta (father of surgery).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The Maukhharis ruled over which of the following cities in western Uttar Pradesh that over the time replaced Pataliputra as a political centre of north India? [SSC CGL 17/07/2024]
solution:
(B) Kannauj
The Maukhari dynasty ruled over Kannauj (Kanyakubja), after the decline of the Gupta Empire. They controlled the Ganga-Yamuna region and made Kannauj a political centre. The Maukhari dynasty was overthrown by Harshavardhana of Pushyabhuti dynasty, who transferred his capital from Thanesar to Kannauj.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: According to the inscriptions, Pushyamitra Shunga was a devotee of which of the following Gods? [SSC CGL 25/09/2024]
solution:
(D) Krishna
[ref Q-3]

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Which of the following pairs of 'Dynasty-Ruled region' is correctly matched? [SSC CGL 08/12/2022]
I. Shakas - North west and north India
II. Vakatakas - Central and western India
solution:
(D) Both I and II
Sakas - ruled in North west and North India. Also known as 'Scythians'. They replaced Indo-Greeks. The most famous ruler was Rudradaman. He defeated Satavahanas and repaired Sudarshan Lake, built constructed by Pushyagupta Vaishya (present in the court of Chandragupta Maurya). Vakataka dynasty (250 AD) - ruled in central and western India. Founded by Vindhyashakti (Brahmin King).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: In the context of Mathura school of Art, during which of the following periods were a number of sculptures of Brahmanical deities, such as Kartikeya, Vishnu and Kubera carved? [SSC CGL 26/09/2024]
solution:
(D) Kushana
During the Kushana period, sculptures of Brahmanical deities were carved in Red Sandstone.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who were the first foreign rulers of northwest India in the post-Mauryan period? [SSC MTS 08/10/2024]
solution:
(D) Bactrian Greeks
Bactrians also known as Indo-Greeks. Most famous ruler - Meander (Milind), He was converted to Buddhism by Nagasen or Nagarjuna. Indo-Greeks were the first to use Gold coins. Introduced Hellenistic art (Gandhara school of art) in the North-Western India, grey sandstone is used in this art.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Pushyamitra Sunga's capital was at: [SSC MTS 21/10/2024]
solution:
(B) Pataliputra
Capital of Pushyamitra Sunga was Pataliputra. The later rulers of Sunga dynasty, including Bhagabhadra, also established a capital at Vidisha.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Kharavela was an important ruler of which dynasty? [SSC MTS 12/11/2024]
solution:
(D) Chedi Dynasty
Chedi dynasty (1st century BC) - ruled over the parts of Kalinga. Greatest ruler - King Kharvela, who embraced Jainism. Evidence - Hathigumpha Inscription (Bhubaneshwar) - "The protector of Jain monks, conqueror of Magadha, and restorer of Kalinga's wealth."

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who among the following Kushana kings was the founder of the Kushana dynasty? [SSC PHASE XII 24/06/2024]
solution:
(C) Kujula Kadphises
[ref Q-2]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following statements about the Kushana dynasty is INCORRECT? [SSC PHASE XII 26/06/2024]

options.a: The Kushanas were a major ruling group in the post-Mauryan period.
options.b: The First Buddhist Council was organised by Kanishka.
options.c: Many Kushana rulers also adopted the title 'devaputra', or 'son of god'.
options.d: Asvaghosha, the author of the Buddhacharita, was the court poet of Kanishka.

solution: (B) The first Buddhist council was organised by Kanishka. The First Buddhist Council was patronised by Ajatashatru in Rajagriha in 483 BC. Kanishka organised the Fourth Buddhist Council held at Kundalavana, Kashmir in 98 AD.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following rulers is considered as the greatest king of Satavahana dynasty and is described as the destroyer of the Sakas, Yavanas and Pahlavas? [SSC PHASE XII 26/06/2024]
solution:
(C) Gautamiputra Satakarni
He defeated Ksatrap Nahapana (Saka ruler) (mentioned in Nasik inscription) and revived the Satavahana dynasty. Yajna Sri Satakarni was the dynasty's last great ruler. Hala was the author of 'Gathasaptasati' or 'Sattasai' in Prakrit. Simuka Satvahana was the founder of Satavahana dynasty. Vashishthiputra Pulumavi was the last ruler of Satavahana dynasty, defeated by Saka ruler Rudradaman I.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The Amaravati School of art was developed under the patronage of ________. [SSC MTS 17/05/2023]
solution:
(C) Satavahanas
Amaravati School of Art (in Andhra region) is famous for its 'Stupas' (Amaravati Mahachaitya) which are made of white limestone, 'Chaityas' and 'Viharas'. Satavahanas patronized Brahmanism and Buddhism.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Around 2000 years ago ________ became the second capital of the Kushanas. [SSC MTS 19/05/2023]
solution:
(D) Mathura
First capital was Purushapura (Peshawar) and second was Mathura. The Kushans controlled the Silk route from these capitals. Kushan rulers who resided in Mathura are Kanishka, Huvishka and Vasudeva. Kushans were the first in India to issue Gold coins on a wide scale.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who among the following was NOT a ruler of the Kushana dynasty? [SSC CHSL 12/04/2021]
solution:
(A) Nahapana
Kshtrap Nahapana was the Saka ruler who was defeated by Gautamiputra Satakarni.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who was the last king of the Shunga dynasty? [SSC CGL 24/07/2023]
solution:
(D) Devabhuti
Devabhuti was murdered by his minister Vasudeva Kanva, who founded Kanva dynasty (73 BC - 28 BC). Kanvas are known as 'Shungabhrityas' which means servants of Shungas.

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Consider the following eras in early India: [CAPF 2024]
1. Shaka era
2. Kalachuri era
3. Vikrama era
4. Harsha era
5. Gupta era
Which one of the following is the correct chronological order of the above eras starting with the earliest?
solution:
(D) 3-1-2-5-4
In order - Vikrama Era (57 BC), Saka Era (78 AD), Kalachuri Era (248 AD), Gupta Era (300 AD), Harsha Era (7th century AD). Vikramaditya Parmar in 57 BC defeated Sakas and started Vikram Samvat (Hindu Calendar). Ruler of Ujjain. Vikramaditya became a coveted title.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The Junagarh inscription goes on to tell us that during the reign of Rudradaman, in the year 72 of the Saka era, there occurred a terrible storm. What would be the time in CE of the occurrence of that terrible storm? [CAPF 2024]
solution:
(A) 150 CE
Saka Era - 78 AD. 72 of Saka Era means 72 + 78 = 150 CE/AD.

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Chronologically arrange the following events: [GEOSCIENTIST 2023]
1. Invasion of Alexander
2. Indo-Greek Rule in the North-West
3. Accession of Kanishka
4. Accession of Chandragupta Maurya
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
solution:
(D) 1-4-2-3
Invasion of Alexander (326 BC), Accession of Chandragupta Maurya (322 BC), Indo-Greek Rule in Northwest (2nd century BC), Accession of Kanishka (78 AD).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Kushanas were the descendants of which of the following Central Asian Tribes? [CISF AC 2021]
solution:
(D) Great Yueh-Chi
Yueh-Chi tribe were an ancient tribe of Central Asia and northwestern China. The Yueh-Chi were split into two groups, Great Yueh-Chi and Little Yueh-Chi. The Kushans were one of the five branches of Great Yueh-Chi or Yuezhi.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: When did Charaka Samhita originate? [NDA I 2022]
solution:
(B) 3rd to 2nd century BCE
Charak Samhita is a Sanskrit text about Ayurveda.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which one of the following statements about Rabatak inscription is not correct? [NDA I 2023]

options.a: It throws important light on Kushana genealogy.
options.b: It refers to Kanishka as 'a king of kings and a son of God'.
options.c: The 23-line inscription is written in Gandhari language.
options.d: It mentions names of States which were part of Kanishka's empire.

solution:
(C) The 23-line inscription is written in Gandhari language
Rabatak Inscription is written in Bactrian language. It was found in Afghanistan and it tells us about Kushana genealogy, Kanishka, and states of Kanishka.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Near which one of the following cities in India have large statues of Kushana rulers been discovered? [NDA I 2023]
solution:
(D) Mathura
Statues of Kushan emperors, including Kanishka, have been discovered near Mathura along with inscriptions like "The Great King, King of Kings, Son of God, Kanishka".

subject: Ancient History
question_text: First coins in Indian history bearing the names and images of rulers were issued by the: [NDA I 2024]
solution:
(D) Indo-Greeks
The Indo-Greeks ruled northwest parts of India. Mauryans issued punch-marked coins of silver and copper. The Kushans issued the largest hoards of gold coins.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which one of the following is a work attributed to playwright Bhasa? [IAS 2024]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The kingdoms of Chola, Chera and Pandyas belong to which period in ancient India? [SSC MTS 15/06/2023]
solution:
(B) Sangam Period
(300 BC - 300 AD). Also referred as Tamil Sangam Period of South India. The area area south of Krishna and Tungabhadra rivers. Three dynasties - the Cheras, the Cholas and the Pandyas.
King was called as - Ko, Mannam, Vendan Korravan or Iraivan. The kingdom was divided into Mandalam/Nadu (province), Ur (town), Perur (Big village), Sirur (small village).
Imp. Officials - Amaichchar (ministers), Purohitar (priests), Dutar (envoys), Senapatiyar (commander), Orar (spies).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Sangam poems mention a Tamil word 'Muvendar' which means ________. [SSC MTS 13/06/2023]
solution:
(C) Three Chiefs
Muvendar refers to heads of Cheras, Cholas and Pandyas. Thinais (known as area): each thinai had a chief called Muvender. Divided into 5 parts. Kurinci Thinai - hunters and gatherers, Palai thinai - cattle lifters and plundering, Mullai thinai - animal husbandry, Marutam thinai - Agriculture, Neytal thinai - fishing and salt collection.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Famous book Arthashastra tells us that the north-west was important for blankets, and ________ for its gold and precious stones during the Mauryan period. [SSC GD 20/02/2024]
solution: (A) South India

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Silappathikaram (the Jewelled Anklet), the earliest epic poem in Tamil was written in the 5th - 6th Century CE by ________. [SSC CGL 09/09/2024]
solution:
(D) Ilango Adigal
Silappadikaram also called 'Illiyad of Tamil Poetry'. It is a story of Kovalan, his wife Kannagi and dasi Madhavi. Love story of Kovalan and Madhavi.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following animals was the emblem of the Chola dynasty? [SSC CGL 17/09/2024]
solution:
(C) Tiger
Chola Dynasty: (early Cholas) also called Cholamandalam situated at Kaveri delta, between Pennar and Vellar river. Capital - Uraiyar (famous for cotton trade) and Puhar (alternate capital). Puhar (Kaveripattanam) was the main port. Earliest ruler - Elara, who conquered Sri Lanka. Greatest ruler - Karaikal, who fought battle of Venni and defeated Cheras and Pandyas combined. Cholas maintained an efficient navy.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Name the Chola inscription that provided details of the way in which the sabha was organised in the local administration in the Chola empire. [SSC CHSL 08/07/2024]
solution:
(B) Uttaramerur Inscription
It provides information about Kudavolai (lottery) system for electing members of assembly (sabha).
Darsanam Koppu Inscription - about land donations to temples or religious institutions.
Thiruvalangadu inscription - about reign of Rajaraja Chola I (later Chola).
Thirunanthikarai inscription - rule of Ay dynasty.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The Pandya Kingdom was first mentioned by Megasthenes, who said that their kingdom was famous for pearls. Their capital was: [SSC CGL 18/09/2024]
solution:
(C) Madurai
Pandyas : Emblem - Fish, Capital - Madurai (on the banks of Vaigai river). They ruled in Tamil Nadu. Pandyas traded with Roman empire and sent emissaries to emperors Augustus and Trojan. Earliest ruler - Mudukudumi, Greatest ruler - Nendujelian. Port city - Korkai. Pandyas were famous for pearls.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The emblem of the Chera Dynasty that controlled the central and northern parts of Kerala and the Kongu region of Tamil Nadu was ________. [SSC MTS 16/05/2023]
solution:
(C) Bow and Arrow
Cheras : Ruled in Kerala and Tamil Nadu. Emblem - Bow and Arrow. Capital - Vanji / Vanchi / Karayur. Port cities - Muziris/Muchiris and Tondi. Udiyangeral was one of the earliest known ruler. Greatest ruler - King Senguttuvan or Red Chera. He was founder of Pattini cult and worshiped Kannagi (goddess of charity). Evidence - Pugalur inscription. In Muziris, Romans built Augustus temple. Cheras also known as Keralaputras.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who among the following was a famous ruler of the Pandya dynasty of South India? [SSC MTS 18/05/2023]
solution:
(A) Nedunjeliyan II
[ref Q-7]

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Which of the following statements is correct with respect to the Sangam literature? [SSC CGL 13/12/2022]
I. These texts were supposed to be composed and compiled in assemblies of poets.
II. The Sangam literature compiled in assemblies of poets were held in the city of Madural.
solution:
(B) Both I and II
Sangam was an assembly of Tamil poets held under royal patronage of Pandyan Kings in Madurai. The 3 assemblies held in Tamil region were known as Muchchangam.
First Sangam - in Madurai, Chairman - Agastasya. Second Sangam - Kapadapuram, Chairman - Tolakapiyyar. Third Sangam - Madurai, Chairman - Nakkirar.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Anuradhapura, the old capital of Sri Lanka, was destroyed by which of the following dynasties of South India in the 10th century AD? [SSC MTS 13/11/2024]
solution:
(C) Chola
Anuradhapura Kingdom was the second established Kingdom of Sri Lanka. Mahavamsa states that it was founded by King Pandukabhaya.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following languages was used by the Sangam poets for composing the texts? [SSC MTS 05/11/2024]
solution:
(B) Tamil
The Kalabras occupied the Tamil region post Sangam between 300 AD to 600 AD. This period was called an interregnum or 'dark age' by earlier historians.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following is the epic of Tamil literature? [RPF CONSTABLE 18/01/2019]
solution:
(A) Silappadikaram
Epics of Tamil Literature - Silappadikaram, Manimekalai, Sivaga Sindamani (Jivaka Chintamani), Valayapathi, Kundalakesi. Naladiyar composed of 400 poems each of 4 lines on morals and ethics, it was a part of 18 minor works (Kilakannaku). Ettuthogai is a collection of 8 anthologies of 18 major works (Melakanakku).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which among the following Chola emperors defeated the Pandya king Maravarman Rajsimha II in the battle of Vellore? [SSC CHSL 05/07/2024]
solution:
(B) Parantaka I
Parantaka I (907 AD - 955 AD) defeated Pandya King Maravarman Rajsimha II in Battle of Vellore. Defeated Rashtrakutas in Battle of Vallala. He was defeated by Rashtrakuta king Krishna III in battle of Takkolam. Parantaka I title - "Madurai Kondan".

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which one of the following statements about Tolkappiyam is not correct? [CAPF 2022]

options.a: It is a treatise on grammar.
options.b: It was composed between first to fourth century CE.
options.c: It was part of the Sangam corpus.

solution:
(D) It was composed by Panini
In 2nd Sangam only surviving work is Tolkappiyam, work of Tamil Grammar written by Tolkapiyyar. Panini composed Ashtadhyayi on Sanskrit Grammar.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which one of the following was an important Pandya port, celebrated for its pearls in Sangam poems and Greek accounts? [CDS II 2021]
solution:
(B) Korkai
[ref Q-7 level 1]

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Arrange the following in correct chronological order: [NDA II 2023]
1. Charak Samhita
2. Bharat's Natyashastra
3. Sangam Literature
4. Ashtadhyayi
solution:
(C) 4-3-1-2
Ashtadhyayi (4th century BC), Sangam literature (300 BC - 300 AD), Charak Samhita (1st century AD), Bharata's Natyashastra (most scholars believe 2nd century AD).

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Consider the following statements about Madurai, the capital city of the Pandya kingdom. [CDS I 2024]
1. It has been described in Maduraikkanchi as a large grand city, enclosed by walls on three sides and the Vaigai river on the fourth side.
2. The Arthashastra mentions it as a centre of fine cotton textiles.
3. Other literary sources describe it as a major craft centre.
solution:
(D) 1, 2 and 3
Maduraikkanchi a poem paints a picture of Madurai as a large, vibrant city enclosed by walls on the three sides and Vaigai river on fourth side, having numerous temples and well structured administration. Arthashastra and other literary sources mentioned it as centre of fine cotton textile and centre of major crafts.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which one of the following Tamil texts deals with grammar? [CISF AC 2023]
solution:
(C) Tolkappiyam
Manimekalai - written by Sittalai Sattanar (Chittalai Chattanar), about daughter of Kovalan and Madhavi.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which one of the following explains the practice of 'Vattakirutal' as mentioned in Sangam poems? [IAS 2023]

options.b: Learned persons assembling in royal courts to discuss religious and philosophical matters.
options.c: Young girls keeping watch over agricultural fields and driving away birds and animals.
options.d: A king defeated in a battle committing ritual suicide by starving himself to death.

solution:
(D) A king defeated in a battle committing ritual suicide by starving himself to death
Vattakirutal means 'fasting facing north'. Ritual suicide done by defeated king to preserve their honour upon defeat.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which one of the following statements about Sangam literature in ancient South India is correct? [IAS 2022]

options.a: Sangam poems are devoid of any reference to material culture.
options.b: The social classification of Varna was known to Sangam poets.
options.c: Sangam poems have no reference to warrior ethic.
options.d: Sangam literature refers to magical forces as irrational.

solution:
(B) The social classification of Varna was known to Sangam poets
The social classification in Sangam poems - Arasar (kings/ruling class), Anthanar (Brahmins), Vanigar (traders/vaishyas), Vellalar (farmers/shudras).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: With reference to ancient South India, Korkai, Poompuhar and Muchiri were well known as [IAS 2023]
solution: (B) Ports

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Rudrasena II of which of the following dynasties married Prabhavatigupta, the daughter of Chandragupta II of the Gupta dynasty? [SSC CGL 10/09/2024]
solution:
(C) Vakataka
Vakataka dynasty (around 3rd century AD): Founder - Vindhyashakti, feudatory of the Satvahanas. Followed Brahminism. Capital - Nandivardhana (Nagardhan, Maharashtra). Pravarasena I was powerful ruler. Vakatakas constructed temples at Ajanta Caves.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: After the fall of the Guptas, different kingdoms emerged in various parts of India. Among them, the Maukhahris emerged in which of the following areas? [SSC CGL 13/09/2024]
solution:
(B) Kannauj
[ref Q-5 level 1 Post Mauryes]

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Which of the following statements is/are correct about the rule of Chandragupta II? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 11/12/2024]
A. Chandragupta II adopted the title 'Vikramaditya'.
B. Kalidasa and Amarasimha were part of his court.
solution:
(B) Both A and B
Chandragupta II (380 - 414 AD) : assassinated his brother Ramgupta. Chandragupta II married Dhruvadevi (Ramgupta's widow). Title - "Vikramaditya". He married to Kubernaga of Naga dynasty. Married his daughter Prabhavatigupta with Vakataka prince Rudrasen II. He defeated Sakas. First Gupta to issue silver coins. Fa-hien (Chinese traveller) visited during his reign. Kalidasa and Amarsimha were part of his court's Navaratnas.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who among the following was the first independent Gupta king to hold the title of Maharajadhiraj? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 11/12/2024]
solution:
(D) Chandragupta I
Chandragupta I (319 - 334 AD): Title - Maharajadhiraja. He married to Kumaradevi (Lichchhavi princess). He started Gupta Era in 319-20 AD. Gold coins - Dinaras. Most number of gold coins - Guptas.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Samudragupta's achievements are recorded in a long inscription called Prashasti by his court poet, ________. [SSC STENOGRAPHER 12/10/2023]
solution:
(A) Harisena
Harisena was a court poet in Samudragupta's reign. He composed Prayag Prashasti (Allahabad Pillar Inscription) about Samudragupta. Other works of Harisena - Apabhramsa Dharmapariksa, Karpuraprakara, Yashodharacanta, Brhatkathakosa, etc. Kalhan was the author of Rajtarangini, a chronicle of Kashmir's history, during the reign of Jayasimha (1128 - 1149 AD).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The paintings in the Bagh caves belong to which of the following periods? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 13/10/2023]
solution:
(D) Gupta
Founder - Sri Gupta, succeeded by his son Ghatotkacha. Bagh cave paintings are found in Dhar district of Madhya Pradesh.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The Vakataka dynasty was directly related to which Gupta emperor? [SSC CGL 20/07/2023]
solution:
(D) Chandra Gupta II
[ref Q-1,2]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: In which period is the first inscriptional example of the practice of Sati found? [SSC CGL 26/07/2023]
solution:
(D) Gupta
The first example of Sati appears in Gupta time in 510 AD in Eran in Madhya Pradesh (Bhanugupta's Eran Inscription). Women were denied property rights except Stridhana (jewellery).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The famous source of the Gupta empire, the Prayaga Prashasti is also known as the ________. [SSC PHASE XI 27/06/2023]
solution:
(A) Allahabad pillar inscription
[ref Q-4]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who is given the credit for starting the Gupta era? [SSC PHASE XI 27/06/2023]
solution:
(D) Chandragupta I
[ref Q-4]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Amar singh was one of the luminaries in the court of ________. [SSC PHASE XI 30/06/2023]
solution:
(B) Chandragupta II
Navratnas of Chandragupta II - Kalidasa, Amarsingh, Dhanvantari (Navantikam - Medicine text), Varahamihira (Pancha Siddhantaka, Brihatsamhita), Vararuchi (Vartika - about Ashtadhyayi and Prakrita Prakasha), Harisena, Kahapanaka (finance and economics), Sanku (architecture), Vetalbhatta (Mantrashashtra).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The ________ period has been described as the "golden age" of Indian history? [SSC CPO 16/03/2019]
solution: (C) Gupta

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who among the following Navratnas from Chandragupta II court wrote 'Mantrashastra'? [SSC MTS 12/05/2023]
solution:
(C) Vetala Bhatta
Kalidasa wrote - Abhigyan Shakuntalam, Malvikagnimitra, Raghuvamsa, Meghaduta, Kumar Sambhavam, Ritu Samhara. Kalidasa is also known as 'Shakespeare of India'. Amarsimha wrote - Amarakosha (Sanskrit grammar).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The reign of Kumaragupta I the son and successor of ________ was one of peace and relative inactivity. [SSC MTS 13/06/2023]
solution:
(C) Chandragupta II
Kumaragupta I (415-455 AD): Son of Chandragupta II and Dhruvadevi. Founded Nalanda University. Title - Mahendraditya. Kumaragupta was the worshipper of God Kartikeya.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which king was shown on his coins as a Veena player? [SSC MTS 14/06/2023]
solution:
(C) Samudragupta
Samudragupta (335 - 380 AD): Greatest and Most powerful king of Guptas. He was never defeated and called 'Napolean of India' by VA Smith. Record of his reign - Prayag Prashasti (Allahabad pillar inscription) by Harisena. Titles - Kaviraja, Param Bhagavat, Sarva-raj-ochchetta (uprooter of all kings). Performed Ashvamedha Yajna. He was a shown Playing Veena on coins.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: After the victory over Sakas, who among the following adopted the title of 'Vikramaditya'? [SSC MTS 20/06/2023]
solution:
(B) Chandragupta II
Skandagupta (455-461 AD): He successfully defeated Huns (Central Asia tribe) and adopted title 'Vikramaditya' (Bhitari Pillar Inscription). Other title - Kramaditya.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who among the following succeeded Ghatotkacha, the Gupta ruler? [SSC MTS 20/06/2023]
solution: (B) Chandragupta I

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Ruler of which dynasty built the famous Iron pillar in Delhi? [SSC CHSL 10/03/2023]
solution:
(A) Gupta
Iron Pillar of Delhi, Mehrauli Iron Pillar was built by Chandragupta II.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following offices was NOT held by the poet Harishena? [SSC CGL 03/12/2022]
solution:
(C) Nagara-shreshthi
Offices held by Harisena: Maha-danda-nayaka (Chief judicial officer), Kumar-amatya (provincial minister), Sandhi-vigrahika (minister of war and peace). He was not Nagar-shreshthi (chief banker of merchant of the city).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Fa Xian began his journey back home from which of the following Indian state? [SSC CGL 08/12/2022]
solution:
(C) Bengal
Fa-Xian started his return journey by ship from Bengal. He was a Chinese Buddhist monk, who visited India around 4th century.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Xuan Zang and other pilgrims spent time studying in Nalanda, the most famous Buddhist monastery, located in which of the following Indian states? [SSC CGL 13/12/2022]
solution:
(C) Bihar
Kumaragupta I, founded the Nalanda Mahavihara or University.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Samudragupta's mother belonged to which of the following gana? [SSC CGL 13/12/2022]
solution:
(B) Lichchhavi
Samudragupta was son of Chandragupta I and Kumaradevi (Lichchhavi princess). [ref Q-15]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following titles was held by Samudragupta, a ruler of the Gupta Dynasty? [SSC MTS 25/07/2022]
solution:
(C) Kaviraja
[ref Q-15]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: In the context of Gupta administration, the term 'vithi' referred to a/an ________. [SSC MTS 05/10/2021]
solution:
(B) Administrative unit
Administrative Units and Heads : Bhukti or Bhoga (Province) - Uparika / Bhogapati. Vishaya (district) - Vishayapati / Ayukta. Vithika / Nagar (city) - Nagarpati / Purapala. Gram (village) - Gramika.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who among the following Gupta rulers married a girl from the Lichchhavi clan? [SSC MTS 08/10/2021]
solution:
(C) Chandragupta I
[ref Q-4]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Prabhavati Gupta, the Queen Regent of the Vakataka empire, was the daughter of Kuberanaga and ________. [SSC CHSL 13/04/2021]
solution:
(C) Chandragupta II
[ref Q-3]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The Gupta imposed a fine called ________ which was a plough tax paid by every cultivator owning a plough. [SSC CGL 03/03/2020]
solution:
(D) Halivakara
Taxes: Bhaga - 1/6th of produce paid by cultivators. Uparikara - Extra tax. Bhoga - periodic supplies of fruits, fire wood, flowers etc to king. Bali - became oppressive tax. Hiranya - Taxes collected in cash of gold. Shulka - Custom or toll tax. Kara - periodic tax. Senabhakti - The army was to be fed by people whenever it passed through the countryside.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following was NOT a part of the "Navratna" at Vikramaditya's court? [SSC CPO 16/03/2019]
solution:
(A) Surdas
Surdas (1478-1583 AD) was a blind poet, who spent his life writing about Lord Krishna. He was contemporary to Akbar. Spent most of his life in Agra and Mathura.

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Which of the following statements is/are correct about the rule of Chandragupta II? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 11/12/2024]
A. Chandragupta II adopted the title 'Vikramaditya'.
B. Kalidasa and Amarasimha were part of his court.
solution:
(D) Both A and B
[ref Q-3]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: During the Gupta period, Gold coins were called by which names? [SSC CHSL 03/07/2019]
solution:
(D) Dinaras
Guptas issued most number of Gold coins called Dinaras. These coins often depict the ruler or a deity. Samudragupta is depicted as playing Veena in these coins. Types of coins - Garuda type, Dhanurdhari (archer type), Axe type, Ashvamedha coin, Lion slayer coin, Chhatra coin, marriage coin, etc.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who among the following was a Gupta ruler? [SSC CPO 13/12/2019]
solution:
(B) Vikramaditya
Chandragupta II also known as Vikramaditya.

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Consider the following eras in early India: [CAPF 2024]
1. Shaka era
2. Kalachuri era
3. Vikrama era
4. Harsha era
5. Gupta era
Which one of the following is the correct chronological order of the above eras starting with the earliest?
solution:
(D) 3-1-2-5-4
In order - Vikrama Era (57 BC), Saka Era (78 AD), Kalachuri Era (248 AD), Gupta Era (300 AD), Harsha Era (7th century AD). Vikramaditya Parmar in 57 BC defeated Sakas and started Vikram Samvat (Hindu Calendar). Ruler of Ujjain. Vikramaditya became a coveted title.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who among the following ancient Indian kings was praised in glowing terms in the Prayaga Prashasti? [CDS II 2021]
solution:
(C) Samudragupta
[ref Q-15 level 1]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Where is the famous Gupta period Deogarh Temple situated? [CDS II 2023]
solution:
(A) Uttar Pradesh
Dashavatara Temple is located in Deogarh (UP), near Betwa river. It is dedicated to Lord Vishnu. It was built by Gupta kings during 6th century AD.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who among the following kings is described in an inscription as the Supreme Being, the cause of the prosperity of the good and the destruction of the bad? [CISF AC 2023]
solution:
(C) Samudragupta
In Prayag Prashasti is called as - an antagonist on earth, He has wiped off the fame of other kings with the soles of his feet; He is Purusha (the supreme being), being the cause of the prosperity of the good and destruction of the bad. He is incomprehensible, the giver of hundred thousands of cows; he is equal to the Kubera (god of wealth), Varuna (god of ocean), Indra (god of rains) and Yama (god of death).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which among the following was not a member of the Adhishthana Adhikarana of Kotivarsha in the Gupta period? [CISF AC 2024]
solution:
(B) Mahattara
Vishayapati administered the Vishayas (districts) with the help of Adhishthana Adhikarana (council of representatives), four representatives - Nagarasreshthi, Sarthavaha, Prathama-Kulika and Prathama-Kayastha. Mahattara was a village headman for administration and revenue collection.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The typical Nagara style Shikhara is visible in which one of the following temples? [GEOSCIENTIST 2023]
solution: (B) Dashavatara Temple, Deogarh

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which Chinese traveller in ancient India wrote the diary called 'Records of the Travels to Middle India'? [NDA I 2022]
solution:
(B) Xuanzang
Xuanzang (Hiuen Tsang) visited India during the reign of Harshavardhana in 7th century AD. He translated Indian manuscripts from Sanskrit to Chinese. "Si-yu-ki" or 'The records of the Western World' also written by him.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which one of the following was composed by Harishena? [NDA II 2022]
solution:
(B) Prayaga Prashasti of Samudragupta
[ref Q-5 level 1]

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Consider the following statements about the literary language Apabhramsha: [CDS I 2025]
1. It appeared in recognizable forms by the sixth century.
2. It developed out of mixtures of regional vernaculars with the Prakrits and Sanskrit.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Mahendravarman I was the ruler of which of the following dynasties? [SSC CGL 09/09/2024]
solution:
(C) Pallava
Pallava dynasty (575-897 AD): Founder - Simha Vishnu, Capital - Kanchipuram. Greatest ruler - Narsimhavarman I. Pallavas established their authority in the Tondaimandalam or the land of creepers.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The ________ were tributary chiefs of the Gupta dynasty. They established an independent kingdom in western India. Dhruvasena II was the most important ruler of this kingdom. [SSC CGL 12/09/2024]
solution:
(C) Maitrakas
Dhruvasena II was the most important ruler and was contemporary to Harshavardhana. He lost war with Harsha but later married his daughter. Capital of Maitrakas was Vallabhi. Maitrakas (5th to 8th century AD) was founded by Senapati Bhatarka, a Gupta general. Followed - Shaivism.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Pushyabhuti, who ruled from Thaneswar, was the founder of ________ dynasty. [SSC CGL 17/08/2021]
solution:
(A) Vardhana
Pushyabhuti / Vardhana dynasty (550-647 AD): founder - Pushyabhuti, in Thaneswar (Haryana). He was succeeded by his son Prabhakarvardhana, who was succeeded by his eldest son Rajyavardhana. He killed Deva Gupta (ruler of Malwa) but was killed by Shashanka (Gaud ruler of Bengal).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Mahabalipuram emerged as an important centre of temple architecture under which of the following kingdoms of south India? [SSC CGL 10/09/2024]
solution:
(B) Pallava
Mahabalipuram (Tamil Nadu) was the major port city of Pallavas. It was founded by Pallava King Narsimhavarman I, who is also known as 'Mamalla' and the city also known as Mamallapuram.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Vallabhi, a city of ancient India was the capital of the ________ dynasty from the 5th to 8th Century CE. [SSC CGL 11/09/2024]
solution:
(B) Maitraka
[ref Q-2]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Vindhyashakti was the founder of which of the following dynasties? [SSC CGL 13/09/2024]
solution:
(D) Vakataka
Vakatakas: founded by Vindhyashakti (255 AD - 275 AD). Followed Brahminism. Initially the capital of Vakatakas was Nandivardhana but later shifted to Pravarapura. Vastagulma was the capital of Vastagulma branch of Vakatakas.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who among the following Pallava kings occupied Vatapi (Badami) and defeated the Chalukyas? [SSC CGL 26/09/2024]
solution:
(D) Narasimhavarman
[ref Q-1]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The Rashtrakuta king who conquered the Cholas was: [SSC STENOGRAPHER 10/12/2024]
solution:
(C) Krishna III
The Rashtrakuta king Krishna III defeated the Chola king Parantaka I in the battle of Takkolam (948-49 AD) and took control of Tondaimandalam.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Maitraka dynasty was founded in which part of India? [SSC MTS 07/10/2024]
solution:
(D) Western
Maitraka dynasty ruled in the region of Gujarat and Saurashtra, with their capital at Vallabhi.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The last Chalukyan King, defeated by the Rashtrakuta King Dantidurga, was ________. [SSC MTS 15/10/2024]
solution:
(C) Kirtivarman II
Chalukyan king Kirtivarman II was defeated by Rashtrakuta king Dantidurga in 753 AD. Dantidurga ended Badami Chalukya dynasty and established Rashtrakuta dynasty, which ruled in the Karnataka region.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The sister of Harshavardhana Rajyashri was married to ________, the king of Maukhari dynasty of Kanyakubja. [SSC MTS 07/10/2024]
solution:
(A) Grahavarman
Harshavardhana's sister Rajyashri married to Maukhari king Grahavarman. Grahavarman was later killed by Deva Gupta of Malwa in alliance with Gaud ruler of Bengal Shashanka and Rajyashri was imprisoned. Rajyavardhana, brother of Rajyashri and Harsha killed Deva Gupta but was killed by Shashanka. Then, Harshavardhana killed Shashanka, rescued Rajyashri in 606 AD and ascended the throne of Pushyabhuti dynasty.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Mahendravarman I who wrote Mattavilasaprahasana is related to which of the following ruling dynasties? [SSC CGL 24/09/2024]
solution:
(D) Pallava
Mattabilasaprahasana is a short one-act Sanskrit play.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The Shore Temple at Mahabalipuram was built during the reign of the ________ king Narasimhavarman II. [SSC MTS 17/10/2024]
solution:
(D) Pallava
Temples during Narsimhavarman II (Rajasimha) - Kailashanatha and Vaikuntha Perumal Temple at Kanchi, Shore temple at Mamallapuram.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following Rashtrakuta rulers performed the ceremony called 'Hiranya Garbha'? [SSC MTS 13/11/2024]
solution: (B) Dantidurga

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who was the first feudatory of Pallavas? [SSC MTS 17/10/2024]
solution:
(D) Vijayalaya
Vijayalaya was the founder of Chola dynasty.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following was the capital of the Pallava Dynasty? [SSC MTS 11/11/2024]
solution:
(C) Kanchipuram
[ref Q-1]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Harshvardhan's ambitions of extending his power to Deccan and South India were stopped by ________. [SSC MTS 15/10/2024]
solution:
(B) Pulakesin II
Harshavardhana's conquests towards Deccan were stopped by Pulakesin II of Chalukya dynasty of Vatapi on the banks of Narmada river. It was only defeat of Harshavardhana. The Chalukyan records describe Harshavardhana as the lord of whole of Northern country (Sakalottarapatheshvara).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The rock-cut caves in Karnataka, mainly at Badami and Aihole, were built under the patronage of the ________. [SSC MTS 12/11/2024]
solution:
(C) Chalukyas
Chalukyas of Vatapi/Badami(543-755 AD): Founder - Jayasimha, Capital - Vatapi (Karnataka). Chalukyas started Vesara or Deccan style architecture, mix of Nagara (north) and Dravida (south) style. Aihole pillar inscription - description about Pulakesin II was composed by Ravikirti (court poet). Other temples of Chalukyas - Ravanphadi caves, Ladh Khan temple, Durga temple, Huchimalligudi temple (Aihole) in Vesara style. Papanath temple (Pattadakal) in Nagara style, Virupaksha temple and Sangameshvara temple (Pattadakal) in Dravida style.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Pulakeshin II was the famous ruler of which of the following dynasties. [SSC MTS 14/11/2024]
solution:
(C) Chalukya
Pulakeshin II (610 - 642 AD) - Greatest ruler of Chalukyas. Defeated Harshavardhana (Pushyabhuti) and assumed title of 'Dakshinapatheshwara' (Lord of South). Defeated Mahendravarman I (Pallava ruler). He was defeated by Narshimhavarman I, who took the title 'Vatapikonda'. Pulakesin II sent ambassador to Persian King Khusrau in 625 AD and also received one from him. The Chinese traveller Hiuen-Tsang visited his kingdom.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who was the court poet of king Harshavardhana? [SSC MTS 30/09/2024]
solution:
(D) Banabhatta
Harshavardhana (606-647 AD): also known as 'Siladitya', He rescued his sister Rajyashri from Vindhyan forest and defeated Shashanka (Gaud ruler of Bengal). He also conquered Magadha. Defeated Dhruvasena II (Maitraka ruler of Vallabhi) and married his daughter with him. He was defeated by Pulakesin II. He sent an envoy to Tai-Tsung, Tang emperor of China in 641 AD. Hiuen Tsang visited Harsha's reign and spent eight years. Banabhatta wrote Harshacharita and Kadambari. Mayur (court) wrote Mayur Shataka and Surya Shataka.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: According to which of the following foreign travellers did Dhruvasena II attend Harsha's assembly at Prayag (Allahabad)? [SSC CGL TIER-II 26/10/2023]
solution:
(B) Hiuen Tsang
Hiuen Tsang mentions two important assemblies during Harsha's reign. Kannauj Assembly (643 AD) in the honour of Hiuen-Tsang and to popularize Buddhism. Prayag Assembly to celebrate religious festivals at sangam of Ganga, Yamuna and Saraswati rivers. Dhruvasena II attended Prayag Assembly.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Pulakeshin II assumed the title of 'Dakshinapatheshvara' (lord of the south) after defeating which of the following rulers of north India? [SSC CGL 25/09/2024]
solution:
(B) Harshavardhana
[ref Q-19]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: In peninsular India, which of the following kingdoms was the local power that ruled over northern Maharashtra and Vidarbha? [SSC CGL 19/09/2024]
solution:
(B) Vakatakas
Vakataka dynasty was founded by Vindhyashakti around 3rd century AD.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: What was the period of Harshavardhana's reign? [SSC CGL 25/07/2023]
solution:
(C) 606 CE to 647 CE
[ref Q-19]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following is NOT correct regarding king Harshavardhana? [SSC CGL 02/12/2022]

options.a: Harshavardhana ruled nearly about 1400 years ago.
options.b: Xuan Zang spent a lot of time at Harsha's court.
options.d: Harshacharita is a biography written on Harshavardhana.

solution:
(C) Harshavardhana's court poet was Harishena
The court poet of Harshavardhana was Banabhatta, who wrote Harshacharita. Harisena was the court poet of Samudragupta.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: King Shashanka, against whom Harshvardhana declared war, was a ruler of the ________ kingdom. [SSC MTS 14/10/2021]
solution:
(D) Gauda II
King Shashanka (7th century) - Gauda kingdom in Eastern part of India. Shashanka killed Rajyavardhan, the King of Pushyabhuti dynasty and brother of Harshvardhana. He was killed by Harsha in his conquest.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: King Harshavardhan ascended the throne of Thaneshwar and Kannauj on the death of his brother, ________. [SSC CGL 04/03/2020]
solution:
(C) Rajyavardhana
[ref Q-11]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The Caves of Elephanta are predominantly dedicated to which of the following deities? [SSC MTS 26/10/2021]
solution:
(A) Lord Shiva
The Elephanta Caves are collection of cave temples dedicated to Lord Shiva. Located in Maharashtra. Large sculptures of Sadashiv, Nataraja and Yogishvara etc. are found. UNESCO site in 1987.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Ravikirti was the court poet of which of the following dynasty rulers? [SSC CGL 03/12/2022]
solution:
(D) Chalukyas
[ref Q-18]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Initially who among the following were subordinate to the Chalukyas of Karnataka? [SSC PHASE XI 28/06/2023]
solution:
(A) Rashtrakutas
Rashtrakutas (752 - 973 AD): Founder - Dantidurga, who fixed his capital at Malkhand/Malkhed (Karnataka). He performed Hiranya-Garbh ritual. Krishna I (son of Dantidurga) built Kailasha Temple at Ellora, it was a monolithic stone temple. Greatest rulers - Govinda III and Amoghvarsha.
