Amoghvarsha wrote Kavirajamarga, the earliest Kannada book on Poetics.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The Jain temple at Pattadakal was built by ________. [SSC MTS 02/05/2023]
solution:
(C) Rashtrakutas
The Jain temple also known as Narayana temple at Pattadakal was built by Rashtrakutas during 9th century AD.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: In the early eleventh century, the Chola king Rajendra I built a Shiva temple and filled it with a Sun-pedestal seized from the ________. [SSC CGL 03/12/2022]
solution: (C) Chalukyas

subject: Ancient History
question_text: In the mid-eighth century, Dantidurga, a Rashtrakuta chief, overthrew his Chalukya overlord and performed a ritual literally called a ________. [SSC MTS 16/05/2023]
solution:
(B) Golden Womb
Also called 'Hiranya-Garbh' ritual. It is a ritual to grant the person rebirth as a Kshatriya, even if not born into that.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: After about 75 years of Harshavardhana's death, ________ rose to power in Kannauj. [SSC STENOGRAPHER 13/10/2023]
solution:
(A) Yashovarman
Yashovarman emerged as the ruler of Kannauj and established Varman dynasty.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Malavikagnimitra, a drama of the 5th century CE, was written by ________. [SSC CPO 03/10/2023]
solution:
(A) Kalidasa
Kalidasa was the court poet of Chandragupta II, his famous works are Malvikagnimitra, Abhigyan Shakuntalam, Meghaduta etc. Harisena was the court poet of Samudragupta, he composed Prayag Prashasti at Allahabad Pillar Inscription. Bhavabhuti was the court poet of Yashovarman of Kannauj, he wrote Malatimadhava, Mahaviracharita and Uttararamacharita. Pingala was an ancient Mathematician of India, worked on the binary system and Fibonacci sequence, mentioned in his book Chandahsastra.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: During the reign of which of the following Pallavas, Hiuen Tsang visited the Pallava capital Kanchi? [SSC MTS 02/05/2023]
solution:
(A) Narasimhavarman I
Pallava King Narsimhavarman I (630-668 AD) - defeated Pulakesin II (Chalukyas of Vatapi) and assumed the title of Vatapikonda, conqueror of Vatapi.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who among the following was the last Pallava king? [SSC MTS 11/05/2023]
solution:
(B) Aparajitavarman
Aditya I of Chola dynasty killed Aparajitavarman (880 - 897 AD).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who sent an envoy to the Chinese court in 641 AD? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 13/10/2023]
solution:
(B) Harsha Vardhan
[ref Q-20]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which one of the following books was authored by Harshavardhana? [CAPF 2022]
solution:
(C) Ratnavali
King Harshavardhana authored Ratnavali, Priyadarshika, Nagananda in Sanskrit. Kadambari and Harshacharita was written by Banabhatta (court poet of Harshavardhana). Gita Govinda was written by Jayadeva during the reign of King Kamarnava Deva of Ganga dynasty of Odisha.

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Which of the following pairs are correctly matched? [CAPF 2023]
(Inscription/Event) - (Time Period)
1. Prayag Prashasti by Harishena - 4th century CE
2. Chinese traveller Fa Xian's Account - 6th century CE
3. Mudrarakshasa of Vishakhadatta - 5th century CE
4. Harshacharita of Banabhatta - 6th century CE
5. Aihole Prashasti of Ravikirti - 7th century CE
6. Kavirajamarga of Amoghavarsha - 8th century CE
solution:
(D) 1, 3 and 5 only
Correct time period: Prayag Prashasti by Harisena - 350 CE or 4th century CE. Fa-Xian's account - Early 5th century. Mudrarakshasa of Vishakhadatta - around 5th century CE. Harshacharita of Banabhatta - 7th century CE. Aihole Prashasti of Ravikirti - 7th century. Kavirajamarga of Amoghavarsha - 9th century CE.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who among the following usurped power in Kanyakubja after the death of Harshavardhana? [CDS II 2022]
solution:
(B) Arunashva
Adityasena was a ruler of Gupta dynasty and he ruled in Magadha region. Arunashva usurped power in Kanyakubja after death of Harshavardhana (no heir of Harsha). Shashanka was the king of Gauda in Bengal and was killed by Harshavardhana. Lalitaditya was the ruler of Karkota dynasty of Kashmir.

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Consider the following pairs:
King - Dynasty
1. Nannuka - Chandela
2. Jayashakti - Paramara
3. Nagabhata II - Gurjara-Pratihara
4. Bhoja - Rashtrakuta
How many pairs given above are correctly matched?
solution:
(B) Only two pairs
Nannuka (831 - 845 CE) was the founder of Chandela dynasty in Bundelkhand region (MP). Jayashakti was also the king of Chandela dynasty and was successor of Vijayashakti. Nagabhata II (795 - 833 CE) was a king of Gurjara-Pratihara dynasty, succeeding his father Vatsaraja. Nagabhata's son Rambhadra ruled for short period and then his son Mihir Bhoja succeeded him. Bhoja was the greatest ruler of this dynasty. He was a devotee of Vishnu and adopted title 'Adivarah'. The Pratiharas ruled first at Ujjain and later shifted to Kannauj.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which one among the following temples, constructed by Queen Loka Mahadevi in Pattadakal, is a UNESCO World Heritage site? [GEOSCIENTIST 2024]
solution:
(B) Virupaksha Temple
Queen Loka Mahadevi wife of Chalukyan king Vikramaditya II, built Virupaksha temple to commemorate his victory over the kings of south. Kailasanatha Temple was built by Pallava king Rajsimha or Narasimhavarman II. Brihadishwara temple at Tanjavur was built by Chola king Rajaraja Chola I. The Sriranganathaswamy Temple in Srirangam is built by Chola ruler Dharmavarma.

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
From the decline of Guptas until the rise of Harshavardhana in the early seventh century, which of the following kingdoms were holding power in Northern India?
1. The Guptas of Magadha
2. The Paramaras of Malwa
3. The Pushyabhutis of Thanesar
4. The Maukharis of Kanauj
5. The Yadavas of Devagiri
6. The Maitrakas of Valabhi
solution:
(B) 1, 3, 4 and 6
The later Guptas or the Guptas of Magadha ruled in northern India after the decline of Guptas during 6th and 8th century AD. The Parmara dynasty ruled western and central India between 9th to 14th century AD. The Pushyabhutis of Thanesar ruled parts of northern India during 6th and 7th century. The Maukharis ruled over UP, Magadha and Kannauj till 606 AD. The Yadavas of Devagiri ruled around 1187 to 1317 AD in western parts of India. The Maitrakas of Vallabhi ruled western and northern parts around 5th to 8th century AD.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The Tripartite struggle happened among the Palas, the Pratiharas and the ________. [SSC CGL 26/09/2024]
solution:
(A) Rashtrakutas
Tripartite Struggle (around 8th century), there were three great powers - The Palas in the East, the Gurjara-Pratiharas in the north and the Rashtrakutas in the Deccan. All wanted to possess the city of Kannauj.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: ________ assumed the title of "Gangaikondachola" or the conqueror of the river Ganga. [SSC CGL 11/06/2019]
solution:
(C) Rajendra Chola
[ref Q-21]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Of which of the following Pratihara kings was Rajashekhara, the famous poet and dramatist, the court poet? [SSC CGL 25/07/2023]
solution:
(D) Mahendrapala
Rajashekhara was a Sanskrit poet and dramatist, known for 'Karpuramanjari' and 'Kavyamimamsa'. He was the court poet of Mahendrapala I and his son Mahipala I around 10th century. He described himself as the teacher of Mahendrapala.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Some important administrative posts were hereditary during the Rashtrakuta and Chola dynasties. The post "nagara-shreshthi" meant? [SSC CGL 03/12/2022]
solution:
(B) Merchant of the city
Maha-danda-nayaka (chief judicial officer), Nagara-shreshthi (top banker or merchant of the city), Sandhi-vigrahika (minister of war and peace), Kumar-amatya (important provincial minister).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: In which of the following temples will you find Gopurams? [SSC CGL 13/08/2021]
solution:
(A) Shiva Temple, Chidambaram
Gopurams are the entrance gateways of a temple. Some imp temples with gopurams - Chidambaram Nataraja Temple, Ekambareswar Temple (Kanchipuram), Ranganathaswamy temple (Srirangam), Virupaksha temple (Hampi), Srivilliputhur Andal Temple (Tamil Nadu).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following Gurjara-Pratihara kings was highly praised by the Arabian scholar Sulaiman for keeping his empire safe from robbers? [RRB RPF SI 03/12/2024]
solution:
(D) Mihira Bhoja
Sulaiman was an Arab traveller, visited India during the reign of Mihira Bhoja (836 - 885 AD) also known as Bhoja I. He was the son of Ramabhadra. Bhoja was the devotee of Lord Vishnu and adopted the title 'Adivaraha'. Gwalior Prashasti of Mihira Bhoja was composed by Baladitya.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who among the following founded Gurjara-Pratihara dynasty after winning Kannauj in the tripartite struggle? [RRB RPF SI 03/12/2024]
solution:
(C) Nagabhata I
Gurjara-Pratihara Dynasty (8th century - 11th century). They ruled over northern and western parts of India. Nagabhata I was the founder of this dynasty. He ruled over the region of Malwa (MP) with the capital Ujjain.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The Pratiharas who first had their capital at Bhinmal gained prominence during the rule of: [SSC CGL 24/09/2024]
solution:
(A) Nagabhatta I
Nagabhata I established his capital at Malwa region from Bhinmal.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following universities was founded by Pala rulers? [SSC CHSL 14/08/2023]
solution:
(A) Vikramasila
Pala king Dharampala founded Vikramshila University in 8th - 9th century CE, it was a major center for Buddhist studies.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who among the following founded the Pala dynasty in 8th Century CE? [SSC CGL 23/09/2024]
solution:
(B) Gopala
The Pala dynasty was founded by Gopala during 750 AD in Bengal region. Capital - Munger (Mudgagiri) in Bihar. Last ruler of Palas was Govinda Pala. Gopala founded Odantapuri university in 8th century.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Dharmapala is known to have founded which of the following universities near Bhagalpur in Bihar? [SSC CPO 29/06/2024]
solution:
(B) Vikramashila
Vajradatta (Buddhist scholar) was the court poet of Devapala and known for his work Lokesvarastatakarastotra. Sandhyakar Nandi was the court poet of Pala dynasty, famous for his work Ramacharitam.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The star-shaped temples were built during the reign of: [SSC CPO 05/10/2023]
solution:
(A) Hoysala
The star-shaped temples in Deccan region were built by Hoysala kings, notably King Vishnuvardhana. Hoysala temples are known for Vesara style of architecture.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The Pala rulers achieved their domination in which province of India? [SSC JE 05/06/2024]
solution: (D) Bengal

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who among the following rulers was a part of the Tripartite Struggle for the possession of Kannauj? [SSC CHSL 05/07/2024]
solution: (C) Dharmapala

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which Pala ruler temporarily occupied Kanauj? [SSC CHSL 03/07/2024]
solution: (A) Dharmapala

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Arrange the following rulers of the Pala dynasty in the chronological order? [RRB RPF SI 09/12/2024]
solution:
(A) Dharampal < Devpal < Mahipal < Naypal
Dharampala (770 - 810 AD), Devapala (810 - 850 AD), Mahipala (988 - 1038 AD), Nayapala (1038 - 1053 AD).

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following statements about the Pala Dynasty of eastern India is INCORRECT? [SSC CHSL 03/07/2024]

options.a: Devapala was the grandson of Gopala.
options.c: The Pala kings were the followers of Buddhism.
options.d: The Pala dynasty was founded by Gopala.

solution:
(B) Dharmapala founded the Valabhi university
Valabhi University was founded by Maitraka ruler Senapati Bhatarka in 5th century.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who founded the Chola empire? [SSC CHSL 03/08/2023]
solution:
(D) Vijayalaya
The Chola empire was founded by Vijayalaya during 850 AD. He captured Kaveri delta from Muttaraiyar rulers and established Chola Empire. He established the city of Tanjore (Thanjavur). Vijayalaya took the title of "Parakesarivarman" and "Narakesari". He was feudatory to Pallavas. Vijayalaya built a temple for goddess Nishumbhasudini (Durga) in Tanjore.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The term "Vetti" in Chola empire is related to ________. [SSC MTS 14/06/2023]
solution:
(C) forced labour
Vetti is a form of labour tax, where common people were compelled to work for the king without any wages. Kadamai was a land revenue tax.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Rajendra I was the son of which of the following Chola kings? [SSC PHASE X 02/08/2022]
solution:
(B) Rajaraja I
Raja Raja I (985 - 1014 AD) - Defeated Cheras at Trivendrum, Defeated Pandyas and conquered their capital Madurai. After this victory he adopted the title "Pandya Kulashani" (thunderbolt to Pandyas). He rename the region as Rajaraja Mandalam.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: In the 11th century, the army of which of the following Cholas reached upto Ganga to invade Bengal? [SSC CHSL 04/08/2023]
solution:
(A) Rajendra I
Rajendra I (1014 - 1044 AD) - son of Rajaraja Chola. Also known as 'Napolean of South India'. Completely overran Cheras and Pandyas. Completely conquered Sri Lanka. He crossed Ganga and defeated Pala dynasty of Bengal and took the title of "Gangaikonda Chola". Founded new capital city Gangaikondacholapuram. Carried naval expeditions against Sri Vijaya Empire and Shailendra dynasty in Southeast Asia.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: One of the greatest of Chola ruler Rajaraja I ruled from 985 AD to ________. [SSC CGL 13/09/2024]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: In south India, the ________ was a village assembly found in areas where the landowners were not brahmins. [SSC GD 23/02/2024]
solution:
(C) UR
2 Assemblies: Ur - assembly for common people, Sabha - assembly of learned Brahmins. Agrahars - Village or settlements of Brahmins.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which amongst the following terms was used for the land given to Brahmins during the Chola empire? [SSC CHSL 01/07/2024]
solution:
(A) Brahmadeya
Vellanvagai - land donated to non-Brahmins. Devadana / Tirunamattukkani - land donated to temples. Pallichchhandam - land donated to jain temples. Shalabhoga - land granted for schools. Brahmadeya - land gifted to Brahmins.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Name the Chola inscription that provided details of the way in which the sabha was organised in the local administration in the Chola empire. [SSC CHSL 08/07/2024]
solution:
(B) Uttaramerur inscription
Darsanam Koppu inscription - about the land grants and donations made to temples. Thiruvalangadu inscription - the reign of Rajaraja Chola I, it includes his conquests in South India, Sri Lanka, Maldives. Thirunanthikarai inscription - about the land grants made during the reign of Ay kings.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which Indian dynasty is associated with the creation of highly skillful bronze sculptures? [SSC MTS 15/10/2024]
solution:
(B) Chola
Nataraja (Dancing Shiva) is a famous example of Chola bronze sculptures. Other bronze sculptors - Sembiyan Mahadevi. Deities such as Shiva, Vishnu and Parvati.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Anuradhapura, the old capital of Sri Lanka, was destroyed by which of the following dynasties of South India in the 10th century AD? [SSC MTS 13/11/2024]
solution:
(C) Chola
King Pandukabhaya (474 - 367 BC) established his capital at Anuradhapura. It was destroyed by Rajendra Chola I. The last king of Anuradhapura was Mahinda V (982 - 1017 CE).

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Which of the following statements is/are true about the Cholas? [SSC MTS 13/11/2024]
1. The capital of the Chola kingdom was Tanjore.
2. Vikram Chola had in his veins the blood of the Cholas and Eastern Chalukyas.
3. Rajendra I Chola is known as Gangaikonda Chola.
solution: (C) All 1, 2 and 3

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following Rashtrakuta rulers performed the ceremony called 'Hiranya Garbha'? [SSC MTS 13/11/2024]
solution: (A) Dantidurga

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
With reference to medieval history, consider the following statements. [SSC MTS 12/11/2024]
1. The founder of the Chola dynasty called the 'Imperial Cholas' was Vijayalaya (9th century Ad.
2. One of the important dynasties which ruled in northern India was Gurjara-Pratiharas.
3. Palas ruled in western India and Rashtrakutas ruled in southern India in the early years (750 - 1000).
solution:
(A) 1 and 2 only
Palas ruled in the Eastern part of India in the region of Bengal and Bihar.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Krishna III of Rashtrakuta dynasty established a victory pillar and a temple at which of the following cities of south India after defeating the Chola ruler Parantaka I? [SSC CPO 28/06/2024]
solution:
(A) Rameswaram
The Rashtrakuta king Krishna III defeated Chola king Parantaka I in the battle of Takkolam (948-49 AD) and took control of Tondaimandalam.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Kulottunga I was a king of which of the following dynasties? [RRB RPF SI 03/12/2024]
solution:
(B) Chola
Kulottunga I, born Rajendra Chalukya was a Chola emperor reigned from 1070 - 1122 AD. He was the prince of Eastern Chalukya dynasty and his mother was the daughter of Chola king Rajendra I. He renamed Vishkhapatnam as 'Kulottunga Chola Pattana'. He succeeded his cousin Athirajendra Chola. He was the first ruler of Chola-Chalukya line.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The kings of which among the following dynasties defeated the kings of Malaya, Java and Sumatra? [RRB RPF SI 09/12/2024]
solution:
(A) Chola
In 1025 CE, Rajendra I conquered the kingdom of Srivijaya (Sailendra) in Southeast Asia which included Malay, Java, Sumatra. He defeated Sailendra ruler Vijayotungavarman and conquered Kadaram and Srivijaya.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who among the following rulers invaded Malaya to liberate their trade with China? [SSC CHSL 07/08/2023]
solution: (B) Chola

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Sabha and Ur, two kinds of village assemblies are referred to in which of the following dynasties? [SSC CGL 20/07/2023]
solution:
(A) Chola
Ur - assembly for common people, Sabha - assembly for learned Brahmins.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Siddheshwar Mahadev Temple in Bengal depicts the architecture of which dynasty.? [SSC CPO 03/10/2023]
solution:
(A) Pala Dynasty
The Siddheshwar Mahadeva Temple in Barakar, Burdwan district of Bengal exhibits early Pala style.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Rajaraja I, considered the most powerful Chola ruler, became king in ________. [SSC CHSL 15/03/2023]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following towns was built by the ruler Vijayalaya in Kaveri delta? [SSC CGL 01/12/2022]
solution:
(D) Thanjavur
[ref Q-18]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Sultan Mahmud of Ghazni was a contemporary of which Chola king? [SSC CGL 02/12/2022]
solution: (D) Rajendra I

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who among the following belonged to the ancient chiefly family of the Cholas from Uraiyur, captured the Kaveri delta from the Muttaraiyar in the middle of the ninth century? [SSC CGL 06/12/2022]
solution:
(B) Vijayalaya
[ref Q-18]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following towns was grown as a temple town during the Chola dynasty? [SSC CGL 08/12/2022]
solution:
(A) Bhillasvamin
Bhillasvamin is located in Madhya Pradesh was a temple town alongside others like Somnath, Kanchipuram, Madurai and Tirupati.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following territories to the south and north were made part of the Chola kingdom by the successors of the ruler Vijayalaya? [SSC CGL 09/12/2022]
solution:
(B) Pandyan and Pallava
The successors of Vijayalaya like Aditya I and Parantaka I, expanded the Chola empire by conquering Pandyan and Pallava territories in south and north.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which dynasty bronze statues were made using the "lost wax" technique? [SSC CGL 12/12/2022]
solution:
(A) Chola
Chola dynasty used the lost-wax technique also known as "cire perdue" or "Madhu Uchchishtta Vidhana" in Sanskrit. It is a technique creating a wax model, coating with clay, then melting out the wax creating a mold and then pouring molten metals like bronze to make statues.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Puhar or Kaveripattinam, was the port of which of the following dynasty? [SSC CGL 13/12/2022]
solution: (B) Cholas

subject: Ancient History
question_text: In the early eleventh century, the Chola king Rajendra I built a Shiva temple and filled it with a Sun-pedestal seized from the ________. [SSC CGL 03/12/2022]
solution:
(C) Chalukyas
A sun pedestal from the Chalukyas, a Ganesha statue and several statues of Durga, a Nandi statue from the eastern Chalukyas, an image of Bhairava (a form of Shiva), and Bhairavi from the Kalingas of Odisha, and a Kali statue from the Palas of Bengal.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who among the following was given the title of 'Uttarapatha Swamin'? [SSC MTS 15/07/2022]
solution:
(C) Dharmapala
He is known as 'Uttarapatha Swamin' means 'Lord of the northern path' because of his contributions to the Buddhism in northern parts.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: In dravidian style of temples the main temple tower is known as ________. [SSC JE 06/06/2024]
solution:
(C) Vimana
The Dravida temple is enclosed within a compound wall. Gopuram is the entrance gateway at the front wall. Mandapa is a pillared hall for the public rituals. Garbhagriha or a sanctum is a place where main Deity is placed. Shikhara is used in Nagara style temple it is the tower that rose above the sanctorum or garbhagriha.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The entrance gateway in Dravidian style temple is known as: [SSC STENOGRAPHER 11/12/2024]
solution: (A) Gopuram

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The architectural marvels, temples of Gangaikonda-Cholapuram, were built by which of the following king? [SSC MTS 07/10/2024]
solution:
(A) King Rajendra
[ref Q-21]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The magnificent Kailasa temple at Ellora was built during the reign of which Rashtrakuta king? [SSC CGL 12/09/2024]
solution:
(A) Krishna I
The Kailasa Temple at Ellora is located in Maharashtra. It is the largest of the rock-cut Hindu temples there. It was constructed by Rashtrakuta king Krishna I (756 - 773 AD). It is built in Dravida style architecture. This temple is a monolithic structure.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The elliptical shapes in temple architecture in Dravidian style are known by which of the given terms? [SSC MTS 05/11/2024]
solution:
(A) Gajaprishta
Shapes of Dravidian temples: Square-kuta, it refers to temple with square base. Rectangular-shala, temple with rectangular base. Elliptical-gajaprishta, it means 'back of an elephant', the shape resembling the curve of an elephant's back.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Valabhi type of Nagara temples are generally ________ in shape, with a roof that rises into a vaulted chamber. [SSC MTS 07/10/2024]
solution:
(A) Rectangular
Vallabhi style is a sub-type of Nagara temple. Temples have rectangular base and roof that rises to the vaulted chambers. These buildings are also called "wagon-vaulted" buildings. Teli Ka Mandir 9th century temple in Gwalior is an example of this style.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The Airavatesvara Temple in Tamil Nadu was built by ________. [SSC MTS 14/06/2023]
solution:
(A) Rajaraja Chola II
The Airavatesvara temple is located in Kumbakonam, Thanjavur in Tamil Nadu. It is a Dravida style Hindu temple. It was built by Rajaraja II in 12th century. It is dedicated to Lord Shiva.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Brihadiswara Temple of Tanjore was built by which of the following Chola emperors? [SSC CHSL 04/07/2024]
solution:
(B) Raja Raja Chola
The Brihadeshwara temple was built between 1003 to 1010 AD by Rajaraja Chola. Dedicated to Lord Shiva. Also known as Peruvudaiyar Kovil. The Brihadeshwara temple, the Gangaikondacholapuram temple & the Airavateshwara temple are a part of "Great Living Chola Temples" UNESCO site.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: In which state is the famous Brihadeshwar temple situated? [SSC MTS 01/09/2023]
solution: (C) Tamil Nadu

subject: Ancient History
question_text: The 'King of Dance' is a description of [CAPF 2022]
solution:
(D) Bronze Nataraja of Cholas
Nataraja statue of Shiva - it shows Shiva dancing (Tandava). Shiva has four arms, surrounded with a ring of fire and symbolic objects like Damaru and Agni, representing creation and destruction. Shiva is balancing himself on right leg and raising left leg. In Upper right hand - he is holding Damaru. Upper left hand - Holds Agni. Lower right hand - showing Abhaya Mudra (fearlessness). Lower left hand - pointing towards raised foot.

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Consider the following statements with reference to the Nataraj bronze sculpture of Chola period: [CDS II 2023]
1. Shiva is shown balancing himself on his right leg representing tirobhava.
2. Shiva is shown raising his left leg in bhujangatrasita stance, representing kicking away the illusion from the devotee's mins.
3. The main right hand holds the damaru, Shiva's favourite musical instrument.
solution:
(D) 1, 2 and 3
The raised right leg is placed on a dwarf representing ignorance signifies "tirobhava" or veiling reality. The raised left leg in "bhujangatrasita" stance represents removal of illusion and granting grace. Shiva is holding Damaru in the right hand signifies the primal sound of creation, the rhythm of universe, sound that initiates the cosmic dance.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which of the following dynasties of South India issued their documents first in Prakrit and later in Sanskrit? [CDS II 2023]

options.b: The Pandyas of the post-Sangam Age

solution:
(C) The Pallavas of Tondaimandalam
Pallava dynasty (575-897 AD): founder - Simha Vishnu. Ruled in the region of Tondaimandalam. Initially used Prakrit language but later transitioned to Sanskrit.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who among the following Chola kings encouraged the Sailendra ruler of Sri Vijaya to build a Buddhist Vihara in Negapatnam? [CAPF 2022]
solution:
(B) Rajaraja I
Chola king Rajaraja I, encouraged the Sailendra ruler of Sri Vijaya, Sri Mara Vijayottungavarman to build a Buddhist Vihara at Nagapattinam. This Vihara was called "Chudamani Vihara" after the father of Sri Mara.

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Consider the following statements about the Ancient Hindu metal images in India: [CDS II 2024]
1. The Chola period is well known for the aesthetic and technical finesse of its metal sculpture.
2. The iconography and style of Chola metal images were similar to those of their stone counterparts.
solution: (C) Both 1 and 2

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Consider the following dynasties: [IAS 2023]
1. Hoysala
2. Gahadavala
3. Kakatiya
4. Yadava
How many of the above dynasties established their kingdoms in early eighth century AD?
solution:
(D) None
Hoysala dynasty (between 10th and 14th century AD) - ruled in Karnataka. Founded by Nripa Kama II. Capital was initially Belur and later Halebidu. Known for their temple architecture (star shaped). Chennakeshava Temple at Belur, Hoysaleswara temple at Halebidu and Keshava temple at Somnathapura. Gahadvala dynasty - Founded by Chandradev between 1080-85 AD in Kannauj. Raja Jayachand (1170-94 AD) belongs to this dynasty. Kakatiya dynasty - ruled around 12th to 14th century AD. Capital - Warangal. It was a Telugu dynasty that ruled in Deccan region. Founder - Betaraju. Rani Rudramadevi belongs to this dynasty. Yadava dynasty - also known as Seuna or Yadavas of Devagiri. Ruled region of Daulatabad (Maharashtra) during 12th to 14th century AD. Founder - Bhillama V (1185-93 AD) he moved capital from Sinnar to Devagiri. Other rulers - Singhana, Ramachandra.

subject: Ancient History
question_text:
Consider the following pairs: [IAS 2022]
King - Dynasty
1. Nannuka - Chandela
2. Jayashakti - Paramara
3. Nagabhata II - Gurjara-Pratihara
4. Bhoja - Rashtrakuta
How many pairs given above are correctly matched?
solution:
(B) Only 2 pairs
Nannuka was the founder of Chandela dynasty. Jayashakti was the ruler of Chandela dynasty. Nagabhatta II and Mihir Bhoja both belongs to Gurjara-Pratihara dynasty. [ref Q-4 level 2 Post Gupta]

subject: Ancient History
question_text: At which one of the following places was a Shiva temple not constructed under the patronage of the Chola rulers? [NDA I 2021]
solution:
(D) Naneghat
Naneghat is located in the Western Ghats of Maharashtra. The Chola empire was not extended to this region.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Which one of the following has been an important early Chola painting discovered at the Brihadisvara Temple, Thanjavur in 1931? [CDS I 2024]

options.a: Buddha with Chauri-bearers on either side
options.d: Parshvanatha with snake-hood on a lion-throne

solution:
(B) Shiva as Yoga-Dakshinamurti
Depiction of God Shiva sitting in a Yoga pose, with four faces representing the different directions. It was discovered in 1931 by historian S.K. Govindaswamy.

subject: Ancient History
question_text: Who among the following Chola Kings conquered and annexed the Maldives? [CBI 2023]
solution:
(B) Rajaraja
Rajaraja Chola I expanded his kingdom by naval expeditions to Sri Lanka, Maldives, Lakshadweep, Thiladhunmadulu atoll and other parts of Indian ocean. He established Chola empire as a maritime power.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Which was the first dynasty of the Delhi Sultanate? [SSC CHSL 05/07/2024]
solution:
(A) Mamluk dynasty
Delhi Sultanate (1206 - 1526 AD): Slave / Mamluk dynasty (1206-90 AD), Khilji dynasty (1290 - 1320 AD), Tughlaq dynasty (1320 - 1414), Sayyid dynasty (1414-51 AD) and Lodi dynasty (1451 - 1526 AD).

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Who among the following was the first sultan of Delhi Sultanate to visit the Ajmer shrine of Khwaja Muinuddin Chishti? [SSC MTS 11/11/2024]
solution:
(D) Muhammad Bin Tughlaq
Muhammad Bin Tughlaq was the first Sultan who visited the Ajmer Shrine of Khwaja Moinuddin Chishti in 1332. Other rulers who visited the Shrine - Akbar, Jahangir, Shah Jahan, Dara Shukoh, Aurangzeb.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Which of the following dynasties' genealogy was found in the Bijolia inscription? [SSC CHSL 02/07/2024]
solution:
(A) Chahamana
Bijolia inscription (1170 AD), provides the genealogy of Chahamana (Chauhan) dynasty of Shakambhari (Ajmer). Founder of Chauhan dynasty - Vasudeva. Greatest Chauhan ruler - Prithviraj III.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: In which year did the Mongols under Genghis Khan invade Transoxiana in North-East Iran? [SSC MTS 07/07/2022]
solution:
(A) 1219
Genghis Khan was the founder of Mongol Empire in 1206. Ghenghis Khan's army invaded Transoxiana in 1219 overwhelming the army of Khwarazm Shah. Iltutmish prevented Ghenghis Khan's attack on his empire.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: In which of the following years did Alexander invade Punjab and in which of the following years did he die? [SSC MTS 23/10/2024]
solution:
(B) 326 BC and 323 BC
Alexander invaded Punjab region in 326 BC, fought the Battles of Hydaspes against King Porus on the banks of Jhelum (Hydaspes). Alexander's troops decided to retreat at the banks of Hyphasis river (Beas). Alexander died in Babylon in 323 BC.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Who among the following was the daughter of Iltumish, a ruler of Slave Mamluk dynasty? [SSC MTS 15/06/2023]
solution:
(C) Razia Sultan
Razia was the first and only Muslim female ruler of India. She was daughter of Iltutmish and Turkan Khatun (daughter of Qutubuddin Aibak). She disregarded purdah and promoted Jalaluddin Yaqut, an Abyssinian slave to an important position.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Who among the following was the first Sultan of Delhi to style himself as 'Zil-i-Ilahi'? [SSC CHSL 05/07/2024]
solution:
(D) Balban
Ghiyasuddin Balban took the title of Zil-i-Ilahi (shadow of Allah) and declared himself a descendant of Afrasiyab, famous Iranian emperor in Persian poems. He called himself Niyabat-i-Khudai (deputy of God).

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Which of the following was the last invasion by Mahmud Ghazni outside Punjab? [SSC CHSL 14/08/2023]
solution:
(A) Somnath
Mahmud Ghazni's last major attack was at Somnath Temple (Gujarat) in 1025 AD, it was his 16th attack in India. Ghazni attacked Multan (1006), Mathura (1018 AD), Kannauj (1021), Kalinjar (1023 AD).

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Between who among the following did the Battle of Chandawar take place? [SSC CHSL 08/08/2023 + SSC JE 07/06/2024]
solution:
(B) Jaychand and Muhammad Ghori
Battle of Chandawar - fought between Jayachand of Gahadwala dynasty and Muhammad Ghori in 1194 AD. On the banks of Yamuna river. Jayachanda was killed by Ghori.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Who was the last sultan of the Lodi Dynasty? [SSC CPO 11/11/2022]
solution:
(C) Ibrahim Lodi
Ibrahim Lodi (1517-26 AD): son of Sikandar Lodi. 1st Battle of Panipat(1526) - between Ibrahim Lodi and Babur, Ibrahim Lodi was killed in this battle. Daulat Khan Lodi, governor of Punjab invited Babur to overthrow Ibrahim Lodi.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: The famous Arabic scholar Al Biruni came into contact with India through ________ in the eleventh century A.D. [SSC CGL 21/07/2023]
solution:
(B) Mahmud Ghaznavi
Writers during Ghaznavi's period - Firdausi (wrote - Shahnama). Al-Beruni, who wrote 'Tahqiq-i-Hind (kitab-ul-Hind).

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Who among the following was the best-known Chahamana ruler? [SSC CGL 05/12/2022]
solution:
(A) Prithviraja III
Prithviraj Chauhan (Prithviraja III) born in 1166 AD, he belongs to Chahamana (Chauhan) dynasty. He ruled from 1177 to 1192. Prithviraj III married to Sanyogita, daughter of Gahadwal ruler of Kannauj, Jaichand. Prithviraj Raso was composed by Chand Bardai. Pritviraj Vijaya by Jayanak.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Mameluke style of architecture belongs to which dynasty? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 13/10/2023]
solution:
(B) Slave dynasty
Slave dynasty: also known as Mamluk dynasty. Started by Qutubuddin Aibak, a slave of Ghori. Other slaves - Yalduz, Bakhtiyar Khilji and Qubacha. Rulers belonged to Ilbari tribe of Turks.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: The Delhi sultan, who succumbed to injuries as a result of his fall from horse, was ________. [SSC CPO 05/10/2023]
solution:
(A) Qutubuddin Aibak
Qutubuddin Aibak (1206-10) - He was the slave of Muhammad Ghori. Founder of Delhi Sultanate. Capital - Lahore. Title "Laksh Baksh" (giver of lakhs). He constructed Quwwat-ul-Islam at Delhi and Adhai din ka Jhopra at Ajmer. He died in 1210 while playing Chaugan or Polo.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: The Tomb of Iltutmish was built in which year? [SSC CPO 05/10/2023]
solution:
(C) 1235 AD
Iltutmish (1210 - 1236): he was son-in-law of Qutubuddin Aibak. Iltutmish killed Aram Shah (son of Aibak). He made Delhi the capital in place of Lahore. Nizam-ul-Mulk was his Wazir (PM), who looked after financial department. Organised 'Iqta' system. Set up 'Chahalgani' group of 40 noble Turkish slaves.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Raziya Sultan was the first and only female ruler of the Sultanate, who ascended the throne in: [SSC CGL 24/09/2024]
solution:
(D) 1236 AD
Raziya Sultan (1236-40 AD): Daughter of Iltutmish. First and only Muslim female ruler of India. In 1240, she was assassinated by her brother Bahram Shah. She patronized Minhaj-i-Siraj, who wrote Tabakat-i-Nasiri.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Ghiyaseddin Balban introduced the customs of 'Sijda' and 'Paibos' as normal forms of salutations to the king as a part of ________ etiquette. [SSC CHSL 04/08/2023]
solution:
(D) Court
Sijdah (prostration) and Paibos (kissing the feet) were introduced as the part of court etiquette by Balban, to demonstrate respect and loyalty to the monarch.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Who started the practice of celebrating Nauroz during the Delhi Sultanate period? [SSC CHSL 07/08/2023]
solution:
(C) Ghiyasuddin Balban
Ghiyasuddin Balban (1266 - 87 AD): original name - Baha-u-Din, also known as 'Ulugh Khan'. Title: 'Zil-i-Ilahi'. Adapted the policy of consolidation rather than expansion. Started blood and iron policy. Established military department Diwan-e-Arz. He started Nauroz (Parsi festival).

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Balban served as naib to which of the following sultans of Delhi? [SSC CHSL 07/08/2023]
solution:
(C) Nasiruddin Mahmud
Nasiruddin Mahmud (1246-66) was placed on throne by Balban in 1246. Balban served as Naib of Nasiruddin Mahmud. Balban broke the power of Chahalgani or Chalisa.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Iltutmish introduced a copper coin called ________ in place of the silver coin. [SSC CGL 18/07/2023]
solution:
(C) Jittal
Iltutmish introduced Silver coin (Tanka) and Copper coin (Jittal). Iltutmish transferred capital from Lahore to Delhi.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Who among the following revolted against Balban, Sultan of Delhi, and declared himself as an independent ruler of Bengal in 1279? [SSC CGL 27/07/2023]
solution:
(B) Tughril Beg
Tughril Beg was governor of Bengal who revolted against Balban and declared himself independent ruler in 1279. Balban sent his forces and Tughril Beg was murdered by his army.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Who occupied Badaun (A.D. 1197-98) and appointed Iltutmish as its first Muslim Governor? [SSC MTS 12/05/2023]
solution:
(A) Qutubuddin Aibak
Qutubuddin Aibak occupied Badaun in 1197-98 and appointed Iltutmish as its first Muslim governor. Iltutmish is also known as 'Slave of Slaves'.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Qutub Minar is an example of ________ architecture. [SSC CHSL 21/03/2023]
solution:
(A) Indo-Islamic
Qutub Minar is a part of Qutub complex in Delhi. It is a UNESCO (1993) site. It is 72.5 m high (5 storey) and is the tallest bricks minaret in the world. Qutubuddin Aibak built the first storey, Iltutmish added three more storeys. Firoz Shah Tughlaq repaired Qutub Minar after a lightning strike and constructed the fifth storey.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Who had assumed the title of Balban? [SSC CGL TIER-II 07/03/2023]
solution:
(C) Ulugh Khan
[ref Q-18]

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Minhaj-I-Siraj was a chronicler during the rule of ________. [SSC CGL 08/12/2022]
solution:
(A) Sultan Iltutmish
Minhaz-i-Siraj was the author of 'Tabakat-i-Nasiri' in Persian. He served in the court of Nasiruddin Qubacha, Iltutmish and Razia Sultan. He recognised that Raziya was more able ruler than her brothers.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Quwwat al-Islam mosque and minaret, built during the last decade of the ________ century. [SSC CGL 08/12/2022]
solution:
(D) twelfth
Quwwat-ul-Islam Mosque and minaret were built around 1192-98 AD, by Qutubuddin Aibak in Delhi. He also built Adhai Din Ka Jhopra in Ajmer.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: What is the approximate height (in metres) of the Qutub Minar situated in Delhi? [SSC CPO 11/11/2022]
solution:
(C) 73
Qutubuddin Aibak started construction of Qutub Minar in honour of Sufi Saint Qutubuddin Bakhtiyar Kaki. Made of red and buff sandstone. It is a 5 storey building and 72.5 m high.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Who among the following was the Wazir of Sultan Iltutmish? [SSC CGL 05/08/2022]
solution:
(C) Nizam-ul-Mulk Junaidi
Nizam-ul-Mulk Junaidi held the position of Wazir (Prime minister) during Iltutmish's reign. Junaidi opposed Razia and supported rebellion against her. Razia Sultan appointed Khwaja Muhazabuddin as her new Wazir. Jalaluddin Yakut held important position in the court of Razia.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Which of the following dynasties of the Delhi Sultanate finally annexed Jaunpur into the empire of Delhi Sultanate? [SSC MTS 22/10/2024]
solution:
(D) Lodi dynasty
Jaunpur was annexed by Bahlol Lodi in 1479. Barbak Shah (son of Bahlol Lodi) was made the Viceroy of Jaunpur.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Which of the following is the correct chronological order of early Turkish rulers who ruled over Delhi from the year 1206 to 1290? [SSC MTS 14/07/2022]
solution:
(A) Qutubuddin Aybak, Shamsuddin Iltutmish, Raziya
Slave Dynasty (1206-90 AD): Qutubuddin Aibak (1206-10), Shamsuddin Iltutmish (1211-36), Raziya Sultan (1236-40). Bahram Shah (1240-42), Masud Shah (1242-46), Nasiruddin Mahmud (1246-66), Ghiyasuddin Balban (1266-87) and Kaiqubad (1287-90).

subject: Medieval History
question_text: In which of the following forts was Razia Sultan imprisoned by Malik Ikhtiyar-ud-din Altunia? [SSC CGL 19/04/2022]
solution:
(A) Qila Mubarak in Bathinda
Malik Ikhtiyar-ud-din Altunia, governor of Bathinda refused to accept suzerainty of Razia. Razia accompanied by Yakut (Ethiopian slave) marched against Altunia. Altunia murdered Yakut and imprisoned Razia in Qila Mubarak, Bathinda. Later, Altunia married Razia.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Which of the following departments of Delhi Sultanate was reorganized by Balban? [SSC CGL 10/09/2024]
solution:
(C) Diwan-i-Arz
Diwan-i-Mustakhraj (department of arrears) established by Alauddin Khilji and Diwan-i-Risalat (department of religious affairs). Diwan-i-Ishtiaq (department of pensions) established by Firoz Shah Tughlaq. Diwan-i-Arz (military department) established by Balban.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Chand Bardai's famous literary work ________, mentions Prithviraj Chauhan's love story and heroic deeds. [SSC CGL 12/09/2024]
solution:
(B) Prithviraj Raso
Chand Bardai was the court poet of Prithviraj Chauhan (Prithviraj III). He composed Prithviraj Raso. Bijolia inscription provides early insights of Chauhan dynasty. Rajamartanda was composed by Bhojaraja of Parmar dynasty. Abhidhana Chintamani is a Jain text composed by Hemachandra. Harikeli Nataka was composed by Chahamana king Vigraharaja IV also known as Visaladeva.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Which of the following Sultans of Delhi primarily adopted a policy of consolidation rather than expansion? [SSC CGL 23/09/2024]
solution:
(D) Balban
Balban adopted policy of consolidation rather than expansion. Strengthening the Sultanate internally and suppressing rebellions, rather than expanding its territory.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Ghiyas ud-din Balban had set up a ________ department called 'Diwan-i-arz'. [SSC CGL 25/09/2024]
solution:
(B) military
Diwan-l-Arz was a military department established by Balban. Diwan-l-Kohl was agriculture department established by Mohammad bin Tughlaq. Diwan-I-Riyasat was commerce department established by Alauddin Khilji. Diwan-I-Wizarat was a finance department.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Chand Minar at Daulatabad is a 210-feet-high tapering tower divided into ________ storeys. [SSC MTS 30/10/2024]
solution:
(A) four
Chand Minar was built by Ala-ud-din Bahmani in 1445 AD, Based on Indo-Islamic architecture. It has 4 storeys and 24 chambers.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: The author of Tarikh-I-Mubarakh Shahi, Yahya Sirhindi claims that the founder of ________ dynasty of Delhi sultanate was a descendant of Prophet Muhammad. [SSC CPO 29/06/2024]
solution:
(D) Sayyid
Sayyid dynasty (1414-51 AD): founder - Khizr Khan; he was the governor of Multan under Tughlaq dynasty. Yahya Sirhindi wrote Tarikh-I-Mubarashahi in Persian language; he mentions that Khizr Khan was a descendant of Prophet Muhammad. Rulers of Sayyid dynasty - Khizr Khan (1414-21), Mubarak Shah (1421-34), Muhammad Shah (1434-43), Alam Shah (1443-51).

subject: Medieval History
question_text: The ________ sultanate is ruled by the sharqi dynasty. [SSC CGL 18/08/2021]
solution:
(C) Jaunpur
Jaunpur Sultanate ruled by Sharqi dynasty (1394-1479 AD) founded by Malik Sarwar.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Sultan Ghari tomb is situated to west of the Qutub in Delhi is like a fortress within a walled enclosure with bastions on the comers, with its ________ grave chamber underground. [SSC MTS 28/10/2024]
solution:
(B) octagonal
Sultan Ghari tomb, built like a small fort. It was built by Sultan Iltutmish in the memory of his son Nasiruddin Mahmood who was the governor of Bengal (Lakhnauti) and was killed in 1229.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Bahlol Lodi was succeeded by his son ________ under the title of Sultan Sikandar Shah in 1489. [SSC CGL 03/08/2022]
solution:
(A) Nizam Khan
Sikandar Lodi or Nizam Khan (1489-1517 AD) - shifted capital from Delhi to Agra (founded by him). Introduced Gaz-i-Sikandari, a measure land. Composed poems in Persia with pen name 'Gulrukhi'. Built Moth ki masjid.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: "Double Dome" feature of Indo-Islamic architecture was introduced by which dynasty of Delhi Sultanate? [SSC MTS 20/06/2023]
solution:
(D) Lodi Dynasty
Lodi dynasty (1451-1526 AD): Bahlol Lodi (1451-88), Sikandar Lodi (1489-1517 AD), Ibrahim Lodi (1517-26). Double dome architecture - The tomb of Sikandar Lodi (1517), Tomb of Humayun (1572), Taj Mahal (1648), Jama Masjid (1656).

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Which of the following mosques/masjids was built during the reign of Lodi dynasty of the Delhi Sultanate? [SSC CPO 03/10/2023]
solution:
(D) Moth ki Masjid, Delhi
Moth ki Masjid in Delhi was built during the reign of Sikandar Lodi. Quwwat ul-Islam Mosque was built by Qutubuddin Aibak. Begumpuri Mosque was built by Mohammad bin Tughlaq. Lal Masjid of Delhi was built in 1728 by Kaniz-i-Fatima (Fakr-i-Jahan) in memory of her husband, Shujaat Khan, a noble in the court of Aurangzeb.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Which chronicler of the medieval age recognised that Raziya was more able and qualified than all her brothers? [SSC MTS 23/10/2024]
solution:
(B) Minhaj-i-Siraj
[ref Q-25]

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Balban's rule as an absolute despot led to which of the following events? [SSC MTS 30/09/2024]
solution:
(A) Destruction of the Forty
Iltutmish set up the official nobility of slaves known as 'Chahalgani' or Chalisa (group of 40). Balban broke the power of Chalisa and restored the prestige of crown.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Who was the first ruler of the Sayyid Dynasty? [SSC MTS 11/05/2023]
solution:
(A) Khizr Khan
Khizr Khan (1414-21 AD): founder of Sayyid dynasty. Adopted the title of 'Rayat-i-Ala'. After invading Delhi in 1398, Timur gave governorship of Multan, Lahore and Dipalpur to Khizr Khan.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Balban followed the tradition of 'Sijda and Pabos' from which of following traditions? [SSC CHSL 03/07/2024]
solution:
(B) Persian
Balban followed the traditions of Sijda (bowing down) and Paibos (kissing the feet) from Persian traditions. He also started Persian new year festival 'Nauroz'.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Who among the following Delhi Sultanate rulers took the policy of 'blood and iron' to deal with the Mewati community? [SSC CHSL 04/07/2024]
solution:
(A) Balban
Blood and Iron is a ruthless approach to maintaining order and suppressing rebellions, use of swords for shedding blood. Balban used this policy to suppress rebellions in Mewat, Ganga-Yamuna doab region and Awadh. Alauddin Khilji also adopted the policy of 'Blood and Iron'.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: In the 11th century, under Mahmud of Ghazni, a small town in ________ became the capital of the vast empire of the Ghaznavids. [SSC CHSL 08/07/2024]
solution:
(A) Afghanistan
The empire of Ghaznavids was the first Muslim dynasty of Afghanistan in 11th century. Conflicts between Hindushahi rulers and Ghazni, Battle of Peshawar - between Ghazni and Jaipala. Battle of Waihind - between Ghazni and Anandpala.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Muhammad Ghori attacked Tabarhinda (Bhatinda) in 1191, a strategic point for ________. [SSC CGL 23/08/2021]
solution:
(C) Prithviraj Chauhan
Muhammad Ghori attacked Tabarhinda (Bathinda) a strategic point of Prithviraj Chauhan; it led to First Battle of Tarain (1191) between Prithviraj Chauhan and Ghori; won by Chauhans. Second Battle of Tarain (1192), won by Ghori.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: ________ attacked the southern coast of Kathiawar along the Arabian Sea, where he sacked the city of Somnath and its renowned Hindu temple in 1026. [SSC CHSL 08/07/2019]
solution:
(B) Mahmud of Ghazni
Mahmud of Ghazni (998 - 1030) attacked India 17 times. 16th attack - he invaded Somnath temple (1025 AD) and 17th attack - Ghazni attacked Jats to take revenge in 1027.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Queen Naikidevi is remembered as the woman who defeated ________ in 1178 CE. [SSC CPO 12/03/2019]
solution:
(D) Muhammad Ghori
Muhammad Ghori (1175-1206 AD): also known as Muizuddin Muhammad. Was the member of Ghurid dynasty. He appointed Qutubuddin Aibak his commander. He attacked and conquered Multan in 1175. In 1178, Bhima II (Solanki ruler) and queen Naikidevi of Gujarat defeated Ghori in battle of Kayadara.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: In the context of state and administration in the Delhi Sultanate, the term "muqti" means: [SSC CPO 11/12/2019]

options.b: The holder of a land assignment called "iqta".
options.c: The head of the village community.

solution:
(B) The holder of a land assignment called "iqta".
Important officers of Delhi Sultanate - Wazir: Prime Minister (finance department). Amil: collects revenue. Amir: governor of Pargana. Naib: incharge of all departments (except finance). Muqti/Wali/Iqtedar: holder of Iqta. Muqaddam/Khut: head of Gram. Qazi: chief judicial officer.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Who among the following sultans of the Delhi Sultanate appointed Ghiyasuddin Balban as his/her Prime Minister? [SSC CHSL 13/04/2021]
solution:
(B) Nasiruddin Mahmud
Nasiruddin Mahmud (1246-66 AD) was grandson of Iltutmish. Known as Darvesi King as he was noble and pious. He made Balban his Wazir and Naib.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: The period between ________ in India's history known as the Delhi Sultanate Period? [SSC CGL 04/06/2019]
solution:
(A) 1206 AD and 1526 AD
[ref Q-1]

subject: Medieval History
question_text: During which years did Khalji dynasty ruled over Delhi? [SSC JE 05/06/2024]
solution:
(D) 1290 - 1320
Khilji dynasty (1290-1320 AD): Founder - Jalaluddin Khilji (1290-96). Alauddin Khilji (1296-1316 AD) was the most famous ruler. Mubarak Khan (1316-20) and Khusrau Khan (1320).

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Who among the following Sultans framed a series of regulations to weaken the power of nobles? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 13/10/2023]
solution:
(C) Alauddin Khilji
Alauddin Khilji issued 4 ordinances to weaken the power of nobles. 1st ordinance aimed at the confiscation of free grants of land and religious endowments. In 2nd ordinance he set up a strong spy network. In 3rd ordinance he prohibited the use of wine. In the 4th ordinance Alauddin banned parties, social gatherings and marriage alliances without his permission.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Who among the following classical poets of the Sultanate period in India popularized Qawwali, a form of music which derived its name from the Arabic word 'qaul', meaning 'to speak'? [SSC CPO 05/10/2023]
solution:
(B) Amir Khusrau
Amir Khusrau (1253-1325 AD) - father of Qawwali (sufi devotional song). He was a court poet of Alauddin Khilji and became a disciple of Sufi saint Nizamuddin Auliya. Jalal al-Din Muhammad Rumi was a persian poet and scholar, he wrote 'Masnavi'. Nizam al-din Awliya or Nizamuddin Awliya (also known as Mahbub-i-Ilahi) was a sufi saint of Chisti order. Shams al-din Muhammad Hafiz was a persian poet known for his Diwan-i-Hafiz (collection of gazals and poems).

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Which of the following Sultans of Delhi was appointed as Amir-i-Tuzuk (Master of Ceremonies) before becoming the Sultan? [SSC CPO 05/10/2023]
solution:
(A) Alauddin Khalji
Alauddin Khilji (1296-1316 AD): nephew of Jalaluddin Khilji. He killed Jalaluddin Khilji and ascended the throne. Real name - Ali Gurshasp. He was Amir-i-Tuzuk (master of ceremonies). Alauddin Khilji separated religion from politics and proclaimed 'Kingship knows no Kinship'.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Who was the first to mention the incident of Jauhar in Persian? [SSC CHSL 11/08/2023]
solution:
(B) Amir Khusrau
Amir Khusrau mentioned about Jauhar incident, during siege of Chittor in 1303 by Alauddin Khilji in his work Khazain-ul-Futuh. Amir Khusrau gave the title of 'Sultan-e-Jahan' to Alauddin and Alauddin conferred the title of 'Tut-e-Hind' (Parrot of India) to Khusrau. Famous books of Khusrau are Nuh-Sipihr, Tughlaq-Nama, Khazain-ul-Futuh, Matla ul Anwar, Hasht Bihisht.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Who among the following was the slave-general Alauddin Khilji, who led his army in the battle against Ramachandra of Devagiri? [SSC CGL 17/07/2023]
solution:
(B) Malik Kafur
Malik Kafur was a eunuch slave. Alauddin bought him from Gujarat market for 1000 dinars; Kafur was known as 'Hazar Dinari'. In Deccan, Alauddin's army led by Malik Kafur, he defeated Ram Chandra (Yadav ruler of Devagiri, in 1308), Pratap Rudradeva (Kakatiya ruler of Warangal, in 1311), Vir Ballal III (Hoyasala ruler of Dwarsamudra, in 1309) and Vir Pandya (Pandya ruler of Madurai, in 1311).

subject: Medieval History
question_text: During Alauddin Khilji's reign, the cloth market was known as ________. [SSC CGL 24/07/2023]
solution:
(A) Sarai-i-Adl
Alauddin Khilji set up 3 markets: one for food grains, second for costly cloths and third for horses, slaves and cattle. Each market was under control of high officer 'Shahna'. Check on markets was kept by two officers - Diwan-i-Riyasat and Shahna-i-Mandi. All goods for sale were brought to an open market called Sara-i-Adal (cloth).

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Who was the first Sultan of the Delhi Sultanate to start military expeditions into southern India? [SSC MTS 27/06/2023]
solution:
(B) Alauddin Khalji
Deccan expedition of Alauddin Khilji was led by Malik Kafur. Alauddin took the title of 'Sikandar-i-Sani' (Alexander, the second) after Deccan victory.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Who among the following was the founder of the Khilji Dynasty? [SSC MTS 08/05/2023]
solution:
(A) Jalal-ud-din Firoz Khilji
He was also called as 'Clemency Jala-uddin' as he followed peace and wanted to rule without violence. Jalaluddin Khilji ruled from 1290 - 1296 AD. He was killed by his nephew Alauddin Khilji in 1296.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Which monument of Khilji dynasty in Delhi has the utilization of red sandstone? [SSC MTS 19/05/2023]
solution:
(D) Alai Darwaza
Alauddin Khilji built - Alai fort, Alai Darwaza, Hazar Sutun (Palace of thousand pillars), Hauz Khas (tank). He established the city of 'Siri', which was second of the seven cities of Delhi. 1st city was Qila Rai Pithora by Tomar dynasty.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Which of the following cities of Delhi was founded by Ghiyasuddin Tughlaq? [SSC CPO 29/06/2024]
solution:
(C) Tughlaqabad
Ghiyasuddin Tughlaq established Tughlaqabad in 1321, it was the third historic city of Delhi after Qila Rai Pithora (Tomar dynasty) and Siri (Alauddin Khilji). Firoz Shah Tughlaq established the fourth city of Delhi, Firozabad in 1354.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: How many types of taxes were in the reign of the ruler Alauddin Khalji? [SSC CGL 03/12/2022]
solution:
(A) 3
Alauddin imposed 3 types of taxes. 1) on cultivation called 'Kharaj' on 50% or produce. 2) on cattle called 'Charai'. 3) on house called 'Gharai'.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: The first southern campaign of Alauddin Khilji in 1307-08 AD was led to which of the following regions? [SSC CGL 09/09/2024]
solution:
(C) Devagiri
First southern campaign of Alauddin Khilji was against Ramchandra, Yadava ruler of Devagiri in 1307-08 AD, it was led by Malik Kafur. [ref Q-60]

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Which of the following Sultans of Delhi set up the officers like Barid (intelligence officer) and Munhiyans (secret spies) to control the market? [SSC PHASE XII 20/06/2024]
solution:
(A) Alauddin Khalji
Alauddin reorganised spy system and fixed the prices of necessary commodities. Barid-i-Mumalik was a department of Espionage and news gathering. Barids and Munhiyans were part of it.

subject: Medieval History
thought: Who among the following Sultans of the Khilji dynasty started giving cash salary to his soldiers and controlled the market during his reign? [SSC JE 06/06/2024]
solution:
(A) Alauddin Khilji
Alauddin Khilji introduced system of paying soldiers in cash instead of granting them land (iqta). He introduced Dagh (branding of horses) and Chehra/Huliya (descriptive roll of soldiers).

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Who was the first Sultan of the Sultanate period who arranged for the translation of Hindu scriptures from Sanskrit to Persian? [SSC JE 07/06/2024]
solution:
(C) Firoz Shah Tughlaq
Sanskrit books on medicine, science and arts were translated to Persian. Firoz Shah Tughlaq wrote his autobiography 'Futuhat-i-Firozshahi'.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: To control the prices, which of the following Sultans of Delhi set up three different markets for different commodities in Delhi? [SSC MTS 04/11/2024]
solution:
(C) Alauddin Khalji
[ref Q-61]

subject: Medieval History
question_text: To promote agriculture, Muhammad Tughlaq founded which of the following new ministries? [SSC CGL 26/09/2024]
solution:
(A) Diwan-i Amir-i kohl
Diwan-i-Kohi (agriculture department) was founded by Mohammad Bin Tughlaq, it was headed by an officer called Amir-i-Kohi.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Who was the famous Venetian traveller of the 13th century who visited Kerala? [SSC MTS 07/10/2024]
solution:
(B) Marco Polo
Marco Polo (1254-1323 AD) born in Venice, Italy; famous book - 'The Travels of Marco Polo'. He visited southern India during the Rudramma Devi of Kakatiyas and Pandyan ruler Maravarman. Nicolo Conti (Italy) visited Vijayanagara empire during Devaraya I around 1420. Abdur Razzaq (Persia) visited during Devaraya II. Barthema (Italy) was one of the first non-Muslim traveller to visit Mecca.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Who built an elegant two-storey 'ship palace' between two reservoirs named Jahaaz Mahal? [SSC MTS 29/10/2024]
solution:
(B) Sultan Ghiyaseddin Khilji
Jahaz Mahal is located between two lakes in a ship-like design in Madhya Pradesh. Sultan Ghiyasuddin Khilji, ruler of Malwa Sultanate in the 15th century.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Who among the following was the successor of Muhammad-Bin Tughlaq to the throne? [SSC CPO 04/10/2023]
solution:
(D) Firuz Shah Tughlaq
Firuz Shah Tughlaq (1351-88 AD): cousin of Mohammad Bin Tughlaq. During his reign the soldiers were not paid in cash but by assignments on land revenue of villages - Vajeha. Jizya become separate tax. He imposed 4 taxes mentioned in Quran. Kharaj - land tax; 1/10 of produce. Zakat - 2% tax on property. Jizya was levied on non-Muslims. Khams - 1/5 of booty captured during war. Firoz Tughlaq repaired no. of canals and imposed Haque-i-Sharb (irrigation tax) and Hasil-i-Sharb (water tax).

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Who invaded India during the rule of Tughlaq Dynasty? [SSC CHSL 10/07/2024]
solution:
(B) Timur
Timur, the lame, was a Mongol leader of Central Asia. He invaded India in 1398 during the reign of Muhammad Shah Tughlaq (last Tughlaq ruler).

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Who amongst the following raised the banner of revolt against Nasiruddin Khusrau? [SSC CHSL 09/08/2023]
solution:
(D) Ghiyasuddin Tughlaq
Nasiruddin Khusrau (last Khilji ruler) was killed by Ghazi Malik, governor of Dipalpur in 1320 AD. Ghazi Malik ascended the throne with title 'Ghiyasuddin Tughlaq' (1320-25) and established Tughlaq dynasty.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Who among the following appointed Aziz Khummar a wine distiller to high administrative post? [SSC CHSL 10/03/2023]
solution:
(B) Muhammad bin Tughlaq
Muhammad Bin Tughlaq (1325-51): son of Ghiyasuddin Tughlaq. Original name - 'Jauna Khan' (Ulugh Khan). He was nicknamed as the 'Wisest Fool'. He gave important positions to Aziz Khummar (a wine distiller), Firuz Hajja (a barber), Manka Tabbakh (a cook), Ladha and Pira (two gardeners).

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Ziyauddin Barani wrote his chronicle first in 1356 and another version ________ years later. [SSC CGL 12/12/2022]
solution:
(C) two
Ziyauddin Barani wrote the first version of his chronicle, Tarikh-i-Firoz Shahi, in 1356 and second version 2 years later. He also wrote Fatwa-i-Jahandari. He served in the court of Muhammad Bin Tughlaq and Firoz Shah Tughlaq.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Who among the following Moroccan travellers came to India during the reign of Muhammad bin Tughlaq? [SSC CPO 11/11/2022]
solution:
(D) Ibn Battuta
Ibn Battuta, from Morocco visited India during 1333-47 AD. He visited the reign of Mohammad Bin Tughlaq. Ibn Battuta wrote famous book Rihla or Safarnama. Abd-al-Razzaq Samarkandi, Persian scholar and ambassador of Timurid ruler Shah Rukh, visited India in mid-15th century. Suleiman al-Tajir (Arab) visited India in 9th century, he wrote 'Kitab al-Masalik wa al-Mamalik' (book of roads and kingdoms). Al-Masudi, also known as 'Herodotus of Arabs', he wrote about India in his work "Muruj al-Dhahab" (the Meadows of Gold).

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Which of the following is the correct chronological order of the rulers of the Tughlaq dynasty who ruled over Delhi from the year 1320 to 1414? [SSC MTS 06/07/2022]

options.a: Ghiyasuddin Tughluq, Firuz Shah Tughluq, Muhammad Tughluq
options.b: Firuz Shah Tughluq, Muhammad Tughluq, Ghiyasuddin Tughluq
options.c: Ghiyasuddin Tughluq, Muhammad Tughluq, Firuz Shah Tughluq
options.d: Muhammad Tughluq, Firuz Shah Tughluq, Ghiyasuddin Tughluq

solution:
(C) Ghiyasuddin Tughluq, Muhammad Tughluq, Firuz Shah Tughluq
Ghiyasuddin Tughlaq (1320-25 AD), Muhammad Tughlaq (1325-51 AD) and Firuz Shah Tughlaq (1351-88 AD).

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Which amongst the following Sultans shifted his capital from Delhi to Daulatabad? [SSC CHSL 08/07/2024]
solution:
(A) Muhammad Bin Tughlaq
Muhammad Bin Tughlaq: transferred the capital from Delhi to Daulatabad (Devagiri) in 1327. He proposed Khurasan expedition (1329) and Qarachil expedition (1330). Introduced Token currency (1329) of Bronze with high value. Increased taxation in Doab (1326).

subject: Medieval History
question_text: The Begumpuri mosque was built during the reign of which of the following rulers of the Delhi Sultanate? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 10/12/2024]
solution:
(A) Muhammad Tughlaq
He built the city of Jahapana. He set up Diwan-i-Kohi, agriculture department. Muhammad Bin Tughlaq had the largest kingdom of all. He gave Sondhar loan (cash in exchange of gold) and Taccavi loan.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Qutb Minar was originally four-storied. Who among the following Delhi Sultans raised the height to the structure by adding the fifth storey? [CAPF 2021]
solution:
(D) Firoz Shah Tughlaq
Firoz Shah Tughlaq Built - Fatehabad, Hisar, Firozabad, Jaunpur (Jauna Khan; name of Mohammad bin Tughlaq). He built 5th storey of Qutub Minar. He established Dar-ul-Shifa (hospital), Diwan-i-Khairat, to take care of orphans and widows and for the marriage of poor girls. Diwan-i-Bandagan (slaves) and Diwan-i-Ishtiaq (pensions). His PM was Khan-i-Jahan Maqbul. He made iqta system hereditary.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: At which of the following places was diamond mining carried out during the Delhi Sultanate period? [CDS I 2024]
solution:
(C) Panna
Panna (The city of diamonds) is located in Madhya Pradesh. Khambhat (Gujarat) - a trading centre for diamonds.

subject: Medieval History
question_text:
Consider the following statements: [IAS 2021]
1. It was during the reign of Iltutmish that Chengiz Khan reached the Indus in pursuit of the fugitive Khwarezm prince.
2. It was during the reign of Muhammad bin Tughluq that Taimur occupied Multan and crossed the Indus.
3. It was during the reign of Deva Raya II of Vijayanagara Empire that Vasco da Gama reached the coast of Kerala.
solution:
(A) 1 only
During the reign of Iltutmish, around 1221 AD, Chengiz Khan reached Indus river in pursuit of Jalal-ud-Din Mingburnu, the Khwarezmian prince, who fled after the Khwarezm Empire was conquered by the Mongols. Iltutmish refused to provide shelter to Mingburnu. Taimur invaded India in 1398, during the reign of Muhammad Shah Tughlaq, not Muhammad Bin Tughlaq (1325-51). Devaraya II of Vijayanagar Empire ruled from 1422-46 AD, Vasco de Gama arrived at Kerala, India in 1498.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: With reference to Indian history, who of the following were known as "Kulah-Daran"? [IAS 2022]
solution:
(D) Sayyids
The Sayyids put on a pointed cap (Kulah) and were known as Kulah Daran. The theologians were collectively known as Dastar-bandan or turban wearers.

subject: Medieval History
question_text:
With reference to Indian history, consider the following statements: [IAS 2022]
1. The first Mongol invasion of India happened during the reign of Jalal-ud-din Khalji.
2. During the reign of Ala-ud-din Khalji, one Mongol assault marched up to Delhi and besieged the city.
3. Muhammad-bin-Tughlaq temporarily lost portions of north-west of his kingdom to Mongols.
solution:
(B) 2 only
The first Mongol invasion happened during the reign of Iltutmish and not Jalaluddin Khilji. During the reign of Alauddin Khilji, when he was laying the siege to Chittor (1303), Mongols under Targhi's leadership attacked and besieged Delhi. In 1329, Tarmashirin Khan (Mongol) reached the outskirts of Delhi but was defeated by Muhammad Bin Tughlaq.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Who among the following has described the medieval Indian postal system as of two kinds-the horse-post called 'Uluq' and the foot-post called 'Dawa'? [NDA II 2022]
solution:
(C) Ibn Battuta
Ibn Battuta explains the Postal system: The horse post known as Uluq was posted every four miles. The foot post or Dawa has 3 stations at every mile.

subject: Medieval History
question_text:
Which of the following were features of the postal system of India as described by Ibn Battuta? [NDA I 2024]
1. The foot post carries a ringing bell in one hand.
2. The horse post is called uluq, stationed at a distance of every four miles.
3. The foot post or dawa has one station at every four kos of distance.
4. The foot post is quicker than horse post.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: The Vijayanagar kingdom was established by ________ in 1336. [SSC CPO 04/10/2023]
solution:
(C) Harihara and Bukka
Vijayanagar Empire (1336 - 1565 AD): Vijayanagar means 'City of Victory'. Founder Harihara I and Bukka Raya I of Sangama dynasty. It was ruled by 4 dynasties - Sangama dynasty (1336 - 1485 AD), Saluva dynasty (1485 - 1505), Tuluva dynasty (1505-70) and Aravidu dynasty (1570 - 1650).

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Hampi, the ancient capital of Vijaynagara is located in: [SSC CHSL 11/07/2019]
solution:
(C) Karnataka
Hampi, the capital of Vijayanagar Empire, located on the banks of river Tungabhadra in Karnataka. Hampi name is derived from goddess Pampadevi (Pampahampi). It is a UNESCO site (1986). It was destroyed in 1565 after the Battle of Talikota. The ruins of Hampi discovered by Colin Mackenzie in 1800 AD.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: What was the dominant source of income in the Vijaynagar Empire? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 12/10/2023]
solution:
(C) land revenue
Dominant source of income was land revenue, collected through a system called 'Athavane' or 'Athavana'.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Which of the following kings of Vijayanagara took the title of 'establisher of the Yavana kingdom'? [SSC CPO 28/06/2024]
solution:
(B) Krishna Deva Raya
Krishna Deva Raya (1509-29 AD): Saluva Timma, chief minister of Vira Narsimha, placed him on throne. Titles taken - Yavanaraja Sthapnacharya (restorer of Yavana Kingdom), Andhra Pitamaha, Andhra Bhoja, Abhinava Bhoja. His court was adorned by Ashtadiggajas. Achyuta Deva Raya ascended the throne after Krishna Deva Raya. He was visited by a French traveller Fernao Nuniz.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: The founders of Vijayanagar empire, Harihara and Bukka Raya were the ministers of: [SSC MTS 18/10/2024]
solution:
(D) Kampili
The Kampili kingdom was annexed by Mohammad Bin Tughlaq in 1327-28 AD, its rulers Kampilideva and his son Kumara Rama were killed. Harihara and Bukka were ministers in Kampili kingdom and later established Vijayanagar kingdom.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Who founded the Tuluva dynasty of the Vijayanagar Empire? [SSC CHSL 04/08/2023]
solution:
(D) Narsimha Deva Raya
Tuluva dynasty (1505-70 AD) was founded by Vir Narasimha. Last ruler - Sadasiva Raya (1542-70 AD). Krishna Deva Raya was the most prominent ruler of Tuluva dynasty.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Who among the following Vijaynagar rulers married his daughter to Firuz Shah Bahmani? [SSC CHSL 10/08/2023]
solution:
(B) Deva Raya I
Deva Raya I, Vijayanagar ruler married his daughter to Bahmani Sultan Taj ud-Din Firuz Shah (1397-1422). Firuz Shah Bahmani defeated Deva Raya I and got defeated in the subsequent battle.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: The founders of Vijayanagara Empire were the feudatories of which of the following dynasties? [SSC CHSL 17/07/2023]
solution:
(C) Kakatiya
Founder of Vijayanagar Empire Harihara and Bukka belonged to a family of 5 brothers who were feudatories of Kakatiyas of Warangal and later became ministers in Kampili kingdom. Kakatiya dynasty was founded by Kakati Rudradeva (Prataprudra I), who defeated Chalukya ruler Tailapa III in 1162 AD.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: The origin of 'Carnatic music' is related to which of the following Dynasties? [SSC CGL 19/07/2023]
solution:
(D) Vijayanagar
Purandara Dasa (1484-1564 AD) is considered as 'father of Carnatic music'. He is also known as 'Carnatic Sangeeta Pitamaha'.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Vijayanagara emperor Krishnadeva Raya founded a suburban township near Vijayanagara called Nagalapuram after his ________ [SSC CGL 14/07/2023]
solution:
(D) Mother
Krishnadeva Raya was the son of Tuluva Narasa Nayaka and his queen Nagamamba (Nagala devi). He defeated Ismail Adil Khan and restored Raichur doab (region between Krishna and Tungabhadra).

subject: Medieval History
question_text: The local communities of merchants were known as ________ in the Vijayanagara empire. [SSC CGL 20/07/2023]
solution:
(C) Kudirai Chettis
Kudirai Chettis (merchants) mainly involved in trade of horses. Rachavarus - cultivators who worked on the lands owned by the empire. Narapati - means 'lord of men' or king. It was used to refer kings of Vijayanagar Empire. Gajapati - it is used for the rulers of Gajapati dynasty of Odisha.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: The 'Pattanulkars' migrated from the Gujarat region to the Vijayanagar state. They Were ________. [SSC CGL 24/07/2023]
solution:
(C) silk weavers
The Vijayanagar state was a major centre of Silk trade and production. The Pattanulkars played an important role in this industry. They migrated from Gujarat to Vijayanagar state.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: After the breakup of the Bahmani kingdom, how many independent states arose? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 13/10/2023]
solution:
(D) 5
Bahmani Kingdom broke into 5 kingdoms: 1) Berar (1484): by Fataullah Imad Shah. Imad Shahi dynasty. 2) Bijapur (1489): by Yusuf Adil Shah. Adil Shahi dynasty. 3) Ahmadnagar (1490): by Malik Ahmad. Nizam Shai dynasty. 4) Golconda (1518): Quli Qutub Shah. Qutub Shahi dynasty. 5) Bidar (1526-27): Amir Ali Barid. Barid Shahi dynasty.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: While historians use the term Vijayanagara Empire and contemporaries of this empire described it as the ________. [SSC CGL 25/07/2023]
solution:
(B) Karnataka Samrajyamu
Gajapati Samrajya was a Hindu kingdom of Odisha, founded by Kapilendra Deva. Last ruler - Kakharua Deva. Vijayanagar Empire was known as 'Karnataka Samrajyamu'.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Raichur doab, the land between Krishna and Tungabhadra was a reason of conflict between the kings of Vijayanagar and ________. [SSC PHASE XI 28/06/2023]
solution:
(C) Bahmani
Bahmani Kingdom (1347 - 1527 AD) founded by Alauddin Hasan Bahman Shah, also known as Hasan Gangu. He selected his capital as Gulbarga and later gave it name of Ahsanabad.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Which battle led to the destruction of the Vijayanagara empire? [SSC MTS 03/05/2023]
solution:
(C) Battle of Talikota
Battle of Talikota (1565) also known as Battle of Rakshasi Tangdi. Fought between Deccan Sultanates (alliance of Bijapur, Ahmadnagar and Golconda) and Vijayanagar kingdom. Sidasiva Raya ruled the Vijayanagar Kingdom but the real power was exercised by Aliya Rama Raya. City of Vijayanagar was destroyed and Rama Raya was killed by Hussain Nizam Shah I.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Provida style of architecture belongs to which empire? [SSC MTS 13/06/2023]
solution:
(A) Vijayanagar Empire
Provida style of architecture is introduced by Vijayanagar rulers. It is a blend of Dravidian, Nagara, and Indo-Islamic influences, characterized with intricate carvings and sculptors.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: The Anantraj Sagar Tank was built by the ________ rulers. [SSC CGL 01/12/2022]
solution:
(A) Vijayanagara
The Anantraj Sagar Tank was built by Vijayanagara Princess Vardharajamma (also known as Ruchidevi), wife of Sri Krishna Deva Raya. It was built across the Maldevi river. Kamalapuram stepped tank in Hampi was built by Vijayanagar rulers.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Krishnadeva Raya composed a work on ________ in Telugu known as the Amuktamalyada. [SSC CGL 20/07/2023]
solution:
(A) statecraft
Krishnadevaraya's works - Amuktamalyada (in Telugu) on polity and statecraft. Jambavati Kalyanam (Sanskrit Drama).

subject: Medieval History
question_text: What did the rulers of Vijayanagara call themselves? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 11/12/2024]
solution:
(A) Raya
Rulers of Vijayanagar Empire called themselves 'Rayas' means 'Lords' or 'Kings'. The rulers also referred to as 'Narapati' (Lord of Men). Early Vijayanagar rulers called themselves as 'Hindu Suratranas'.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: The Bidar Fort of Karnataka is irregularly ________ in shape. [SSC MTS 01/10/2024]
solution:
(C) rhomboid
Bidar fort of Karnataka built by Sultan Ahmad Shah I of Bahmani dynasty in 1427. It contains 30 monuments. It was added to UNESCO in 2014.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: The Vijayanagara empire included people from different cultural regions. Which of the following regions was NOT a part of it? [PHASE XII 26/06/2024]
solution:
(D) Maratha
Vijayanagar Empire was based in the Southern part of India. It controlled regions like Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh, Tamil Nadu and parts of Kerala. Maratha (Maharashtra) region was not part of it.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: The village servants or functionaries, who constituted of groups of families in Vijayanagar empire, were called ________. [SSC MTS 11/11/2024]
solution:
(B) Ayagars
Nayakars - military chiefs. Vellalars - land owners. Ayagar system - Village committee (12 members).

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Who among the following Portuguese travellers visited the Vijayanagar empire during the reign of Krishnadevaraya? [SSC MTS 23/10/2024]
solution:
(D) Domingo Paes
Duarte Barbosa and Domingo Paes, Portuguese travellers, visited Vijayanagar during Krishnadevaraya. Nicolo de Conti (Italy) visited during Deva Raya I. Ludovico de Varthema was from Italy and Athanasius Nikitin was from Russia.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Which of the following was the last dynasty of the Vijayanagara Empire? [SSC CHSL 02/07/2024]
solution:
(C) Aravidu dynasty
Aravidu dynasty (1570 - 1650 AD) founded by Tirumala Raya, brother of Rama Raya who ruled in the name of Sadasiva Raya. Sriranga I and Venkata II were most prominent rulers. Sriranga III was the last ruler of this dynasty.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Who among the following was the founder of the Bahmani Sultanate, who took the title of Bahman Shah after his accession to the throne? [SSC CGL 26/07/2023]
solution:
(D) Alauddin Hasan
Alauddin Hasan Bahman Shah (1347-58): founder of Bahmani Kingdom. Capital - Gulbarga (first capital). He was also known as Hasan Gangu. Defeated Kakatiyas of Warangal.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: The title of 'Chief of the Merchants' or 'Malikut-Tujjar' was conferred on Mahmud Gawan by which of the following Bahmani kings? [SSC MTS 12/11/2024]
solution:
(A) Humayan Shah
Humayun Shah (1458-61) sultan of Bahmani Sultanate. Also known as Humayun Shah 'Zalim' for his cruelty. He appointed Mahmud Gawan as his Prime Minister. Mahmud Gawan (a trader): Peshwa during Bahmani Kingdom. Title 'Malik-ut-Tujjar' (chief of merchants) by Humayun Shah. Also received title of 'Khwaju-i-Jahan'. Gawan conquered Vijayanagar territories to Kanchi.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Under the Bahmani Empire, the ________ provinces were administered by. [SSC CPO 27/06/2024]
solution:
(B) Tarafdar
[ref Q-30]

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Who among the following Bahmani sultans transferred his capital from Gulbarga to Bidar? [SSC CPO 04/10/2023]
solution:
(A) Ahmad Shah
Ahmad Shah Wali (1422-35) transferred capital from Gulbarga (Ahsanabad) to Bidar (Muhammadabad) in 1427. He was ruler of Bahmani Sultanate and succeeded Firoz Shah Bahmani.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: What is the meaning of the word 'Taraf' under the Bahmani Sultanate? [SSC JE 06/06/2024]
solution:
(D) Province
In Bahmani Sultanate, province is called 'Taraf' and officer of each province was 'Tarafdar'. Bahmani kingdom was divided into 4 provinces (Tarafs) - Gulbarga, Bidar, Daulatabad and Berar.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Which of the following did not come into existence as a result of the disintegration of the Bahmani Empire during the 16th century? [SSC CPO 05/10/2023]
solution:
(D) Gaur
During 16th century Bahmani Kingdom broke into 5 kingdoms: Berar, Bijapur, Ahmadnagar, Golconda and Bidar.

subject: Medieval History
question_text: Select the INCORRECT pair of a famous minaret and its construction year in India. [SSC CPO 05/10/2023]
solution:
(B) Char Minar-1664 CE
Char Minar was built by Muhammad Quli Qutub Shah in 1591. He was the greatest ruler of Qutub Shahi dynasty. He founded city of Hyderabad.

subject: Medieval History
