Raja Sawai Jai Singh of Amber and the Maratha General Parshuram Bhau tried to promote widow remarriage but failed. Raja Ram Mohan Roy opposed Sati and due to his efforts it was banned by Sati Regulation Act 1829.

subject: Modern History
question_text:
Swami Dayanand Saraswati: [NDA I 2025]
1. was opposed to the worship of idols of Gods and Goddesses.
2. regarded the Vedas as infallible.
3. had met and had discussions with Ishwar Chandra Vidyasagar.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
solution:
(A) 1, 2 and 3
Swami Dayanand Saraswati believed in monotheism, he was against idol worship of Gods and Goddesses. He was inspired with Vedas and considered them as infallible and true seed of Hinduism. Dayanand met other reformers Keshab Chandra Sen, Ishwar Chandra Vidyasagar, MG Ranade, Deshmukh, etc and had discussions with them.

subject: Modern History
question_text:
Consider the following Women's Associations and their important associates in early twentieth century India: [NDA I 2025]
1. Women's Indian Association of 1917: Margaret Cousins
2. National Council of Women in India of 1925: Sarala Devi Chaudhurani
3. Bharat Stree Mahamandal: Lady Meherbai Tata
Which of the above pairs is/are correctly matched?
solution:
(D) 1 only
Women's Indian Association (WIA) was founded in 1917 in Adyar, Madras (Chennai) by Annie Besant, Margaret Cousins. Published a journal 'Stri Dharma'. National Council of Women in India (NCWI) was founded in 1925 by Lady Meherbai Tata (wife of Dorabji Tata) and Lady Aberdeen of International Council of Women. Bharat Stree Mahamandal of 1910 was founded by Sarla Devi Chaudhurani in Allahabad. (So 2 and 3 are incorrect).

subject: Modern History
question_text:
Which one among the following statements about Dr. Muthulakshmi Reddy, a freedom fighter, is not correct? [CDS I 2025]
A) She was the first girl student to be admitted into a men's college.
B) She was the first woman legislator in British India.
C) She was a medical professional.
D) She was honoured with Bharat Ratna in 1956.

options.a: She was the first girl student to be admitted into a men's college.
options.b: She was the first woman legislator in British India.
options.d: She was honoured with Bharat Ratna in 1956.

solution:
(D) She was honoured with Bharat Ratna in 1956.
Dr. Muthulakshmi Reddy was the first Indian girl student to be admitted to men's college and also the first woman to be admitted to Madras Medical College, where she became the first woman House Surgeon. She was the first woman legislator of British India and served as the member of Legislative Council from Madras. She founded Adyar Cancer Institute in Chennai (1954) and was honoured with Padma Vibhushan in 1956.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who, among the following, introduced a resolution in 1882 which is also called the Magna Carta of Local Self-Government in India? [NDA I 2025]
solution:
(C) Lord Ripon
In 1882, Lord Ripon, the then Viceroy introduced local self-government. It laid the foundation of democratic decentralization in India and aimed to increase the powers of local bodies like municipalities and district boards. Lord Ripon (1880 - 1884) : Father of Local Self Government. Brought Hunter Commission (1882) which was related to education. 1st synchronised/ complete Census in 1881. Repealed Vernacular Press Act.

subject: Modern History
question_text: In which year was the first war of independence taken place in India? [SSC GD 24/07/2024, SSC CHSL 02/07/2024]
solution:
(A) 1857
The Indian Rebellion or the first war of Independence took place in 1857. It is also known as Sepoy Mutiny of 1857. VD Savarkar termed it as the 'first war of Independence' in his book 'The History of the War of Indian Independence'. Lord Canning was the Governor General of India in 1857 and also the first Viceroy of India.

subject: Modern History
question_text: The revolution of 1857 started in May from which of the following cantonments in India? [SSC CGL 26/08/2024]
solution:
(B) Meerut
The revolution of 1857 was unsuccessful uprising in India in 1857 against the rule of British East India Company. It started from the Meerut cantonment of India. Kanpur, Jhansi, Lucknow were major centres of the rebellion. In Rajasthan, the revolt started from Naseerabad cantonment.

subject: Modern History
question_text: With reference to Sepoy Mutiny of 1857, on which of the following dates did the soldiers at Meerut start their journey to Delhi? [SSC CGL 02/09/2024]
solution:
(B) 10 May
On April 24, 90 men of 3rd Native Cavalry refused to accept the greased cartridges. On May 9, 85 of them were arrested and dismissed. This sparked a mutiny among Indian soldiers stationed in Meerut. On May 10, they released their imprisoned Indian soldiers, killed British officers and set out for Delhi after sunset.

subject: Modern History
question_text: The sepoys of Meerut arrived at the Gates of Red Fort to meet the Mughal emperor, Bahadur Shah on ________. [SSC MTS 18/05/2023]
solution:
(D) 11 May 1857
The sepoys of Meerut marched for Delhi on the evening of 10 May 1857 and reached Red Fort in Delhi on 11 May, 1857. They declared Bahadur Shah Zafar (last Mughal emperor) their leader. Delhi soon became the centre of the revolt and Bahadur Shah, its symbol.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who among the following leaders led the Revolt of 1857 in Bihar? [SSC MTS 04/09/2023]
solution:
(D) Kunwar Singh
Veer Kunwar Singh led the revolt of 1857 in Bihar. He was the Zamindar of Jagdishpur and belonged to Ujjainiya Rajput clan. He joined the sepoys when they reached Arrah from Dinapore. In March 1858, he conquered Azamgarh. William Taylor suppressed revolt in Bihar.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Nana Sahib, a rebel at Kanpur was the son of which of the following Peshwas? [SSC CGL 17/07/2023]
solution:
(C) Baji Rao II
Nana Saheb (or Dhondu Pant) was the adopted son of Peshwa Baji Rao II. Nana Saheb expelled the English from Kanpur, proclaimed himself as Peshwa, acknowledged Bahadur Shah as the emperor of India and declared himself his governor. Colin Campbell defeated Nana Saheb.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Khan Bahadur led the revolt of 1857 from: [SSC CGL 24/07/2023]
solution:
(D) Bareilly
Khan Bahadur was the descendant of former ruler of Rohilkhand. He formed his own government in Bareilly in 1857. Bareilly was recaptured by British on 5 May, 1858. Colin Campbell suppressed revolt in Bareilly and Kanpur.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which Mughal ruler contributed to the revolt of 1857? [SSC CGL 25/07/2023]
solution:
(D) Bahadur Shah II
Bahadur Shah II (Bahadur Shah Zafar), he was the last Mughal ruler of India and became the symbol of revolt of 1857. General Bakht Khan was leading the revolt in Bahadur Shah's name at Delhi. The British captured Delhi and Bahadur Shah was exiled in Rangoon (Myanmar) where he died in 1862. John Nicholson suppressed the revolt in Delhi.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Mangal Pandey was hanged to death for attacking British officers in Barrackpore on ________. [SSC MTS 13/07/2023]
solution:
(D) 8 April, 1857
Anger among Indian soldiers on use of cow and pig fat in greased cartridges on new Enfield rifle, Mangal Pandey was a sepoy of 34th Native Infantry, fired at the sergeant major at Barrackpore on 29 March, 1857. He was arrested and hanged on 8 April, 1857. This sparked the sepoy mutiny of 1857.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Whose proclamation of November 1, 1858 declared that thereafter India would be governed by and in the name of the British Monarch through a Secretary of State? [SSC MTS 08/10/2021]
solution:
(C) Queen Victoria's
After the revolt of 1857, the British Parliament passed Government of India Act of 1858. It established Crown Rule and ended company rule. 'Queen's Proclamation' was issued on 1 Nov. 1858, it made Queen Victoria the ruler of British East India territories. Governor General was made 'Viceroy', Lord Canning was the 1st Viceroy.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who among the following was the Governor General of India when the Permanent Settlement was introduced in Bengal for revenue collection by the East India Company? [SSC CHSL 14/08/2023]
solution:
(A) Lord Cornwallis
Permanent Settlement was brought in Bengal by East India Company headed by Governor-General Lord Cornwallis in 1793. Also called the 'Zamindari system'. It was an agreement between Company and Zamindar for fixed land revenue. Regions - Bengal, Bihar and Odisha.

subject: Modern History
question_text: What was the meaning of 'Ryot' in British records in the 18th century? [SSC CHSL 19/04/2021]
solution:
(A) Peasants
In British records in 18th century, 'Ryot' referred to a peasant or cultivator who work on a land belonging to a landlord or a state. Ryots were not the owner of the land.

subject: Modern History
question_text: ________ introduced the daroga system, replacing zamindar thanadars under the direct control of the district head and on its payroll. [SSC CGL 18/07/2023]
solution:
(B) Lord Cornwallis
In 1791, Cornwallis organised a regular police force and introduced Daroga system, which replaced zamindari thanedars. Thanas (circles) were headed by a Daroga under a superintendent of police (SP) as the head of district. Brought Cornwallis Code in 1793. Cornwallis also called 'Father of Indian Civil Services'.

subject: Modern History
question_text: The system of Mahalwari settlement was devised by ________. [SSC GD 07/03/2024]
solution:
(C) Holt Mackenzie
Mahalwari system was introduced by Holt Mackenzie in Bengal in 1822. The word 'Mahal' means group of villages. It was reformed and introduced by William Bentinck in Punjab in 1833. In this, the land revenue was collected by a village headman, who then pay it to the Company.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which of the following terms indicates a system/arrangement implemented in Bengal by Lord Cornwallis under which zamindars were granted legal ownership of land for receiving a certain revenue by a certain date? [SSC CPO 15/10/2020]
solution:
(D) Permanent settlement
Permanent Settlement (Zamindari system) in 1793 by Cornwallis in Bengal region. The Zamindars were made the owner of the land and got the right of succession over land. Talukdari system, land revenue collected by Talukdars, in Bombay region, introduced by Canning.

subject: Modern History
question_text: From which places did Lord Cornwallis start the permanent settlement system? [SSC CHSL 10/08/2023]
solution:
(A) Bengal and Bihar
The Permanent Settlement was introduced first in Bengal, Bihar and Odisha and later in Varanasi and some northern districts of Madras. It was brought by Cornwallis in 1793.

subject: Modern History
question_text: The Ryotwari system, devised by Thomas Munro in which peasant cultivators had to pay annual taxes directly to the government, was prevalent in which of the following present-day states/provinces? [CGL 28/05/2024]
solution:
(C) Tamil Nadu
Ryotwari System was introduced by Thomas Munro and Alexander Reed. Implemented in 1820. Direct relationship between British and cultivators. Peasants (Ryots) had to pay taxes directly to British. Landownership was given to the ryots. It was started in Madras and later in Bombay and Assam.

subject: Modern History
question_text: The Permanent Settlement introduced by Cornwallis was NOT prevalent in which of the following regions of India? [SSC MTS 14/10/2024]
solution:
(D) Punjab
The Permanent Settlement system was introduced by Cornwallis in Bengal, Bihar and Odisha region. Mahalwari system was established in Punjab. Ryotwari system was introduced in Madras region.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Ryotwari system of revenue collection in India, introduced by the British, was based on the ________. [SSC CGL 14/07/2023]
solution:
(B) Ricardian theory of rent
Ryotwari system was introduced by Thomas Munro in 1820. It was based on Ricardian Theory of rent, given by David Ricardo in 1817. It suggests that landowners earn surplus income due to land fertility differences.

subject: Modern History
question_text: After the revolt of 1857, which of the following changes was not made by the British government? [SSC MTS 13/08/2019]

options.a: The secretary of State for India was appointed
options.b: The number of Indian soldiers was increased in the British Army
options.c: The rights of East India Company were handed over to the British Empire
options.d: The Governor General of India became the Viceroy of India

solution:
(B) The number of Indian soldiers was increased in the British Army
After the revolt of 1857, the British Parliament ended Company rule and the Crown rule was established. It handed the rights of the Company to British Empire. A Secretary of State for India was appointed. The Governor General of India became the Viceroy of India, Canning was the first Viceroy. Doctrine of Lapse was withdrawn. The army was reorganised and number of Indian soldiers was reduced and British soldiers were increased.

subject: Modern History
question_text: "Janjatiya Gaurav Diwas" is observed on the birth anniversary of ________. [SSC MTS 16/06/2023]
solution:
(C) Birsa Munda
Janjatiya Gaurav Diwas was celebrated on Nov. 15, on birth anniversary of Birsa Munda, a tribal leader. Ho and Munda uprising (1899) or Ulgulan uprising against Dikus (outsiders), led by Birsa Munda in Ranchi, Singhbhum. Khuntkatti System (collective ownership) was followed by tribes. Jharkhand foundation day also celebrated on Nov 15. Birsa Munda died in 1900.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which of the following revolts was led by Kanhu and Sidhu? [SSC CGL 05/12/2022]
solution:
(D) Santhal Revolt
Santhal Rebellion (1855) by two brothers Sidhu and Kanhu in Rajmahal hills (Bihar) against Zamindars and Britishers. Santhal is the third largest tribe in India after Bhil and Gond. Damini-i-Koh, is a large area demarcated for Santhals.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who among the following led of the famous Indigo Revolt in Bengal? [SSC MTS 20/07/2022]

options.b: Bishnu Charan Biswas and Digambar Biswas

solution:
(B) Bishnu Charan Biswas and Digambar Biswas
Indigo Revolt (1859) in Nadia district of Bengal led by Bishnu Charan Biswas and Digambar Biswas. Farmers revolted against Britishers who forced them to grow Indigo on large scale. It was a successful revolt. Dinabandhu Mitra wrote Nil Darpan (play about Indigo revolt).

subject: Modern History
question_text: Veer Surendra Sai was a freedom fighter from: [SSC CGL 13/08/2021]
solution:
(D) Odisha
Veer Surendra Sai, belonged to royal family of Sambalpur, Odisha. After death of his father Raja Maharaja Sai in 1827, British rejected Surendra Sai's legitimate claim and appointed a puppet ruler. He led armed rebellion with Gond and Binjhal tribes against British and he was captured in 1864.

subject: Modern History
question_text: As a result of which act did Queen Victoria, who was the monarch of Britain, also became the sovereign of British territories in India, with the title "Empress of India" in 1858? [SSC MTS 10/05/2023]
solution:
(B) Government of India Act
The Government of India Act was passed by British Parliament on August 2, 1858. It declared Queen Victoria the sovereign of British India territories and a Secretary of State for India was appointed. Queen's Proclamation issued on Nov. 1, 1858 was announced by Lord Canning at a Durbar in Allahabad.

subject: Modern History
question_text: The Permanent Settlement came into operation in which of the following year? [SSC CGL 23/09/2024, SSC GD 2024]
solution:
(D) 1793
The Permanent Settlement was introduced first in Bengal, Bihar and Odisha and later in Varanasi and some northern districts of Madras. It was brought by Cornwallis in 1793.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which of the following princely states was annexed by the East India Company in the year 1852 AD under the Doctrine of Lapse? [SSC JE 06/06/2024]
solution:
(A) Udaipur
Doctrine of Lapse (1848) was introduced by Dalhousie. British EIC can annex a state if the ruler died without male heir. Acquired Satara (1848), Jaitpur and Sambhalpur in 1849, Udaipur (1852), Jhansi (1853), Nagpur (1854).

subject: Modern History
question_text: The First Cotton Mill in India was set up as a spinning mill in 1854 in ________. [SSC CHSL 09/07/2019]
solution:
(B) Bombay
'Bombay Spinning and Weaving Company' was founded by Cowasjee Nanabhoy Davar in 1854. It was the first spinning cotton mill in India. First Cotton mill in India was at Fort Gloster, Calcutta in 1818 but it failed due to lack of infrastructure.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which of the following war/battle is associated with the hanging of Mangal Pandey? [SSC GD 21/02/2024]
solution:
(A) Indian Rebellion of 1857
Mangal Pandey was a freedom fighter of Indian Rebellion of 1857. Belonged to 34th Native Infantry. He was hanged by British on April 8, 1857. First Anglo-Burmese War (1824 - 1826) ended with Treaty of Yandabo. Second Anglo-Maratha War (1803 - 1805), Battle of Plassey (1757).

subject: Modern History
question_text: 18th century freedom fighter Veerapandya Kattabomman was from which present state? [SSC MTS 16/08/2019]
solution:
(B) Tamil Nadu
Veerapandya Kattabomman, a Palayakkarar or Polygar of Panchalankurichi in Tamil Nadu. Kattabomman refused to accept British suzerainty in 1795 and declined to pay taxes, it resulted in First Polygar War of 1799.

subject: Modern History
question_text: During the 1857 uprising, who among the following took up the leadership at Faizabad? [SSC CPO 13/12/2019]
solution:
(B) Maulvi Ahmadullah
In 1857 revolt, Maulvi Ahmadullah was the leader of Faizabad (Awadh region). Also known as 'lighthouse of rebellion'. Bihar - Kunwar Singh, Lucknow - Begum Hazrat Mahal. Bareilly - Khan Bahadur Khan (Rohilla).

subject: Modern History
question_text: Manikarnika Tambe is better known in history as ________. [SSC CPO 15/03/2019]
solution:
(B) Rani Laxmibai
Rani Laxmibai also known as Manikarnika Tambe (Manu) was the leader of 1857 revolt from Jhansi with Tantia Tope. It was supressed in Jhansi by Hugh Rose, who defeated Laxmibai in 1858. After her husband Raja Gangadhar Rao died without male heir, Dalhousie annexed Jhansi under Doctrine of Lapse in 1853 which led to the revolt.

subject: Modern History
question_text: In which of the following cities is the tomb of Rani of Jhansi situated? [SSC CGL 05/03/2020]
solution:
(B) Gwalior
The Samadhi (tomb) of Rani Lakshmi Bai is located in Phool Bagh in Gwalior. She died while fighting in a battle of Kotah ki Serai in Gwalior on 17 June 1858. Hugh Rose described Lakshmi Bai as 'most dangerous of all Indian leaders'. The revolt of 1857 ended on 20 June 1858 in Gwalior.

subject: Modern History
question_text: In which of the following years did the revolt in the countryside of the Bombay Deccan occur? [SSC CGL 16/08/2021]
solution:
(D) 1875
The revolt in Bombay Deccan countryside occurred in 1875. It was a peasant uprising against moneylenders. It began at Supa village in Poona. Due to heavy taxation under Ryotwari system. The government increased the land revenue to 50%, which led to riots.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Dadabhai Naoroji offered a scathing criticism of the economic impact of British rule through his book entitled ________. [SSC CGL 03/12/2022]

options.c: Poverty and Un-British Rule in India

solution:
(C) Poverty and Un-British Rule in India
Dadabhai Naoroji offered criticism of economic impact of British rule through his book 'Poverty and Un-British Rule in India' (1901). Gives Drain Theory, theory of Indian wealth drain into Britain. Also known as 'Grand Old Man of India'.

subject: Modern History
question_text: A large area of land demarcated as Damin-i-Koh in 1832 was declared as the land of the ________. [SSC CHSL 12/04/2021]
solution:
(D) Santhals
A large area in Rajmahal hills region of Jharkhand, Bihar was demarcated as Damin-i-Koh (means 'skirts of the hills') for Santhals in 1832. Santhals were invited to clear forests and settle land for cultivation. Their exploitation by landlords led to Santhal rebellion (1855).

subject: Modern History
question_text:
Which of the following pairs is/are correctly matched? [CAPF 2021]
1. Permanent Settlement: Lord Cornwallis
2. Ryotwari Settlement: Thomas Munro
3. Mahalwari Settlement: Holt Mackenzie
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
solution:
(D) 1, 2 and 3
Permanent Settlement (Zamindari System), by Cornwallis in 1793 in Bengal, Bihar, Odisha region. Fixed Tax collected by Zamindars from cultivators. Ryotwari System by Thomas Munro and Reed in 1820 in Madras region, based on Ricardian Theory of Rent. It is a direct relationship between Ryots (peasants) and the British. Mahalwari System, by Holt Mackenzie and Bentinck in 1822 in Punjab and Northwest region. Tax collected by a village headman.

subject: Modern History
question_text:
Which one of the following statements with regard to ryotwari settlement is not correct? [CDS I 2021]
A) In southern and western India, the ryotwari settlement was adopted.
B) Ryotwari was in principle a direct contract between the ryot and the state.
C) It means a tax contract valid for usually 30 years.
D) In principle, it strengthened the former elite, the zamindars and weakened the peasantry.

options.a: In southern and western India, the ryotwari settlement was adopted.
options.b: Ryotwari was in principle a direct contract between the ryot and the state.
options.c: It means a tax contract valid for usually 30 years.
options.d: In principle, it strengthened the former elite, the zamindars and weakened the peasantry.

solution:
(D) In principle, it strengthened the former elite, the zamindars and weakened the peasantry
The Ryotwari System was adopted in Southern (Madras) and western India. By Thomas Munro in 1820. Direct contract between ryots (peasants) and the state (British). Tax contract often had a period of 20 to 30 years. Zamindars used to get some part of revenue in Zamindari system. Ryotwari system bypassed the Zamindars and weakened them. It reduced the exploitation of peasantry by Zamindars and strengthened them.

subject: Modern History
question_text:
Which of the following statements about the Sanyasi and Faqir disturbances/rebellions are correct? [CDS I 2021]
1. Governor-General Warren Hastings faced the persistent Sanyasi and Faqir disturbances in Bengal and Bihar.
2. There were a number of Shaivite Naga Sanyasis who formed into armed bands.
3. Majnu Shah, who led bands into Bengal from 1771, was their prominent leader.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
solution:
(B) 1, 2 and 3
Governor-General Warren Hastings faced the persistent Sanyasi and Faqir disturbances in Bengal and Bihar. Sanyasi revolt started in 1763. There were a number of Shaivite Naga Sanyasis who formed into armed bands. It was led by Majnu Shah, Bhawani Pathak, Chirag Ali and Debi Chaudharani. Majnu Shah (a Sufi Faqir) led bands into Bengal from 1771. Bankim Chandra Chatterjee's novel Anand Math mentions about Sanyasi Rebellion of 1763.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who among the following introduced the policy of 'Doctrine of Lapse'? [CISF AC 2021]
solution:
(D) Lord Dalhousie
Doctrine of Lapse, implemented by Dalhousie in 1848. It states that 'if the ruler of a state dies without a natural male heir, his kingdom would lapse (be annexed) to the British East India Company. Adopted sons were not considered as natural heirs. Dalhousie annexed Satara (1848), Jaitpur and Sambhalpur (1849), Baghat (1850), Udaipur (central India) in 1852, Jhansi (1853), Nagpur (1854) by using Doctrine of Lapse and Awadh (1856) annexed on grounds of 'misgovernance'.

subject: Modern History
question_text:
With reference to revenue collection by Cornwallis, consider the following statements: [IAS 2024]
1. Under the Ryotwari Settlement of revenue collection, the peasants were exempted from revenue payment in case of bad harvests or natural calamities.
2. Under the Permanent Settlement in Bengal, if the Zamindar failed to pay his revenues to the state, on or before the fixed date, he would be removed from his Zamindari.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
solution:
(B) 2 only
Under the Ryotwari Settlement the peasants were not exempted from revenue payment in case of natural calamities. Failure in payment could result in loss of land. The Sunset clause in Zamindari system (Permanent Settlement) was a provision that required Zamindars to pay fixed amount of revenue by a specific date, before sunset and if failed, he would be removed and his Zamindari will be auctioned.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which one of the following caused the ruin of the rural artisan industries in India in the 19th Century? [GEOSCIENTIST 2022]

options.a: Flooding of Indian markets with machine-produced goods on a mass scale
options.c: Promotion of expensive silk and woolen goods
options.d: Import of raw materials from Britain at high prices

solution:
(A) Flooding of Indian markets with machine-produced goods on mass scale
Due to rise in Industrialisation in Britain in early 19th century, machine made goods were important in India in large quantities. They were mass produced and cheaper, which ruined the handmade industry of Indian rural artisans.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Nil Darpan, which deals with the condition of indigo planters, was written by: [GEOSCIENTIST 2023]
solution:
(D) Dinabandhu Mitra
Indigo Revolt (1859) started in Nadia (West Bengal). Dinabandhu Mitra mentions Indigo revolt in his Nil Darpan. Michael Madhusudan Dutta wrote 'Meghnad-Badh-Kabya' (1861) and play 'Sarmistha' (1859). Bankim Chandra Chatterjee wrote Anandmath mentions about Sanyasi revolt 1763. Lal Behari Dey wrote 'Folk-Tales of Bengal' and a magazine 'Arunodaya'.

subject: Modern History
question_text:
Which of the following statements about the Deccan Riots Commission is/are correct? [NDA I 2021]
1. The Commission did not hold enquiries in the districts which were not affected.
2. The Commission did record the statements of ryots, sahukars and eye-witnesses.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
solution:
(C) Both 1 and 2
The Deccan Riots Commission was established after series of riots in Deccan region of India from 1875, in Pune, Ahmednagar, Bombay. It was led by Alexander Arbuthnot. Outcomes - Deccan Agriculturists Relief Act (1879) was passed. The commission recorded the statements of ryots, sahukars and eye-witnesses but did not hold enquires in districts which were not affected.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which kingdom was described by Governor-General Lord Dalhousie as "A cherry that will drop into our mouth one day"? [CDS 2024]
solution:
(B) Awadh
Lord Dalhousie, Governor General of India from 1848 to 1856. He described Awadh as 'A cherry that will drop into our mouth one day'. Mirza Wajid Ali Shah was the last Nawab of Awadh. Dalhousie annexed Awadh (1856) after deposing the Nawab on the grounds of misgovernance. The Nawab was exiled to Calcutta. People of Awadh felt it as an insult and rebelled against British in 1857.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which one of the following statements about the ishtahars issued during the Revolt of 1857 is correct? [NDA I 2021]

options.a: They glorified the Muslim rule in India.
options.b: They glorified the co-existence of different communities under the Mughal Empire.
options.c: They glorified the message of Islam.
options.d: They glorified the role of Queen Victoria.

solution:
(B) They glorified the co-existence of different communities under the Mughal Empire
During the revolt of 1857, Ishtahars were issued by rebel leaders that glorified the co-existence of different communities under Mughal Empire, under rulers like Akbar. Aim was to unite Indians across religious lines. Issued on the name of Bahadur Shah Zafar and it criticized Britishers.

subject: Modern History
question_text:
Which one of the following propositions from the book History of British India' by James Mill is correct? [GEOSCIENTIST 2024]
A) The tax payable by the zamindars should not be more than thirty percent of the total produce.
B) The rights of cultivators should be protected by the State.
C) The State should remain as the landlord and that each peasant should hold land directly from the State as its tenant.
D) Sufficient advances should be given to the peasants SO that they are encouraged to cultivate cash crops.

options.a: The tax payable by the zamindars should not be more than thirty percent of the total produce.
options.b: The rights of cultivators should be protected by the State.
options.c: The State should remain as the landlord and that each peasant should hold land directly from the State as its tenant.
options.d: Sufficient advances should be given to the peasants SO that they are encouraged to cultivate cash crops.

solution:
(C) The State should remain as the landlord and that each peasant should hold land directly from the State as its tenant
James Mill (Scottish economist and historian) wrote 'The History of British in India' in 1817. He divided Indian history into 3 parts - Hindu, Muslim and British. Mill supported Ryotwari system, where the state should act as landlord and peasants (ryots) pay taxes directly to government. Mill believed zamindars were corrupt and inefficient.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which of the following organisations was established in 1885? [SSC CGL 27/07/2023]
solution:
(D) Indian National Congress
Indian National Congress established was established on December 28, 1885 in Bombay. It is the oldest political party of India. Communist Party of India was founded in 1925 by MN Roy in Kanpur. Muslim League was established in 1906 in Dhaka.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who among the following was one of the founders of the Indian National Congress in 1885? [SSC MTS 15/10/2024]
solution:
(B) Allan Octavian Hume
Indian National Congress (1885) was founded by Allan Octavian Hume along with Dadabhai Naoroji, WC Banerjee, Dinshaw Edulji Wacha. A.O. Hume was a qualified civil servant and known as 'Father of Indian Ornithology'. Ornithology - study of birds.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who among the following was the Viceroy of India during the formation of the Indian National Congress? [SSC CPO 11/11/2022]
solution:
(C) Lord Dufferin
Lord Dufferin was Viceroy of India from 1884 to 1888. AO Hume obtained the Viceroy's Consent from Dufferin in May 1885 to establish the 'Indian National Congress'. Lord Lytton was Viceroy from 1876 - 1880. Lord Ripon (1880 - 1884). Lord Mayo (1869 - 1872).

subject: Modern History
question_text: In which year was the first session of the Indian National Congress held? [SSC MTS 17/10/2024]
solution:
(B) 1885
First Session of Indian National Congress (INC) held in 1885 at Gokuldas Tejpal Sanskrit College in Bombay, presided over by WC Banerjee. Planned to be held in Pune but was not possible due to plague. Attended by 72 delegates. No women and 2 Muslims participated. First resolution was proposed by G Subramania Iyer.

subject: Modern History
question_text: In which of the following sessions of the Indian National Congress did George Yule become the President in 1888? [SSC CGL 12/04/2022]
solution:
(B) Allahabad
Fourth Session of INC was held at Allahabad in 1888, presided by George Yule. He was the first British President of INC. 1st session (1885) - Bombay, President - WC Banerjee. 2nd Session (1886) - Calcutta, President - Dadabhai Naoroji. 3rd Session (1887) - Madras, President - Badruddin Tyabji. William Wedderburn presided INC session 2 times, most by any foreigner.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Indian National Congress celebrated the first Independence Day in Colonial India on ________. [SSC STENOGRAPHER 12/10/2023]
solution:
(D) 26 January, 1930
Lahore Session (1929) of INC: President - Jawaharlal Nehru. Resolution for 'Poorna Swaraj'. Civil Disobedience movement for Complete Independence was launched. 26 January 1930 to be observed as 'Independence Day'.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who led the Indian National Congress at its first annual session? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 13/10/2023]
solution:
(C) W.C. Banerjee
W.C. Banerjee led the first session of Congress in 1885 in Bombay. Surendranath Banerjee led the Ahmedabad session of Congress in 1902. AO Hume is the founder of Indian National Congress. Indian Association was founded in 1876 by Surendranath Banerjee and Ananda Mohan Bose.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who was the first Woman President of Indian National Congress? [SSC MTS 08/09/2023]
solution:
(C) Annie Besant
Annie Besant was the first women president of INC. She presided the Calcutta Session of 1917. Founded Central Hindu College in Banaras in 1898, which later became part of Banaras Hindu University (founded by Madan Mohan Malviya in 1916). Annie Besant founded Home Rule League (1916) at Madras.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Sarojini Naidu served as President of the Indian National Congress in ________. [SSC GD 21/02/2024]
solution:
(D) 1925
Kanpur Session of 1925, President - Sarojini Naidu. She was the first Indian woman President of INC. She published 'In the Bazaars of Hyderabad'. Wrote: The Golden Threshold, The Bird of Time, The Broken Wing. Also known as 'The Nightingale of India'.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Sarojini Naidu was elected first Indian woman President of the Indian National Congress at ________. [SSC CGL 06/12/2022]
solution:
(A) Kanpur
Sarojini Naidu, first Indian women President of Congress in Kanpur Session (1925). She was the first woman Governor of Independent India, Governor of United Provinces (Uttar Pradesh) in 1947, died in office of Governor in 1947. The Name 'United Province' changed to Uttar Pradesh in Jan 1950.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which among the following sessions of Indian National Congress was the first session to be presided by a lady? [SSC MTS 02/05/2023]
solution:
(A) Calcutta - 1917
Calcutta Session (1917) was presided by Annie Besant, First Women President. Also known as 'Annie Wood'. Kanpur Session (1925) was presided by Sarojini Naidu. Lahore Session (1929) presided by Jawaharlal Nehru. Surat Session (1907) presided by Rash Behari Ghosh.

subject: Modern History
question_text: The Indian National Congress was established when 72 delegates from all over the country met at Bombay in December ________. [SSC MTS 20/06/2023]
solution:
(C) 1885
On December 28, 1885, Indian National Congress was founded in Bombay. It was attended by 72 delegates across India. AO Hume was the General Secretary and WC Banerjee was President.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who amongst the following became the president of the second session of the Indian National Congress held in Calcutta in December 1886? [SSC CPO 28/06/2024]
solution:
(A) Dadabhai Naoroji
Dadabhai Naoroji presided second session of INC at Calcutta in 1886. Also known as 'Grand Old Man of India'. Started journal 'Rast Goftar' for Parsi community and newspaper 'Voice of India' in 1883. Also presided Lahore Session (1893) and Calcutta Session (1906). Used the term 'Swaraj' for first time in 1906. William Wedderburn was the President of Bombay Session (1889) and Allahabad Session (1910) of Congress. P. Ananda Charlu was President of Nagpur Session (1891).

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which flag of the Indian National Congress was adopted in 1931? [SSC MTS 12/09/2023]

options.a: A two colour flag with charkha made by cloth
options.b: A tricolour flag made by Khadi with a Charkha symbol
options.c: One colour flag with charkha symbol
options.d: A blue colour flag made by cloth

solution:
(B) A tricolour flag made by Khadi with a Charkha symbol
A tricolour flag, Saffron at top, White in middle and Green at bottom with a spinning wheel (Charkha) at centre. It was made of Khadi and adopted in 1931. Charkha and Khadi symbolises self-reliance and the Swadeshi movement. Called 'Swaraj Flag'. The first unofficial flag of India was hoisted in Calcutta in 1906.

subject: Modern History
question_text: In the 1939, Tripuri Congress Presidential Election, Pattabhi Sitaramayya was defeated by: [SSC MTS 13/09/2023]
solution:
(C) Subhas Chandra Bose
In 1939, Congress session held at Tripuri, Madhya Pradesh. Pattabhi Sitaramayya was appointed by Mahatma Gandhi against Subhas Chandra Bose. Bose defeated him and was elected as President but later resigned due to Gandhi's protest. Rajendra Prasad was elected as President in 1939.

subject: Modern History
question_text: In which session of All India Congress Committee was the Quit India Resolution accepted? [SSC CHSL 03/08/2023]
solution:
(D) Bombay
Quit India Resolution was accepted in Bombay Session of All India Congress Committee (AICC) on 8 August 1942. It started the Quit India Movement, also known as August Kranti. It was a civil disobedience movement launched by Mahatma Gandhi. In this session, Gandhi gave the slogan 'Do or Die'.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who among the following was one of the founders of the Swaraj Party? [SSC PHASE XII 20/06/2024]
solution:
(C) Chittaranjan Das
Swaraj Party was founded by Chittaranjan Das (CR Das) and Motilal Nehru on 1 Jan 1923 in Allahabad. Due to disagreement with Gandhi's withdrawal of Non-Cooperation Movement (1922) in response to Chauri Chaura incident.

subject: Modern History
question_text: In which session of the Indian National Congress was the resolution declaring Poorna Swaraj passed? [SSC CHSL 07/08/2023]
solution:
(D) Lahore
The resolution declaring 'Poorna Swaraj' was passed in Lahore Session (1929) of INC. Jawaharlal Nehru was the President. A declaration was passed to celebrate Independence Day of India on 26 January 1930 in this session.

subject: Modern History
question_text: The Swaraj party was formed within ________ with the thought of contesting elections to enter the legislative councils with a view to obstructing the governance of the colonial government. [SSC MTS 05/11/2024]
solution:
(A) Indian National Congress
The Swaraj Party was formed in 1923 in Allahabad. Founded by C.R. Das and Motilal Nehru. In the Gaya session (1922) presided by C.R. Das, proposal for entering the legislatures was rejected. Swaraj Party was formed within Congress and it advocated the idea of entering the legislative councils.

subject: Modern History
question_text: The second annual session of Indian National Congress was held at which of the following places? [SSC CHSL 11/08/2023]
solution:
(C) Calcutta
Second annual Session of INC, Calcutta Session (1886), President - Dadabhai Naoroji. Known as 'Grand Old Man of India'. Started journal 'Rast Goftar' for Parsi community and newspaper 'Voice of India' in 1883.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Self-Government or 'Swaraj' as the ultimate goal of the Indian National Congress-this declaration was made by Dadabhai Naoroji in which of the following sessions of the INC? [SSC CGL 20/07/2023]
solution:
(B) Calcutta
Calcutta Session (1906) was presided by Dadabhai Naoroji. The declaration of Self-Government or Swaraj was made. Dadabhai Naoroji was the first Indian elected to the British House of Commons in 1892 as a member of Liberal Party.

subject: Modern History
question_text: When did the All India Congress formalize the demand for 'Purna Swaraj' or full independence for India? [SSC CGL 21/07/2023]
solution:
(B) At its Lahore Session in 1929
Congress formalised the demand of 'Purna Swaraj' at its Lahore Session in 1929. Presided by Jawaharlal Nehru. Karachi Session (1931), Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel. Guwahati Session (1926), S. Srinivasa Iyengar. Madras Session (1927), M.A. Ansari.

subject: Modern History
question_text: In which year was the Lucknow Pact signed between Congress and Muslim League? [SSC GD 07/03/2024]
solution:
(C) 1916
Lucknow Session (1916) presided by Ambika Charan Majumdar. Lucknow pact was signed between INC headed by Bal Gangadhar Tilak and Muslim League led by Muhammad Ali Jinnah. Merger of Moderates and Extremists after Surat Split (1907). Sarojini Naidu gave Jinnah title of 'Ambassador of Hindu-Muslim Unity'.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which of the following Viceroys of British India took the first step separate provincial finance from central finance and introduced financial decentralisation in India in 1870? [SSC CGL 03/08/2022]
solution:
(C) Lord Mayo
Lord Mayo (1869 - 1872): 4th Viceroy. 1st Census held in 1872 but was incomplete (unsynchronised). Established Statistical Survey of India. Financial Decentralisation (1870), Provinces were given certain Revenue sources like, land revenue, excise, stamps, forest. First and only Viceroy of India to be assassinated. He was killed by Sher Ali, during Andaman visit in 1872.

subject: Modern History
question_text: In ________ at its Madras session, the Indian National Congress set up an All Parties Conference to draft a constitution for India. [SSC MTS 03/05/2023]
solution:
(C) 1927
Madras Session (1927) was presided by Mukhtar Ahmad Ansari. In response to Simon Commission (1927). INC decided to not cooperate with commission and it set up All Parties Conference to draft a Constitution for India. Resolution against the use of Indian troops in China, Mesopotamia and Persia.

subject: Modern History
question_text: The Indian National Congress passed Purna Swaraj resolution in 1929 under the presidency of ________. [SSC MTS 09/05/2023]
solution:
(C) Jawaharlal Nehru
Indian National Congress, Lahore Session (1929), President - Jawaharlal Nehru. Resolution on 'Purna Swaraj' passed. Belgaum Session (1924), President - Mahatma Gandhi. Gandhi only presided one session of INC. Calcutta Session (1901) - first time Gandhi appeared in a session. Special Session of Congress, Calcutta Session (1920), President - Lala Lajpat Rai. Resolution on Non-Cooperation Movement.

subject: Modern History
question_text: The split in Indian National Congress occurred in ________. [SSC MTS 17/05/2023]
solution:
(D) 1907
Surat Session (1907) presided by Rash Behari Ghosh. Ideological rift between INC members which led to Split of Congress into two - Moderates and Extremists.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Badruddin Tyabji addressed the congress as president in ________. [SSC MTS 13/06/2023]
solution:
(A) 1887
Madras Session (1887) presided by Badruddin Tyabji. It was the 3rd session of Congress. He was the first Muslim president of Congress. He also started the Bombay Presidency Association (1885) with Pherozshah Mehta and K.T. Telang.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who among the following was the President of the Indian National Congress during the 1915 session? [SSC CGL TIER-II 06/03/2023]
solution:
(A) Satyendra Prasanna Sinha
INC, Bombay Session (1915) presided by Satyendra Prasanna Sinha, first Indian member of British House of Lords in 1919. Ambika Charan Mazumdar - Lucknow (1916). Bhupendra Nath Bose - Madras Session (1914). Madan Mohan Malviya - Lahore (1909), Delhi (1918), Delhi (1932) and Calcutta (1933). Madan Mohan Malviya was conferred Bharat Ratna in 2014. He presided INC most number of times (4 times).

subject: Modern History
question_text: How many delegates participated in the second session of Indian National Congress? [SSC CGL 07/12/2022]
solution:
(A) 434
2nd Session of INC, at Calcutta in 1886, President - Dadabhai Naoroji. Attended by 434 delegates. Congress decided to set up Provincial Congress Committees across India. First Session of INC, Bombay Session (1885) was attended by 72 delegates.

subject: Modern History
question_text: In which of the following years did the resolution at the Karachi Session of the Indian National Congress dwell on how Independent India's Constitution should look? [SSC CGL 13/04/2022]
solution:
(A) 1931
Karachi Session (1931), President - Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel. It took place after Gandhi-Irwin Pact on March 5, 1931 and after execution of Bhagat Singh, Sukhdev and Rajguru on March 23, 1931. Resolution on Fundamental Rights of Indian citizens and Economic Policy.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who among the following people was popular as the "Nightingale of India"? [SSC GD 23/02/2024]
solution:
(B) Sarojini Naidu
Mahatma Gandhi gave the title 'Nightingale of India' to Sarojini Naidu. She was first Indian Women President of INC in Kanpur Session (1925). Sarojini Naidu, born on February 13, celebrated as National Women's Day in India. Margaret Thatcher, first Female Prime Minister of UK. Indira Gandhi, first and only female PM of India from 1966 to 1777 and 1980 to 1984. Indira Gandhi was also the first women finance minister of India.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who among the following was elected as the President of the Indian National Congress in the year 1938 and 1939? [SSC MTS 07/10/2021]
solution:
(A) Subhash Chandra Bose
Subhas Chandra Bose elected as president in Haripura Session (1938) and Tripuri Session (1939). Bose defeated Pattabhi Sitaramayya in Tripuri Session. Bose resigned and Rajendra Prasad was elected as president in 1939. Bose founded All India Forward Block in 1939.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel served President of the Congress in ________. [SSC GD 23/02/2024]
solution:
(C) 1931
Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel served President of Karachi Session (1931) of INC. It adopted resolutions on Fundamental Rights and Economic Policy. Gandhi was nominated to represent INC in Second Round Table Conference in London in 1931.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who among the following presided over the special session of Congress in September 1923 and at the age of 35 became the youngest man to be elected as the President of the Congress? [SSC CHSL 06/08/2021]
solution:
(D) Maulana Abul Kalam Azad
Special session of INC, Delhi (1923). President - Maulana Abul Kalam Azad at age of 35 years. He was the youngest president to preside over Congress Sessions. Also elected as President of Ramgarh Session (1940). He was awarded Bharat Ratna in 1992.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who was the President of the Indian National Congress when India won its freedom in the year 1947? [SSC CHSL 09/08/2021]
solution:
(D) J.B. Kripalani
J.B. Kripalani was the President of Congress during Independence in 1947. Kripalani presided the Meerut Session (1946), last session before Independence. Kripalani founded Kisan Mazdoor Praja Party in 1951. First Session after Independence, Jaipur Session (1948), President - Pattabhi Sitaramayya.

subject: Modern History
question_text: The National Anthem of India, 'Jana Gana Mana' was first sung at the ________ Session of the Indian National Congress. [SSC CPO 24/11/2020]
solution:
(A) Calcutta
National Anthem of India, 'Jana Gana Mana', first sung on 27 December 1911 at Calcutta Session (1911), President - Bishan Narayan Dhar. Originally written as Bharoto Bhagyo Bidhata by Rabindranath Tagore. National Anthem of India was adopted on 24 Jan 1950 along with National Song of India, Vande Mataram. Rabindranath Tagore, first Non-European to win Noble Prize for his literature 'Gitanjali' in 1913.

subject: Modern History
question_text: The book 'Poverty and Un-British Rule in India' was written by ________. [SSC CGL 23/09/2024]
solution:
(B) Dadabhai Naoroji
Dadabhai Naoroji wrote 'Poverty and Un-British Rule in India' in 1901. He criticised economic impact of British rule. He gave the theory of economic drain. First to calculate National Income and Poverty Line of India.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who is called the father of Local Self Government in India? [SSC CGL 24/09/2024]
solution:
(C) Lord Ripon
Viceroy Lord Ripon, (1880 - 1884) - Ilbert Bill Controversy. Repealed Vernacular Press Act, Lytton in 1878. 1st complete/ synchronised Census held in 1881. Father of Local Self Government. Hunter Commission (1882) was related to education. Factory Act (1881).

subject: Modern History
question_text: When was the All India Trade Union Congress (AITUC) established? [SSC CGL 24/09/2024]
solution:
(A) 1920
All India Trade Union Congress (AITUC) established in 1920 in Bombay with Lala Lajpat Rai as President. Founded by Lala Lajpat Rai, N.M. Joshi, Joseph Baptista. Headquarter is in Delhi. It is associated with Communist Party of India.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Where was the 1912 session of the Indian National Congress held in Bihar? [SSC CGL 24/09/2024]
solution:
(A) Bankipore
1912 Session of INC, held at Bankipore, Patna (Bihar). President - Raghunath Narasinha Mudholkar. 1922 Session of INC, held at Gaya (Bihar). President - C.R. Das. Held after suspension of Non-Cooperation Movement. Swaraj Party (1923) was formed after this session.

subject: Modern History
question_text: The Vernacular Press Act was enacted in which year in British India to limit the freedom of the Indian-language press? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 11/12/2024]
solution:
(A) 1878
Lord Lytton (1876 - 1880) : brought Arms Act (1878). Vernacular Press Act (1878). 1st Delhi Durbar in 1877, to proclaim Queen Victoria as Empress of India. He lowered the minimum age for Civil Services from 21 to 19 years. 1st newspaper Bengal Gazette by James Augustus Hickey published in 1780.

subject: Modern History
question_text:
Consider the following lists:
List I (Congress Session) - List II (President)
1. Bombay, 1885 - a. Rahimtullah M. Sayani
2. Calcutta, 1896 - b. Rash Behari Ghosh
3. Surat, 1907 - c. Sarojini Naidu
4. Kanpur, 1925 - d. Womesh Chandra Bannerjee
Select the correct answer using the code given below: [SSC MTS 17/10/2024]
solution:
(D) 1-d, 2-a, 3-b, 4-c
Bombay, 1885: W.C. Banerjee. Calcutta, 1896: Rahimtullah M. Sayani. Surat, 1907: Rash Behari Ghosh. Kanpur, 1925: Sarojini Naidu.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who among the following had written the National anthem of Bangladesh "Amar Sonar Bangla"? [SSC GD 27/02/2024]
solution:
(D) Rabindranath Tagore
The national anthem of Bangladesh 'Amar Sonar Bangla' written by Rabindranath Tagore. He composed it in 1905 during Bengal Partition by Curzon. First 10 lines adopted by Bangladesh as national anthem in 1971. Hasrat Mohani coined the slogan 'Inquilab Zindabad' in 1921. Shyamlal Gupta wrote song 'Jhanda Uncha Rahe Hamara'. Swami Yogananda Pramhansa is regarded as 'Father of Yoga in the West'.

subject: Modern History
question_text: In which year did Lord Dalhousie introduce the railway line in British India? [SSC CPO 28/06/2024]
solution:
(B) 1853
The first Railway Line was introduced by Lord Dalhousie in 1853. 1st train, from Bori Bunder (Bombay) to Thane, 34 km (21 Miles) distance, ran on 16 April 1853. The train had 14 carriages and was pulled by 3 steam locomotives - Sahib, Sindh and Sultan. It carried 400 passengers.

subject: Modern History
question_text: The Indian National Congress formally took up the Swadeshi call in its Benaras session of 1905 under the presidentship of ________. [SSC CPO 29/06/2024 + SSC MTS 21/10/2024]
solution:
(D) Gopal Krishna Gokhale
INC, Banaras Session (1905), President - Gopal Krishna Gokhale. Swadeshi resolution was passed, boycott of British goods. Protest against the Bengal Partition 1905, by Curzon on the lines of Hindu-Muslim.

subject: Modern History
question_text:
Consider the following historical events in India carefully:
1. The Indian press failed to protest against the Vernacular Press Act of 1878.
2. The Vernacular Press Act of 1878 was introduced by Viceroy Lytton against the counsel of his own Law Member.
Which of the above is/are correct? [CAPF 2022]
solution:
(B) 2 only
The Vernacular Press Act of 1878 was introduced by Viceroy Lytton. He passed this Act in spite of resistance from the Member of his Council. To control and punish the press criticising British India. The Indian Press actively protested against the Act. Due to strong opposition, the Vernacular Press Act was repealed in 1882 by Ripon. Charles T Metcalfe is known as liberator of Indian Press.

subject: Modern History
question_text:
Which one of the following pairs of Newspaper and Editor is not correctly matched? [CDS I 2022]
Newspaper - Editor

options.d: Voice of India - Gopal Krishna Gokhale

solution:
(D) Voice of India
Mahatma Gandhi was the editor and founder of Navjivan (1919). The Mahratta was English weekly newspaper started by Bal Gangadhar Tilak in 1881. Bengalee newspaper was founded by Girish Chandra Ghosh in 1862, Surendranath Banerjee served as its editor. Dadabhai Naoroji was the Editor of 'Voice of India'. He started this newspaper in 1883 in Bombay.

subject: Modern History
question_text:
Consider the following statements about the early phase of the National Movement in India: [CDS I 2024]
1. Ferozeshah Mehta, Badruddin Tyabji and others formed the Bombay Presidency Association.
2. Surendranath Banerjee and his group planned an Indian National Conference at Calcutta.
3. Allan Octavian Hume decided to create an all-India body as a rival to the above organisations to give vent to the grievances of the poor and marginalised Indians.
How many of the above statements is/are correct?
solution:
(B) 2
Bombay Presidency Association was formed in 1885 by Ferozeshah Mehta, Badruddin Tyabji and K.T. Telang. Surendranath Banerjee and Ananda Mohan Bose with their group organised the Indian National Conference in 1883 and in 1885 in Calcutta. AO Hume decided to create an all-India body, Indian National Congress in 1885. It was created not to act rival to existing organisations but to work with them together. To support the grievances of marginalised Indians. (So statements 1 and 2 are correct).

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who among the following established the Public Works Department (PWD) in India? [GEOSCIENTISTS 2022]
solution:
(A) Lord Dalhousie
Lord Dalhousie (1848 - 1856): Doctrine of Lapse (1848). 1st Railway Line - from Bombay to Thane (34 km). Widow Remarriage Act (1856). Wood's Dispatch (1854) - Magna Carta of Indian Education. Founded Public Works Department (PWD) in 1854. Postal Act, telegraph lines spread across India.

subject: Modern History
chapter_name: PARTITION OF BENGAL AND SWADESHI MOVEMENT
question_text: The Partition of Bengal took place in ________. [SSC GD 06/03/2024]
solution:
(A) 1905
Partition of Bengal was announced by Curzon in July 1905 and came into force in October 1905. Bengal was divided into two parts: West Bengal (Hindu majority) - West Bengal, Odisha, Bihar. East Bengal (Muslim majority) - Assam and Bangladesh.

subject: Modern History
chapter_name: PARTITION OF BENGAL AND SWADESHI MOVEMENT
question_text: In which amongst the following acts was Delhi declared the capital of British India in place of Calcutta? [SSC CPO 27/06/2024]
solution:
(A) Morley-Minto Act, 1909
Morley - Minto Act 1909 declared Delhi as the Capital replacing Calcutta. Announced by King George V during third Delhi Durbar in 1911. Delhi officially became capital in 1912. Act is also known as Indian Councils Act 1909.

subject: Modern History
chapter_name: PARTITION OF BENGAL AND SWADESHI MOVEMENT
question_text: What is the official reason given by Britishers after partition of Bengal in 1905? [SSC MTS 08/08/2023]
solution:
(D) Administrative Convenience
The British gave reason of administrative convenience for Partition of Bengal. It constituted 1/4th of total British India. It was the center of Nationalistic activities and British wanted to weaken it. So Britishers created division among Hindu and Muslims.

subject: Modern History
chapter_name: PARTITION OF BENGAL AND SWADESHI MOVEMENT
question_text: By what name did Vinayak Savarkar form a secret organization of revolutionaries in the year 1904? [SSC CHSL 11/08/2023]
solution:
(C) Abhinav Bharat
Abhinav Bharat Society founded by V.D. Savarkar in 1904. It was a secret organization of revolutionaries. Also known as 'Young India Society'. Hindustan Republican Association (1924) in Kanpur. Naujawan Bharat Sabha (1926) Lahore. All India Forward Block (1939) in West Bengal.

subject: Modern History
chapter_name: PARTITION OF BENGAL AND SWADESHI MOVEMENT
question_text: Who among the following stated in 1902, India was not ruled for its benefit, but rather for the benefit of its conquerors? [SSC CPO 28/06/2024]
solution:
(B) Annie Besant
Annie Besant had written the 'India was not ruled for its benefit, but rather for the benefit of its conquerors' in 1902. During World War I, Besant declared 'England's need is India's opportunity'. She was the editor of New India newspaper.

subject: Modern History
chapter_name: PARTITION OF BENGAL AND SWADESHI MOVEMENT
question_text: Before the Partition of Bengal in 1905, Bengal was the biggest province of British India. It consisted of parts of which of the following states? [SSC CHSL 20/03/2023]
solution:
(C) Bihar and Orissa
Bengal was the biggest province of British India and covered 1/4th of its part. It consisted of parts of Bihar, Orissa, West Bengal, Assam, Bangladesh. Before partition of Bengal, the population of Bengal province was around 78.5 million.

subject: Modern History
chapter_name: PARTITION OF BENGAL AND SWADESHI MOVEMENT
question_text: Who among the following founded the Servants of India Society in 1905 with the help of MG Ranade? [SSC MTS 30/10/2024]
solution:
(A) Gopal Krishna Gokhale
Servants of India Society (1905) was founded in Pune by Gopal Krishna Gokhale with assistance of M.G. Ranade. G.K. Gokhale was the president of Banaras Session (1905) of Indian National Congress. In this session, INC took the call of Swadeshi and Boycott movement.

subject: Modern History
chapter_name: PARTITION OF BENGAL AND SWADESHI MOVEMENT
question_text: Which of the following parties supported the move for partition of Bengal? [SSC CGL 18/04/2022]
solution:
(C) All India Muslim League
All India Muslim League was founded in Dec 1906 in Dhaka. Founded by Nawab Salimullah, Aga Khan III, Syed Amir Ali and others. First President - Aga Khan. Muslim League supported the Bengal Partition (1905). Supported separate electorate for Muslims during Morley - Minto Reforms (1909). In 1940, Lahore resolution, it demanded independent state (Pakistan) for Muslims.

subject: Modern History
chapter_name: PARTITION OF BENGAL AND SWADESHI MOVEMENT
question_text: In 1908, ________ and Prafulla Chaki threw a bomb at a carriage that was occupied, they believed, by Kingsford, the unpopular judge of Muzaffarpur. [SSC CGL 19/07/2023]
solution:
(B) Khudiram Bose
In 1908, Alipore Bomb Conspiracy or Manicktola Bomb Conspiracy. Khudiram Bose and Prafulla Chaki attempted murder of a judge Kingsford but mistakenly killed two women. Kanailal Dutta killed the witness who saw them throwing the Bomb. Aurobindo Ghosh was arrested and charged with 'waging war against the King'. Prafulla Chaki committed suicide. Khudiram Bose was hanged on 11 August 1908 in Muzaffarpur Jail.

subject: Modern History
chapter_name: PARTITION OF BENGAL AND SWADESHI MOVEMENT
question_text: Moved by the Swadeshi Movement, ________ painted his famous image of Bharat Mata portraying her as an ascetic figure. [SSC MTS 05/10/2021]
solution:
(B) Abanindranath Tagore
Abanindranath Tagore painted image of Bharat Mata in 1905 as symbol of nationalism. He was principal artist of Indian School of Oriental Art in 1907. Satyendranath Tagore, first Indian to qualify Indian Civil Services (ICS) in 1863.

subject: Modern History
chapter_name: PARTITION OF BENGAL AND SWADESHI MOVEMENT
question_text: What was the Swadeshi Movement called in the state of Andhra Pradesh? [SSC MTS 12/09/2023]
solution:
(D) Vande Mataram Movement
Swadeshi Movement (1905) also known as Vande Mataram Movement in Andhra Pradesh, started there in 1907. Boycott of foreign goods and promotion of local goods. Bipin Chandra Pal influenced Vande Mataram movement during his visit to Vijayawada and Masulipatnam in 1907.

subject: Modern History
chapter_name: PARTITION OF BENGAL AND SWADESHI MOVEMENT
question_text: At which of the following places was the formal proclamation of the Swadeshi movement made on 7 August 1905? [SSC CPO 09/11/2022]
solution:
(B) Calcutta Town Hall
The Swadeshi Movement's formal proclamation was made on August 7, 1905, at Calcutta Town hall. Boycott resolution was passed. It was a response after Bengal Partition initiated by Indian National Congress.

subject: Modern History
chapter_name: PARTITION OF BENGAL AND SWADESHI MOVEMENT
question_text: Sohan Singh Bhakna and Har Dayal were primarily associated with which of the following organisations? [SSC CGL 17/07/2023]
solution:
(A) Ghadar Party
Ghadar Party (1913) was formed by Indian immigrants in San Francisco (USA). It was founded by Lala Hardayal, Sohan Singh Bhakna, Barkatullah, Parmanand, Kartar Singh. Also published 'Ghadar Newspaper'. They intended to bring a revolt in India. Defense of India Act 1915 was brought in to suppress the Ghadar Party.

subject: Modern History
chapter_name: PARTITION OF BENGAL AND SWADESHI MOVEMENT
question_text: Abhinav Bharat Society was a secret society of revolutionaries. It was established in ________. [SSC CGL 09/12/2022]
solution:
(A) Bombay Presidency
Abhinav Bharat Society (1904) by V.D. Savarkar with his brother in 1904 at Bombay Presidency. Savarkar Brothers, V.D. Savarkar and Ganesh Savarkar organized Mitra Mela in 1899 and merged it with Abhinav Bharat Society in 1904.

subject: Modern History
chapter_name: PARTITION OF BENGAL AND SWADESHI MOVEMENT
question_text: Sachindra Nath Sanyal established a branch of Anushilan Samiti at Patna in which of the following years? [SSC JE 06/06/2024]
solution:
(A) 1913
Anushilan Samiti was established in 1902 in Bengal. Its branch was established by Sachindra Nath Sanyal in Patna (Bihar) in 1913. It was a secret organization that aimed at Indian Independence through armed struggle. Sanyal wrote 'Bandi Jeevan' while in prison.

subject: Modern History
chapter_name: PARTITION OF BENGAL AND SWADESHI MOVEMENT
question_text: Who among the following revolutionaries was associated with Swadesh Bandhav Samiti? [SSC CGL 13/12/2022]
solution:
(D) Ashwini Kumar Dutta
Swadeshi Bandhab Samiti was founded in August 1905 by Ashwini Kumar Dutta in Barisal (Bangladesh). Set up to propagate the Swadeshi Movement and led Muslim peasants of Barisal in their protest. British banned this samiti in 1908.

subject: Modern History
chapter_name: PARTITION OF BENGAL AND SWADESHI MOVEMENT
question_text: The Anushilan Samiti was active in which part of the Indian subcontinent in the 20th century? [SSC CPO 11/11/2022]
solution:
(B) Bengal
The Anushilan Samiti was founded in Calcutta (Bengal) by Satish Chandra Basu, Jatindranath Banerjee, Barindra Kumar Ghosh. It was influenced by Sri Aurobindo (Aurobindo Ghosh). Weekly newspaper 'Yugantar' was a means for Anushilan Samiti, through which they can express their ideas.

subject: Modern History
chapter_name: PARTITION OF BENGAL AND SWADESHI MOVEMENT
question_text: Which of the following organisations was established in 1906? [SSC JE 07/06/2024]
solution:
(B) All India Muslim League
All India Muslim League (1906) in Dhaka by Nawab Salimullah. Akhil Bharat Hindu Mahasabha (1915) in Kumbh Mela, Haridwar by Madan Mohan Malviya. Servants of India Society (1905) by GK Gokhale. Parsi Religious Reform Association (1851) by Furdunji Naoroji and S.S. Bengalee.

subject: Modern History
chapter_name: PARTITION OF BENGAL AND SWADESHI MOVEMENT
question_text: Which of the following revolutionists was associated with the Anushilan Samiti? [SSC CPO 29/06/2024]
solution:
(D) Jatindra Mohan Sengupta
Jatindra Mohan Sengupta was associated with Anushilan Samiti (1902). He was a lawyer, defended freedom fighters in Court. He served as the Mayor of Calcutta in 1931. Got title 'Deshpriya'.

subject: Modern History
chapter_name: PARTITION OF BENGAL AND SWADESHI MOVEMENT
question_text: At which of the following jails was Khudiram Bose sent to the gallows? [SSC CGL 20/04/2022]
solution:
(C) Muzaffarpur
Khudiram Bose and Prafulla Chaki threw a bomb at the carnage of judge, Kingsford, in Muzaffarpur in 1908. Prafulla Chaki shot himself. Trial was held against Khudiram Bose and was hanged in Muzaffarpur jail. The whole Anushilan group was arrested including Ghosh brothers.

subject: Modern History
chapter_name: PARTITION OF BENGAL AND SWADESHI MOVEMENT
question_text: Editor of a Marathi newspaper "Kesari" was: [SSC MTS 17/05/2023]
solution:
(D) Bal Gangadhar Tilak
Bal Gangadhar Tilak: Newspaper published - Mahratta (In English) and Kesari (in Marathi). Established Deccan Education society (1884). Wrote - The Arctic Home in the Vedas in 1903, Geeta Rahasya, The Orion. Bipin Chandra Pal wrote books - Indian Nationalism, The Soul of India, Memories of Life and Times. Lala Lajpat Rai wrote books - Unhappy India, Young India, England's debt to India, The story of My Deportation.

subject: Modern History
chapter_name: PARTITION OF BENGAL AND SWADESHI MOVEMENT
question_text: Who among the following Indian revolutionaries conceived an armed insurrection against the British in cooperation with Germany? [SSC MTS 14/10/2021]
solution:
(D) Bagha Jatin
Bagha Jatin also known as 'Jatindranath Mukherjee' was a leader of Jugantar (Yugantar) revolutionary group. He conceived an armed insurrection against British rule with German cooperation. Planned to import German arms in India through sea route. This plan was called 'German Plot' or the 'Zimmerman Plan'.

subject: Modern History
chapter_name: PARTITION OF BENGAL AND SWADESHI MOVEMENT
question_text: The split in Indian National Congress occurred in ________. [SSC MTS 17/05/2023]
solution:
(D) 1907
Surat Session (1907), President - Rash Behari Bose. Indian National Congress split in two groups, Moderates (Naram Dal) and Extremists (Garam Dal). Moderates - SN Banerjee, KK Mitra, GK Gokhale. Expressed views through petitions, newspapers and pamphlets. Extremists - Lala Lajpat Rai, Bal Gangadhar Tilak, Bipin Chandra Pal, Aurobindo Ghosh. Believed in direct political action. Idea of Swaraj. Ready to follow radical approach.

subject: Modern History
chapter_name: PARTITION OF BENGAL AND SWADESHI MOVEMENT
question_text: The Paris Indian Society was an Indian nationalist organisation founded in ________. [SSC CGL 06/12/2022]
solution:
(B) 1905
Indian Home Rule Society - India House was started in London in 1905 by Shyamji Krishnavarma. Started journal - The Indian Sociologist. Members - Savarkar and Hardayal. The Paris India Society (1905) established as its branch in Paris by Madam Bhikaji Cama, Burjorji Godrej and S.R. Rana. Madam Bhikaji Cama - 1st Indian to hoist flag on foreign soil in Stuttgart, Germany in 1907.

subject: Modern History
chapter_name: PARTITION OF BENGAL AND SWADESHI MOVEMENT
question_text: The claim for 'Swarajya' or self-government within the British Empire, on the model of self-governing colonies like Australia and Canada, was put forward from the Congress platform in 1905 by: [SSC CPO 10/11/2022]
solution:
(B) Dadabhai Naoroji
The Claim of 'Swarajya' or self-government within British Empire, like Australia and New Zealand was put forward by Dadabhai Naoroji in Calcutta Session (1906) under his presidency. Four resolutions in this session - Swaraj as goal was adopted, Boycott Resolution, Swadeshi, National Education. G. Subramanya Iyer moved the first resolution.

subject: Modern History
chapter_name: PARTITION OF BENGAL AND SWADESHI MOVEMENT
question_text: The Home Rule League Movement of Bal Gangadhar Tilak had its base across Karnataka and Maharashtra, but excluding which of the following? [SSC MTS 14/11/2024]
solution:
(D) Bombay
Bal Gangadhar Tilak set up his Home Rule League in April 1916. Headquarter - Pune. First Home Rule meeting held at Belgaum. Centre - Maharashtra (excluding Bombay city), Karnataka, Central Provinces and Berar. It had 6 branches. After Tilak's death in 1920, his Home Rule league was renamed as 'Swarajya Sabha' by M K Gandhi.

subject: Modern History
chapter_name: PARTITION OF BENGAL AND SWADESHI MOVEMENT
question_text: Which of the famous personalities belonged to the extremist phase of modern history? [SSC MTS 10/05/2023]
solution:
(D) Bal Gangadhar Tilak
Extremism increased during the Bengal Partition in 1905. Bal Gangadhar Tilak - 'Lokmanya Tilak'. Organized Ganpati and Shivaji festivals to attract and mobilize the youth in Maharashtra. 'Father of Indian Unrest' by Valentine Chirol, in his book 'Indian Unrest'.

subject: Modern History
chapter_name: PARTITION OF BENGAL AND SWADESHI MOVEMENT
question_text: The Swadeshi Movement started in India during ________. [SSC CPO 12/03/2019]
solution:
(A) Anti-Bengal Partition agitation
The Swadeshi Movement in India started in 1905 as response to Bengal Partition by Curzon in 1905. It was launched on August 7, 1905 at Calcutta Town Hall. INC Banaras Session (1905) adopted the resolution on Swadeshi movement.

subject: Modern History
chapter_name: PARTITION OF BENGAL AND SWADESHI MOVEMENT
question_text: The partition of ________ was annulled in 1911. [SSC MTS 04/11/2024]
solution:
(D) Bengal
Partition of Bengal (1905) was announced by Lord Curzon into East Bengal and West Bengal. It was annulled by Hardinge II (Viceroy) in 1911. Hardinge II organized 3rd Delhi Durbar in 1911 for Coronation of King George V. Capital shifted from Calcutta to Delhi in 1911.

subject: Modern History
chapter_name: PARTITION OF BENGAL AND SWADESHI MOVEMENT
question_text: Which of the following organisations, divided into two branches, was established by Lokmanya Bal Gangadhar Tilak and Annie Besant? [SSC CGL 11/09/2024]
solution:
(B) Home Rule League
Home Rule Movement (1916) was India's response to the First World War in a more effective way. Two Home Rule Leagues were made one by Bal Gangadhar Tilak and other by Annie Besant. B.G. Tilak's league formed in April 1916 in Belgaum. Headquarter - Pune. Annie Besant's league formed in September 1916 in Madras. HQ - Madras and Bombay.

subject: Modern History
chapter_name: PARTITION OF BENGAL AND SWADESHI MOVEMENT
question_text: In 1910, The United India House at Seattle, USA, was set up by ________. [SSC CGL 25/09/2024]

options.c: Tarak Nath Das and GD Kumar

solution:
(C) Tarak Nath Das and GD Kumar
In 1910, Tarak Nath Das and GD Kumar established United India House at Seattle (USA). Taraknath Das started journal Free Hindustan in 1908 in Vancouver. G D Kumar set up Swadesh Sewak Home in Vancouver and published a Gurmukhi monthly called Swadesh Sewak in 1909.

subject: Modern History
chapter_name: PARTITION OF BENGAL AND SWADESHI MOVEMENT
question_text: What is the name of the famous British social reformer known for founding the Indian Home Rule League in 1916? [SSC CHSL 11/07/2024]
solution:
(B) Annie Besant
Annie Besant launched All India Home Rule League in September 1916 in Madras and covered India including Bombay City. It had over 200 branches. Wrote 2 magazines, New India and Common Weal. Became INC President in Calcutta Session (1917). She laid the foundation of Central Hindu College in 1898 in Banaras.

subject: Modern History
chapter_name: PARTITION OF BENGAL AND SWADESHI MOVEMENT
question_text: Who among the following was the first principal of the Bengal National College started in 1906? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 11/12/2024]
solution:
(A) Aurobindo Ghosh
Bengal National College started in 1906 with Aurobindo Ghosh as its first Principal. 'Sri Aurobindo' works - The Life Divine, Savitri: A legend and a Symbol, The Synthesis of Yoga. He wrote Essays of Gita, The Human Cycle, The Ideal of Human Unity. Was editor of English newspaper Bande Mataram, which was founded by Bipin Chandra Pal in Calcutta in 1905.

subject: Modern History
chapter_name: PARTITION OF BENGAL AND SWADESHI MOVEMENT
question_text: The Arms Act which disallowed Indians from having arms was passed in ________. [SSC CGL 03/12/2022]
solution:
(A) 1878
Viceroy Lytton (1876 - 1880) passed the Arms Act in 1878. It stopped Indians from having rifles and pistols. To prevent any uprising like 1857. British and Europeans were exempted from this law. Lytton also passed Vernacular Press Act (1878).

subject: Modern History
chapter_name: PARTITION OF BENGAL AND SWADESHI MOVEMENT
question_text: The provision for separate electorate for Muslims was given in: [CDS I 2022]
solution:
(A) Morley-Minto Reforms, 1909
Morley-Minto Reforms 1909 also known as 'Indian Council Act of 1909'. Minto was Viceroy of India from 1905 - 1910. Morley was Secretary of the State. Brought separate electorate for Muslims. Minto is called the 'Father of Communalism' in India. For the first time, one Indian was allowed in Viceroy's executive council, Satyendra Prasanna Sinha was the first.

subject: Modern History
chapter_name: PARTITION OF BENGAL AND SWADESHI MOVEMENT
question_text:
The year 1916 is important in Indian history for: [CDS I 2022]
1. Lucknow Session of the Congress
2. Congress-League Pact
3. Formation of Home Rule League
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
solution:
(D) 1, 2 and 3
The year 1916 is highly important for Lucknow Session of Congress (where moderates and extremists merged under Ambika Charan Majumdar's presidency), the signing of the historic Congress-League Pact (Lucknow Pact) to form a joint political front, and the launching of the Home Rule League movement by Bal Gangadhar Tilak and Annie Besant to demand self-government.

subject: Modern History
chapter_name: PARTITION OF BENGAL AND SWADESHI MOVEMENT
question_text: The headquarters of Ghadar Movement/Party were at: [CDS I 2022]
solution:
(A) San Francisco
Ghadar Party was established in 1913 by Indian expatriates, primarily in the US and Canada. The headquarters of the Ghadar Movement was established at Yugantar Ashram in San Francisco, California. Key leaders included Lala Hardayal, Sohan Singh Bhakna, and Kartar Singh Sarabha.

subject: Modern History
chapter_name: PARTITION OF BENGAL AND SWADESHI MOVEMENT
question_text:
Consider the following freedom fighters: [IAS 2022]
1. Barindra Kumar Ghosh
2. Jogesh Chandra Chatterjee
3. Rash Behari Bose
Who of the above was/were actively associated with the Ghadar Party?
solution:
(D) 3 only
Barindra Kumar Ghosh was a prominent revolutionary leader of Bengal associated with Anushilan Samiti and Jugantar. Jogesh Chandra Chatterjee was a leader of the Anushilan Samiti and Hindustan Republican Association (HRA). Rash Behari Bose was actively associated with the Ghadar Party and planned the Pan-India armed uprising (Ghadar Mutiny) of February 1915 along with Ghadar revolutionaries.

subject: Modern History
chapter_name: PARTITION OF BENGAL AND SWADESHI MOVEMENT
question_text: Who among the following was the Viceroy of India in 1905, when the Partition of Bengal was announced? [GEOSCIENTISTS 2023]
solution:
(C) Lord Curzon
Lord Curzon was the Viceroy of India from 1899 to 1905. He announced the partition of Bengal in July 1905 as a measure to split the nationalistic movement in Bengal, leading to the Swadeshi and Boycott movements. He also passed the Calcutta Corporation Act (1899) and established the University Commission (1902).

subject: Modern History
question_text: Vallabhbhai Patel and ________ helped Gandhi in Kheda by organising his tour of the villages and urging the peasants to stand firm against the government. [SSC CPO 03/10/2023]
solution:
(C) Indulal Yajnik
Kheda Satyagraha (1918) led by Mahatma Gandhi in Gujarat. In support of peasants who were not able to pay land tax due to failure of crops. Gandhi's first Non-Cooperation Movement. Vallabhbhai Patel and Indulal Yajnik helped Gandhi with other local leaders.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who among the following designed a campaign that came to be known as the 'Rowlatt Satyagraha'? [SSC CPO 04/10/2023]
solution:
(A) Mahatma Gandhi
Rowlatt Act (1919) by Sydney Rowlatt (British judge), also known as Anarchial and Revolutionary Crimes Act. Gandhi organised Rowlatt Satyagraha in April, 1919 against Rowlatt Act and called it Black Act. Jinnah, Madan Mohan Malviya, Mazhar-ul-Haq also resigned in protest of this act.

subject: Modern History
question_text: On whose advice did Gandhi spend a year traveling around British India to know the people of India? [SSC MTS 14/09/2023]
solution:
(D) Gopal Krishna Gokhale
Gandhi returned from South Africa in 1915. Gokhale advised him to travel across India to understand the real situations of the country. Gandhi considered Gopal Krishna Gokhale as his political guru. Gandhi's autobiography 'The Story of My Experiments with Truth'.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which of the following events occurred in 1919? [SSC MTS 14/09/2023]

options.b: Representation of the People Act was passed
options.c: Suppression of Fraud and Dacoity Act passed

solution:
(D) Satyagraha against Rowlatt Act
Renewal of Charter - every 20 years from 1793 to 1853. Representation of People act was passed in 1918. Satyagraha against Rowlatt Satyagraha was organised in 1919 by Gandhi in protest of Rowlatt Act Session (1919) which allowed the British to arrest Indians without trial for 2 years.

subject: Modern History
question_text: In which year was Mohandas Karamchand Gandhi born? [SSC CHSL 10/08/2023]
solution:
(B) 1869
Mohandas Karamchand Gandhi (1869 - 1948). Born on 2nd October 1869 in Porbandar, Gujarat. Father - Karamchand Gandhi, Mother - Putli Bai, Wife - Kasturba Gandhi. Title 'Mahatma' was given by Rabindranath Tagore. Worked as Lawyer in South Africa. President of Belgaum Session (1924) of Congress. Gandhi setup Natal Indian Congress in South Africa.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who was the founder of Harijan Sevak Sangh founded in 1932? [SSC CGL 17/07/2023]
solution:
(B) Mahatma Gandhi
The Harijan Sevak Sangh, founded in 1932 by Mahatma Gandhi. For the upliftment of Harijans (Dalits) and eradicate untouchability in India. Original name - All India Anti-Untouchability League. Gandhi published journal 'Harijan'.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who among the following leaders led the Bardoli Satyagraha? [SSC CGL 18/07/2023]
solution:
(D) Vallabhbhai Patel
Bardoli Satyagraha (1928) was led by Vallabhbhai Patel. Bardoli village in Surat. British government increased land revenue by 22%. Non-violent protest by farmers against British. Women participants of Bardoli Satyagraha gave title of 'Sardar' to Vallabhbhai Patel in 1928.

subject: Modern History
question_text: The famous scientist, Albert Einstein was inspired by whose idea on non-violence? [SSC CGL 18/07/2023]
solution:
(A) Mahatma Gandhi
Albert Einstein was inspired by Gandhi's philosophy of non-violence. Einstein about Gandhi - 'Generations to come will scarce believe that such a one as this ever in flesh and blood walked upon this earth'. Einstein gave 'Theory of Relativity'. Won Nobel Prize in Physics in 1921 for his explanation of Photoelectric Effect.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Primarily under whose persuasion did Mahatma Gandhi visit Champaran to understand the problems of peasants? [SSC CGL 26/07/2023]
solution:
(D) Rajkumar Shukla
Rajkumar Shukla, peasant leader from Champaran (Bihar) had gone to Lucknow to invited Gandhi to Champaran to understand the problems of peasants. Gandhi led the successful Champaran Satyagraha in 1917, it was his first major active participation in India.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who among the following returned his/her medal of Kaisar-i-Hind in 1920? [SSC CGL 27/06/2023]
solution:
(D) Mahatma Gandhi
