Mahatma Gandhi returned his title of 'Kaisar-i-Hind' given by Hardinge II in 1915 for Gandhi's services during Boer War (1899 - 1902) and work in South Africa. Gandhi returned it as a protest of Jallianwala Bagh Massacre (1919) by Britishers.

subject: Modern History
question_text: In 1895, Mahatma Gandhi, along with other Indians has established ________ to fight against racial discrimination. [SSC MTS 03/05/2023]
solution:
(C) Natal Congress
Mahatma Gandhi set up Natal Indian Congress in 1895 in South Africa against the racial discrimination faced by Indian and Africans. Dev Samaj (1887) was established by Pandit Shiv Narayan Agnihotri in Lahore. Brahmo Samaj (1828) was established by Raja Ram Mohan Roy in Calcutta. Sabarmati Ashram was founded by Gandhi in 1915 in Gujarat.

subject: Modern History
question_text: In 1918, Mahatma Gandhi successfully led a strike of ________. [SSC MTS 09/05/2023]
solution:
(C) Mill workers
Ahmedabad Mill Strike (1918): Gandhi's first Hunger Strike. Invited by Anusuya Sarabhai. Workers of Mills in Ahmedabad fought for economic injustice when the mill owners discontinued their Plague Bonuses. Workers demanded 50% hikes due to inflation. After the Satyagraha workers got 35% hike.

subject: Modern History
question_text: In which Indian movement, were the issues faced by Indigo planters, first brought to light in 1917? [SSC MTS 15/06/2023]
solution:
(A) Champaran Satyagraha
Champaran Satyagraha (1917): Gandhi was invited by Rajkumar Shukla. 1st Civil Disobedience movement. Regarding the Tinkathiya system, farmers of Champaran were forced to cultivate Indigo in 3/20th part of land. It was a successful Event. Rajendra Prasad, Mazhar-ul-Haq, J.B. Kriplani were also involved in Champaran Satyagraha.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Mahatma Gandhi called off the Non-cooperation Movement in February 1922 after which incident? [SSC MTS 19/06/2023]
solution:
(D) Chauri Chaura Incident
Non-Cooperation Movement was launched on August 1, 1920 by Mr. Gandhi. It was called off by Gandhi in 1922 due to Chauri Chaura incident. Chauri Chaura Incident, on 4 Feb 1922 in Chauri Chaura village in Gorakhpur (United Province), 22 policemen died due to a local protest that caused fire in the police station.

subject: Modern History
question_text: In Kheda, Gujarat, Patidar peasants organised non-violent campaigns against which of the following demand of the British? [SSC CHSL 14/03/2023]
solution:
(C) High land revenue
In Kheda, Patidar peasants organised campaigns against high land revenue by Britishers. Kheda Satyagraha was organised in 1918 led by Gandhi, Vallabhbhai Patel, Indulal Yagnik, Shankarlal Banker, Mohanlal Pandya, Mahadev Desai etc. It was the 1st Non-Cooperation movement.

subject: Modern History
question_text: When was the Champaran Agrarian Act passed? [SSC CHSL 20/03/2023]
solution:
(C) 1918
Champaran Satyagraha (1917) was successful under Gandhi against forced indigo cultivation. The Champaran Agrarian Act was passed in 1918, it abolished Tinkathiya system and abolished Abwab Tax. The ryots who paid Tawan (tax) to factories received one-fourth of it back.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Gandhiji, aged ________, arrived in India in 1915 from South Africa. [SSC CHSL 21/03/2023]
solution:
(B) 46
Mahatma Gandhi was born on 2 Oct 1869 and arrived in India from South Africa on 9 Jan 1915 (Pravasiya Diwas), aged 46 years. He set up Natal Indian Congress in South Africa in 1894. Set up Phoenix Farm (1904), Tolstoy Farm (1910) and developed new technique of Satyagraha.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which of the following Indian freedom fighter said that "Be the change you wish to see in the world"? [SSC CGL 03/12/2022]
solution:
(D) Mahatma Gandhi
Mahatma Gandhi said 'Be the change you wish to see in the world'. Other quotes of Gandhi - 'An eye for an eye will make the whole world blind', 'Live as if you were to die tomorrow. Learn as if you were to live forever', 'Freedom is not worth having if it does not include the freedom to make mistakes'.

subject: Modern History
question_text: The All-India Khilafat Conference in November 1919 took place at: [SSC CPO 10/11/2022]
solution:
(B) Delhi
All-India Khilafat Conference in November 1919 took place at Delhi. Head - Gandhi (first president). It was opposed by Congress and B G Tilak. Protest against the dismantling of Ottoman Caliphate by the British after World War I.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which of the following events occurred as an after-effect of the Jallianwala Bagh Massacre (13th April 1919)? [SSC MTS 08/07/2022]

options.a: Hunger strike to settle disputes between the mill owners and the workers.
options.b: Individual princes could sign a separate agreement with the British.
options.c: Hunter Commission was appointed by the British Government.
options.d: Popularisation of Charkha and Khadi and Jail Bharo by Congress volunteers.

solution:
(C) Hunter Commission was appointed by the British Government
Jallianwala Bagh Massacre took place in Amritsar on 13 April 1919 by General Reginald Dyer. Hunter Commission was setup by British to investigate the actions of Dyer, Headed by William Hunter. Also known as Disorders Inquiry Committee. Consisted 4 British and 3 Indian members. British elites started 'Morning Post' (fund collection) for Dyer. Rudyard Kipling also funded this.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who among the following was a leader of the Khilafat Movement? [SSC MTS 12/07/2022]
solution:
(C) Maulana Abul Kalam Azad
Khilafat movement (1919) : Maulana Abul Kalam Azad used his newspaper Al-Hilal and supported Khilafat Movement. Led by Mohammad Ali, Shaukat Ali, Hakim Ajmal Khan and Maulana Abul Kalam Azad. Merged with Non-Cooperation movement in 1920. Supported by Gandhi.

subject: Modern History
question_text: In which year did Mahatma Gandhi first introduce a nation wide satyagraha in India? [SSC MTS 15/07/2022]
solution:
(C) 1919
Mahatma Gandhi introduced first nation wide Satyagraha in India in 1919 against the Rowlatt Act (1919), brought by Sydney Rowlatt. It states, activists should be imprisoned without trial for 2 years. Rowlatt Act was repealed in 1922 by the British government.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which of the following is NOT a reason for slowing down the Non-cooperation Movement? [SSC MTS 19/07/2022]

options.a: Due to the absence of alternative Indian Institution, boycott of British Institutions posed a problem.
options.b: Leaders of the movement shifted their focus towards domestic work thus created a void.
options.c: Many lawyers resigned but then absence of income for a longer period of time compelled them to rejoin.
options.d: Khadi cloth was often more expensive than mass-produced mill cloth and poor people could not afford to buy it.

solution:
(B) Leaders of the movement shifted their focus towards domestic work thus created a void
Non-Cooperation Movement was started by Gandhi in 1920. Calls for boycott of British institutions, it caused problems due to less Indian institutions. Indians in British government resigned but rejoined it due to lack of income. Gandhi advocated for boycott of foreign goods and promoted Khadi but it was expensive and unaffordable for the poor.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which Newspaper was founded by Mahatma Gandhi in South Africa in 1903? [SSC MTS 13/10/2021]
solution:
(B) Indian Opinion
Mahatma Gandhi published 'Indian Opinion' in 1903 in South Africa. Gandhi was involved in Satyagraha against Registration of Marriage Act and led campaigns against restriction of Indian Migration, when Hindu marriages were not recognised in South Africa. Also started Hind Swaraj (1909), Young India (1919) and Navajivan (1919).

subject: Modern History
question_text: When was the Jallianwala Bagh massacre brought into action by the Britishers in Amritsar? [SSC MTS 13/11/2024]
solution:
(B) 13 April, 1919
Jallianwala Bagh Massacre took place on 13th April 1919 on Baisakhi. In Amritsar, people gathered due to Baisakhi and arrest of Saifuddin Kitchlew and Dr. Satyapal. General Reginald Dyer closed the only exit and opened fire the crowd. Hunter Commission was setup to investigate the actions of Dyer.

subject: Modern History
question_text: With which of the following Government of India Acts did the Montagu-Chelmsford Reforms introduce Dyarchy in the provinces? [SSC CGL 24/07/2023]
solution:
(D) Government of India Act, 1919
Montagu Chelmsford Reforms also known as Government of India Act 1919 by Lord Chelmsford (Viceroy) and Edwin Montagu (Secretary). It extended separate electorate to Anglo Indians and Christians. Introduced Bicameral legislature at centre. Introduced Dyarchy at Provinces, where power was divided between Indian ministers and British officials.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which among the following Acts introduced the Diarchy, i.e., rule of two which means executive councillors and popular ministers? [SSC MTS 04/05/2023]
solution:
(C) Montague-Chelmsford Reform (Government of India Act), 1919
Montague Chelmsford Reforms (1919) - introduced 'Dyarchy' at provinces. Charter Act (1813) - ended trade monopoly of East India Company, allowed Christian missionaries in India. Government of India Act 1858 - ended company rule and established Crown rule. Indian Council Act (1861) - powers of Crown exercised by Secretary of State for India assisted by a council of fifteen members.

subject: Modern History
question_text: As a part of the Non-Cooperation Movement in 1922, the angry peasants had set ablaze the police station of which place? [SSC MTS 30/10/2024]
solution:
(D) Chauri Chaura
Kakori Incident (1925), by Hindustan Republican Association, Ram Prasad Bismil and Ashfaqulla Khan were hanged by British. Dandi March (Salt March) by Gandhi from Sabarmati Ashram to Dandi in 1930. Chauri Chaura Incident (1922) - angry peasants set ablaze the police station which killed 22 police officers in Gorakhpur (United Province).

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which Committee among the following was formed in Bombay in March 1919? [SSC MTS 27/10/2021]
solution:
(B) Khilafat Committee
Khilafat Committee - at Bombay in March 1919 by Muslim leaders Mohammad Ali, Shaukat Ali, Abul Kalam Azad. All India Khilafat Conference at Delhi in November 1919. Hunter Commission - October 1919. Sapru Committee - November 1944. Sargent Committee - 1944.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which of the following places is related to Gandhi's Satyagraha of the year 1917? [SSC CGL 10/09/2024]
solution:
(C) Champaran
Champaran Satyagraha (1917) - by Gandhi, against forced indigo cultivation. Bardoli Satyagraha (1928) by Vallabhbhai Patel, against tax hike by British. Kheda Satyagraha (1918) by Gandhi, against high land revenue. Ahmedabad Mill Strike (1918) by Gandhi, hike in wages of mill workers.

subject: Modern History
question_text: In which of the following cities did Mahatma Gandhi lead the peasant movement against the imposed indigo cultivation by the British planters? [SSC CGL 11/09/2024]
solution:
(C) Champaran
Champaran Satyagraha (1917): Gandhi was invited by Rajkumar Shukla. 1st Civil Disobedience movement. Regarding the Tinkathiya system, farmers of Champaran were forced to cultivate Indigo in 3/20th part of land. It was a successful Event. Rajendra Prasad, Mazhar-ul-Haq, J.B. Kriplani were also involved in Champaran Satyagraha.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which of the following events took place in the United Provinces in February, 1922? [SSC CGL 11/09/2024]
solution:
(C) Chauri Chaura
Chauri - Chaura incident (1922) - Gorakhpur (United Provinces). Khilafat Movement (1919) - started in Bombay. Kakori Incident (1925) - near Lucknow (United Province). Quit India movement (1942) - launched at Gowalia Tank Maidan in Bombay.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Due to which of the following events did Rabindranath Tagore return the title of 'Knighthood' to the British Government while expressing his anguish? [SSC CGL 19/09/2024]
solution:
(A) Jallianwala Bagh Massacre
After Jallianwala Bagh Massacre in 1919, Rabindranath Tagore returned his 'Knighthood' title, he got this title in 1905. Mahatma Gandhi returned the title of 'Kaisar-i-Hind'. Charan Paduka incident (1931) - 'Jallianwala Bagh of Madhya Pradesh'. British opened fire on peaceful protestors in Singhpur village in Chhatarpur (MP).

subject: Modern History
question_text: In which of the following years was VD Savarkar sent to Andaman Jail by the British? [SSC CGL 19/09/2024]
solution:
(A) 1911
V.D. Savarkar established Abhinav Bharat Society in 1904. Sent to Andaman Jail on 8 April 1911 under Nashik Conspiracy case. He was sent to Cellular Jail (Kala Pani). Sentenced to two life terms (50 years). Wrote 'Essentials of Hindutva' (1923), The Indian War of Independence 1857.

subject: Modern History
question_text: The Non-Cooperation Movement was called off after which of the following incidents? [SSC CGL 24/09/2024]

options.b: Outbreak of the First World War

solution:
(C) Chauri - Chaura incident
Non-Cooperation Movement (1920 - 1922) called off after Chauri - Chaura incident in 1922 in Gorakhpur, United Province. Komagata Maru incident (1914) - 376 Indian passengers (Sikhs) denied entry in Canada due to discriminatory immigration laws, they reached there by Japanese vessel S.S. Komagata Maru. This event was related to Ghadar Party.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which of the following Commissions was set up to investigate the incident of Jallianwala Bagh in 1919? [SSC MTS 14/11/2024]
solution:
(A) Hunter Commission
Hunter Commission (1919) - to investigate Jallianwala Bagh massacre. Sadler Commission (1917) - to examine the state of Calcutta University. Simon Commission (1927) - examine the Government of India Act of 1919. Peel Commission (1857) - to bring changes in military system after 1857 revolt.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Mahatma Gandhi Kashi Vidyapith was founded by Shiv Prasad Gupta and Bhagwan Das in the year ________. [SSC MTS 16/10/2024]
solution:
(C) 1921
Mahatma Gandhi Kashi Vidyapeeth was founded by Shiv Prasad Gupta and Bhagwan Das on 10 Feb 1921 during the Non-Cooperation movement. It was originally named Kashi Vidyapith. Bhagwan Das was awarded Bharat Ratna in 1955.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which of the following is NOT an option related to the non-cooperation movement launched by Mahatma Gandhi? [SSC PHASE XII 25/06/2024]

options.c: Signing the resolution at Lahore for Poorna Swaraj

solution:
(C) Signing the resolution at Lahore for Poorna Swaraj
Gandhi launched Non-Cooperation movement in 1920. Calls for boycott of foreign goods, distribution of Charkha for self-reliance. Promoted Khadi. Advocated for picketing of liquor shops to oppose British taxes. Movement was called off in 1922. Lahore Session (1929): President - Jawaharlal Nehru. Poorna Swaraj resolution was signed on 19 Dec 1929. Declared 26 January 1930 as India's Independence Day.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which British colonel is remembered as the Butcher of Amritsar for his role in the Jallianwala Bagh massacre in 1919? [SSC CHSL 11/07/2024]
solution:
(A) Reginald Dyer
General Reginald Dyer was responsible for Jallianwala Bagh Massacre in 1919. He closed the only exit and open fired at the peaceful crowd. Udham Singh killed Michael Francis O'Dwyer, the lieutenant governor of Punjab, who supported Reginald Dyer.

subject: Modern History
question_text: What was the name of the campaign led by the Ali brothers and Maulana Abul Kalam Azad to save the Ottoman Caliphate as a symbol of unity among the Muslim community? [SSC CHSL 09/07/2024]
solution:
(C) Khilafat Movement
Khilafat Movement (1919 - 1924): protest against removal of Khalifa (Caliphate) of Turkey (Ottoman Empire) from power by British after World War I. Led by Maulana Mohammad Ali, Shaukat Ali and Abul Kalam Azad. Maulana Hazrat Mohani coined the slogan 'Inquilab Zindabad'.

subject: Modern History
question_text: The Rowlatt Act of 1919 granted the British colonial government the power to arrest and detains Indians without trial. Who was the British Viceroy of India at that time? [SSC GD 27/02/2024]
solution:
(A) Lord Chelmsford
Lord Chelmsford : Viceroy (1916 - 1921), Government of India Act 1919, Montagu - Chelmsford Reforms, Rowlatt Act (1919), Jallianwala Bagh Massacre (1919). Lord Curzon (1899 - 1905), Partition of Bengal in 1905. Lord Dalhousie (1848 - 1856), brought Doctrine of Lapse (1848). Lord Irwin (1926 - 1931), signed Gandhi-Irwin pact in 1931.

subject: Modern History
question_text: The Jallianwala Bagh massacre, took place in consequence to the protest against the ________. [SSC CGL 20/02/2024]
solution:
(B) Rowlatt Act
Jallianwala Bagh Massacre took place in 1919, in consequence to the Rowlatt Act of 1919, which allowed the British to arrest and imprison Indians without trial. Pitt's India Act (1784), Regulating Act (1773), Government of India Act in 1858, 1915, 1919 and 1935.

subject: Modern History
question_text:
Consider the following statements about the Rowlatt Act of 1919:
1. It was passed by the Imperial Legislative Council.
2. Khilafat Committee was formed to oppose the Act.
3. While Gandhiji was arrested, the local leaders were allowed to continue the protest.
How many of the above statements is/are not correct? [CAPF 2024]
solution:
(A) One
Rowlatt Act (1919) or the Anarchial and Revolutionary Crimes Act of 1919, was passed by Imperial Legislative Council of British India. Khilafat Committee was formed to oppose the removal of Caliphate of Ottoman Empire from power. It was supported by Gandhi. While Gandhiji was arrested, the British tried to suppress the movement and no one was allowed to protest, local leaders were also arrested.

subject: Modern History
question_text: In 1911, who among the following introduced a bill in the Imperial Legislative Council for introduction of compulsory and free primary education in India? [CDS I 2021]
solution:
(D) Gopal Krishna Gokhale
Gopal Krishna Gokhale was the member of Imperial Legislative Council, in 1911 he introduced a bill for compulsory and free primary education. It is known as Gokhale's private Bill of 1911. He was president of Banaras Session of Congress in 1905. Established Servants of India Society in 1905.

subject: Modern History
question_text: The call for 'renunciation of (all) voluntary associations with the (British) Government' was given during: [CDS II 2021]
solution:
(A) Non-Cooperation Movement
Non-Cooperation Movement (1920 - 1922) call for renunciation of voluntary associations with British. It was endorsed in Khilafat Andolan. Initiated by Gandhi. Adopted by INC in Nagpur Session (1920) and Calcutta Session (1920). Resignation from jobs. Promotion of Swadeshi goods. 1 crore collected through Tilak Swaraj Fund (1921). Withdrawn after the Chauri Chaura incident in Gorakhpur (UP) in 1922.

subject: Modern History
question_text: In India, the first major public appearance of Mahatma Gandhi was in: [CDS II 2021]
solution:
(C) Inauguration of Banaras Hindu University (1916)
Gandhi returns India from South Africa in 1915. First major public appearance was at during inauguration of Banaras Hindu University (1916). It was founded by Madan Mohan Malviya. Champaran Satyagraha (1917) - first Satyagraha of Gandhi. Ahmedabad Mill Strike (1918) - first hunger strike. Kheda Satyagraha (1918) - first Non-Cooperation movement. Rowlatt Satyagraha (1919) - first all India strike.

subject: Modern History
question_text:
Arrange the following events chronologically starting from the earliest: [CDS I 2022]
1. Ahmedabad Mill Strike
2. Champaran Satyagraha
3. Foundation of the Satyagraha Sabha
4. Jallianwala Bagh Massacre
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
solution:
(B) 2, 1, 3, 4
Champaran Satyagraha (1917) by Gandhi against forced indigo cultivation by British, Ahmedabad Mill Strike (1918) by Gandhi for wages hike of workers, Foundation of Satyagraha Sabha by Gandhi in Feb, 1919 in response to Rowlatt Act. Jallianwala Bagh Massacre on 13 April 1919 by General Reginald Dyer.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who among the following did not belong to the group of 'No-Changers'? [CDS I 2022]
solution:
(B) Vithalbhai Patel
Non-Cooperation movement was officially suspended in the Congress Session in Bardoli in 1922. Congress split into 'No Changers' and 'Pro Changers'. No Changers supported Gandhi's non-cooperation policy without participating in legislative councils. Pro Changers wanted to enter legislative councils and obstruct British Policies. After Gaya Session (1922) of Congress, Pro Changers made All India Khilafat Swaraj Party in 1923 to enter councils. Pro Changers - C.R. Das, Motilal Nehru, Vithalbhai Patel, Ajmal Khan, Subhash Chandra Bose. No Changers - M.A. Ansari, C. Rajagopalachari, Rajendra Prasad, K.R. Iyengar, Vallabhbhai Patel.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who among the following was not a woman labour leader who organised workers in the strikes in the 1920s? [CDS II 2022]
solution:
(A) Sarala Devi Chaudhurani
Sarla Devi Chaudhurani worked for women's education, founded Bharat Stree Mahamandal in 1910 in Allahabad. Ushabai Dange involved in organising textile mill workers in Bombay and only female to participate in Jinnah Hall gathering in 1931. Prabhavati Devi, led scavengers strike in 1928 and jute strike in 1929. Anusuya Behn Sarabhai organised Ahmedabad mill strike in 1918 and invited Mahatma Gandhi. She formed Majdoor Mahajan Sangh.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which one among the following was not a part of the action programme of the Non-Cooperation Movement? [CDS I 2024]

options.a: The Congress organisation was to reach down to the village and the Mohalla level.
options.b: Boycott of government affiliated schools and colleges.
options.c: Taking control of the law and order machinery of the State by the Congress Working Committee.
options.d: Surrender of titles and honours given by the government.

solution:
(C) Taking control of the law and order machinery of the State by the Congress Working Committee
Non-Cooperation movement (1920 - 1922) was a non-violent movement by Gandhi. The Congress aimed to reach villages and mohallas to organise people. Boycott of government schools, colleges and British goods. Gandhi called for surrender of titles and honours given by British government. Gandhi surrendered his title - Kaisar-i-Hind after Jallianwala bagh massacre in 1919.

subject: Modern History
question_text: The distribution of charkhas on a wide scale among the people was decided by: [CDS I 2024]

options.c: Mahatma Gandhi at the start of Dandi March

solution:
(B) All India Congress Committee in 1921
The All India Congress Committee decided the distribution of Charkhas on a wide scale in 1921. Gandhi's plan to promote Swadeshi movement and self-reliance among Indians. Charkha and Khadi became the symbols of Indian independence movements.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which one of the following measures was not a consequence of the successful Champaran Satyagraha of 1917? [CBI 2023]

options.a: The Sharabeshi rent was reduced by 26 percent
options.c: The Thekedari system was allowed to continue as it was
options.d: The Tawan, a fine on peasants, was abolished

solution:
(C) The Thekedari system was allowed to continue as it was
The Champaran Satyagraha (1917) was successful under Gandhi. As a result, Champaran Agrarian Act was passed in 1918. The Sharabeshi rent was reduced by 26 percent. The Abwabs were declared illegal and Abwab tax also suspended. Tawan, a fine on peasants, was abolished and ryots received one-fourth of it back. The Thekedari system was criticised and changes were demanded.

subject: Modern History
question_text:
Consider the following events: [NDA I 2024]
1. Launch of Non-Cooperation Movement
2. All-India Khilafat Conference in Delhi
3. Passing of the Rowlatt Act
4. Jallianwala Bagh incident
Which one of the following is the correct chronological order of the given events (earliest to latest)?
solution:
(B) 3, 4, 2, 1
Rowlatt Act was passed in March 1919, it allowed British govt to arrest Indians without trial for 2 years. Jallianwala Bagh Massacre took place on 13 April 1919, General Dyer open fired on Indians in Amritsar. All-India Khilafat Conference in Delhi was held in November 1919, Gandhi was the President. Non-Cooperation Movement was launched on 1 August, 1920 by Gandhi.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Salt Satyagraha was associated with the: [SSC STENOGRAPHER 13/10/2023]
solution:
(A) Civil Disobedience Movement
Salt Satyagraha also known as Dandi March was led by Mahatma Gandhi in 1930 as a part of Civil Disobedience movement against the Salt tax. Gandhi marched 240 miles (385 km) with 78 delegates from Sabarmati Ashram (Ahmedabad) to Dandi (Navsari). It started on 12 March 1930 and ended on 6 April 1930.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which freedom fighter who involved in the Kakori Train Action in 1925, for which the British government condemned him to death? [SSC MTS 15/06/2023]
solution:
(A) Ram Prasad Bismil
Kakori Train Incident (1925) : members of Hindustan Republican Association robbed a train on 9 Aug 1925 at Kakori village, Lucknow. Participants - Ram Prasad Bismil, Ashfaqulla Khan, Rajendra Lahiri, Roshan Singh, Chandrashekhar Azad. They all were arrested and hanged to death in 1927 by the British except Chandrashekhar Azad.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which of the following revolutionary groups' members assassinated Assistant Superintendent of Police John Saunders in 1927? [SSC CGL 13/12/2022]
solution:
(B) Hindustan Republican Association
John Saunders was mistaken for James Scott and was shot dead by Bhagat Singh, Azad and Rajguru in 1927 in Lahore to avenge the death of Lala Lajpat Rai. Hindustan Republican Association was founded in 1924 in Kanpur by Ram Prasad Bismil, J.C. Chatterjee, Sachin Sanyal. Abhinav Bharat Secret Society - by V.D. Savarkar in 1904. Anushilan Samiti - in 1902 by S.C. Basu, Jatindranath Banerjee etc. Bhagat Singh, Sukhdev, Rajguru were arrested in Lahore Conspiracy case and were hanged to death on 23 March 1931, celebrated as Shaheed Diwas or Martyrs Day.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which of the following revolutionaries, who was arrested in Lahore conspiracy case by the British Government of India, was martyred in the hunger strike of 63 days in jail? [SSC MTS 30/09/2024]
solution:
(A) Jatindra Nath Das
Jatindra Nath Das was involved in Lahore Conspiracy Case with Bhagat Singh and other leaders. He began his hunger strike in Lahore Central Jail for better treatment of political prisoners and continued it for 63 days until his death.

subject: Modern History
question_text: In 1927, where was the conference against colonial oppression and imperialism, in which Jawaharlal Nehru participated on behalf of Indian National Congress, held? [SSC MTS 29/10/2024]
solution:
(B) Brussels
In 1927, the conference against colonial oppression and imperialism held in Brussels (Belgium). Jawaharlal Nehru represented Indian National Congress in this conference. Jawaharlal Nehru was the first and longest serving Prime Minister of India (1947 - 1964). He wrote - Discovery of India, Glimpses of World History, Letters from a father to his daughter.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who among the following signed the Poona pact with Mahatma Gandhi? [SSC CGL 20/07/2023]
solution:
(D) Bhimrao Ambedkar
Poona Pact was signed between Mahatma Gandhi and Bhimrao Ambedkar on 24 Sept 1932. It was signed by Madan Mohan Malviya on behalf of Gandhi. Gandhi had undertaken a fast unto death at Yerwada Jail, Poona against the Communal Award (1932) by Ramsay MacDonald.

subject: Modern History
question_text: When did depressed classes get the reserved seats in provincial and central legislative councils, but they were to be voted in by the general electorate? [SSC CHSL 15/03/2023]
solution:
(D) September 1932
In 1932, British PM Ramsay MacDonald announced the Communal Award, which provides separate electorates to Depressed Classes. Gandhi opposed it and began fast unto death in Yerwada Jail in Poona. Poona Pact was signed in Sept 1932 between Ambedkar and Gandhi it rejected separate electorate and Reserved seats were given to Depressed Classes, which was to be voted by General electorate.

subject: Modern History
question_text: In the ________, Sarojini Naidu persuaded Mahatma Gandhi to allow women to join the movement. [SSC CGL 08/12/2022]
solution:
(C) Salt Satyagraha
Mahatma Gandhi started Salt Satyagraha on 12 March 1930 from Sabarmati and reached Dandi on 6 April. Sarojini Naidu persuaded Gandhi to allow women join the movement. She led the Dharasana Satyagraha in 1930 after the arrest of Gandhi. Kheda Satyagraha (1918) was the first Non-Cooperation movement. Champaran Satyagraha (1917) was the first civil disobedience movement.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which of the following events took place in the year 1928? [SSC MTS 17/10/2024]
solution:
(C) Bardoli Satyagraha
Kakori Incident took place in Aug 1925 by the members of Hindustan Republican Association. Dandi March, led by Gandhi in 1930. Bardoli Satyagraha, led by Vallabhbhai Patel in 1928. Kheda Satyagraha, led by Mahatma Gandhi in 1918.

subject: Modern History
question_text: In which of the following years was the civil disobedience campaign completely Ceased? [SSC CGL 12/04/2022]
solution:
(B) 1934
The Civil Disobedience movement was formally launched by Gandhi on 6 April 1930 after breaking the Salt law during Dandi March. It was initially withdrawn in 1931 after Gandhi-Irwin pact but resumed after failure of 2nd Round Table Conference (1931) in 1932. In April 1934, Gandhi completely ceased this movement.

subject: Modern History
question_text: C Rajagopalachari led the Salt Satyagraha in which of the following states? [SSC CGL 20/08/2021]
solution:
(C) Tamil Nadu
In April 1930 in Tamil Nadu, C. Rajagopalachari organised a march from Thiruchirapalli (Trichinopoly) to Vedaranniyam on the Tanjore coast to break the salt law. Other leaders - K Kelappan (Malabar), Ambika Kant Sinha (Bihar), Gopalbandhu Chaudhary (Orissa), Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan (Peshawar), Rani Gaidinliu (Manipur and Nagaland).

subject: Modern History
question_text: On 8th April 1929, who among the following two revolutionaries threw a bomb in the Central Legislative Assembly? [SSC MTS 04/09/2023]

options.b: Bhagat Singh and Ram Prasad Bismil

solution:
(C) Bhagat Singh and Batukeshar Dutt
On 8 April 1929, Bhagat Singh and Batukeshwar Dutt threw a bomb at Central Legislative Assembly in Delhi to protest against the Public Safety Bill (1928) and Trade Dispute Bill. Motilal Nehru termed Public Safety Bill as 'the Slavery of India, Bill No.1' and said it was a direct attack on Indian Nationalism.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which great leader died due to his injuries in the Anti-Simon Commission Protest of 1928? [SSC CHSL 11/08/2023]
solution:
(A) Lala Lajpat Rai
In 1928, Lala Lajpat Rai led a protest against Simon Commission in Lahore and recited slogan 'Simon Go Back'. He died due to injuries sustained during lathi charge by police under James Scott during this protest. Lala Lajpat Rai is also known as 'Punjab Kesari'. He founded Servants of People Society (1921) in Lahore. Wrote book - Unhappy India, Arya Samaj, England's debt to India.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Hindustan Republican Association took to socialist ideas, under whose leadership, in 1928? [SSC CGL 30/06/2023]
solution:
(D) Chandra Sekhar Azad
Hindustan Republican Association (1924) was founded by Ram Prasad Bismil, J.C. Chatterjee, Sachin Sanyal. After the Kakori incident (1925), revolutionaries reorganised HRA and formed Hindustan Socialist Republican Association in 1928 under leadership of Chandra Shekhar Azad.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who among the following killed Michael O'Dwyer, the British officer responsible for the Jallianwala Bagh massacre? [SSC MTS 08/10/2021]
solution:
(B) Udham Singh
General Reginald Dwyer was responsible for Jallianwala Bagh Massacre (1919) in Amritsar. Udham Singh killed Michael O'Dwyer (Lt. Governor of Punjab in 1919) in London in 1940. Udham Singh used the name 'Ram Mohammad Singh Azad'.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which of the following Satyagrahas was started by Dr B R Ambedkar in 1927? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 13/10/2023]
solution:
(D) Mahad Satyagraha
Mahad Satyagraha (1927) was organised by Dr. B R Ambedkar to allow dalits to use water from the Chavdar tank in Mahad, Raigad (Maharashtra). It started on 20 March, celebrated as Social Empowerment Day in India. Ambedkar burnt Manusmriti during Mahad Satyagraha, it was a caste movement like that of Self Respect movement started by EV Ramaswamy Naicker.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who moved the resolution proposing that the National Flag of India be a 'horizontal tricolor of saffron, white and dark green in equal proportion'? [SSC MTS 11/09/2023]
solution:
(B) Jawaharlal Nehru
Jawaharlal Nehru moved the resolution on 22 July 1947 that the National Flag of India should be a horizontal tricolor of Saffron (top), white (middle) and dark green (bottom) with the Ashoka Chakra (24 spokes) in navy blue at centre. Flag was designed by Pingali Venkayya.

subject: Modern History
question_text: The origin of Directive Principles of State Policy can be traced to which of the following? [SSC CGL 18/07/2023]
solution:
(D) Karachi Resolution
The Directive Principles of State Policy was inspired Karachi Resolution (1931) adopted during INC session. Karachi Session (1931): Presided by Sardar Patel, Gandhi-Irwin pact was endorsed here. Congress agreed to participate in 2nd round table conference. Resolutions adopted on Fundamental Rights and Economic Policy.

subject: Modern History
question_text: At which of the following places did C. Rajagopalachari break the salt law in the year 1930? [SSC MTS 22/10/2021]
solution:
(A) Vedaranyam
C. Rajagopalachari led the Salt Satyagraha in Tamil Nadu as a part of Civil Disobedience movement. He started march from Trichinopoly to Vedaranyam and covered 240 km. C. Rajagopalachari - also known as Rajaji. Was the first and last Indian Governor-General of Independent India (1948 - 1950) after Mountbatten. Founded Swatantra Party in 1959. Received Bharat Ratna in 1954.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who among the following was one of the founders of the Congress Socialist Party? [SSC CHSL 20/10/2020]
solution:
(D) Ram Manohar Lohia
Congress Socialist Party was founded in 1934 by Ram Manohar Lohia, Jayaprakash Narayan, A N Dev, Minoo Masani. To promote socialism within the freedom movement. Ram Manohar Lohia advocated for Sapta Kranti. Jayaprakash Narayana launched Sampoorna Kranti or JP movement in Bihar in 1974. He was awarded Bharat Ratna in 1999.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who was the Viceroy of India when Mahatma Gandhi started Dandi March on 12 March 1930? [SSC CGL 12/09/2024]
solution:
(B) Lord Irwin
Dandi March was led by Mahatma Gandhi, he took 24 days to reach Dandi from Sabarmati Ashram. Lord Irwin (1926 - 1931) - Simon Commission came to India in 1928. Dandi March (1930). Civil Disobedience movement started. Gandhi-Irwin Pact in 1931. First Round Table Conference held in 1930-31.

subject: Modern History
question_text: The All-India Kisan Sabha was founded in Lucknow in April ________ with Swami Sahjanand Saraswati as the president. [SSC MTS 23/10/2024]
solution:
(D) 1936
All-India Kisan Sabha was founded in Lucknow in 1936 during INC session with Swami Sahjanand Saraswati as president. It was associated with Tebhaga Movement (Bengal), Telangana Rebellion, Bakasht Movement (Bihar).

subject: Modern History
question_text: Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel was the leader of which major peasant movement in 1928? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 11/12/2024]
solution:
(B) Bardoli Satyagraha
Bardoli Satyagraha (1928) - against increased land revenue by British, it was led by Vallabhbhai Patel. He got the title of Sardar in 1928 by the women participants in Bardoli Satyagraha. Jungle Satyagraha (1930) was led by Mahadev Ganesh Datar in Surkandi. Champaran Satyagraha (1917) and Dandi Satyagraha (1930) was led by Gandhi.

subject: Modern History
question_text: In which year, and with what aim, was the Simon Commission appointed? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 10/12/2024]

options.a: In 1927, to recommend a further reform of India's Constitution
options.b: In 1942, to enhance troop movements during the war
options.d: In 1939, to evaluate India's war preparedness

solution:
(A) In 1927, to recommend a further reform of India's Constitution
Simon Commission was appointed in 1927 by then PM of Britain Stanley Baldwin under John Simon to review the Government of India Act of 1919 and recommend further reforms. It arrived in India in 1928, was a 7 membered commission, all members were white which led to protest. Madras Session (1928) of INC: special session under presidency of M.A. Ansari. Decision to Boycott of Simon Commission.

subject: Modern History
question_text: In 1928, who among the following wrote the Congress Party's Nehru Report, a future constitution for independent India, based on the granting of dominion status? [SSC MTS 30/09/2024]
solution:
(C) Motilal Nehru
Nehru Report (1928) was drafted under chairmanship of Motilal Nehru. Demands - to end separate electorate. Proposed Dominion Status. Recommended fundamental rights and secularism. SC Bose, J L Nehru and S. Iyengar opposed dominion status and formed Indian Independence League in 1928. Lahore Session (1929) of INC, President - J L Nehru, demanded Purna Swaraj.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who among the following signed a pact with Lord Irwin on March 5, 1931? [SSC MTS 30/10/2024]
solution:
(C) Mahatma Gandhi
Gandhi-Irwin Pact was signed in 1931 between Gandhi and Viceroy Irwin. Gandhi's demands - release political prisoners, not convicted of violence. Right to produce salt for personal consumption. Irwin's demands - Suspend civil disobedience movement. Participate in 2nd round table conference.

subject: Modern History
question_text: The Simon Commission, appointed in 1927, was criticized in India because: [SSC GD 01/03/2024]

options.b: It did not include any Indian Members
options.c: It recommended the withdrawal of British rule from India.
options.d: It proposed to divide India into two separate countries

solution:
(B) It did not include any Indian Members
Simon Commission was a 7 membered body, all were white, it did not include any Indian. Indians revolted against Simon Commission with slogans 'Simon Go Back' coined by Yusuf Mehrally. Lala Lajpat Rai led the protest and was killed due to injuries sustained during lathi charge.

subject: Modern History
question_text: In which year was the Gandhi - Irwin Pact signed? [SSC GD 20/02/2024]
solution:
(D) 1931
Gandhi-Irwin pact was signed in 1931 between Gandhi and Viceroy Irwin. Gandhi's demands - release political prisoners, not convicted of violence. Right to produce salt for personal consumption. Irwin's demands - Suspend civil disobedience movement. Participate in 2nd round table conference.

subject: Modern History
question_text: In which year was the Dandi March by Mahatma Gandhi launched? [SSC GD 22/02/2024]
solution:
(C) 1930
Salt Satyagraha also known as Dandi March was led by Mahatma Gandhi in 1930 as a part of Civil Disobedience movement against the Salt tax. Gandhi marched 240 miles (385 km) with 78 delegates from Sabarmati Ashram (Ahmedabad) to Dandi (Navsari). It started on 12 March 1930 and ended on 6 April 1930.

subject: Modern History
question_text: The Simon Commission arrived in India in ________. [SSC GD 22/02/2024]
solution:
(C) 1928
Simon Commission was appointed in 1927 by then PM of Britain Stanley Baldwin under John Simon to review the Government of India Act of 1919 and recommend further reforms. It arrived in India in 1928, was a 7 membered commission, all members were white which led to protest. Madras Session (1928) of INC: special session under presidency of M.A. Ansari. Decision to Boycott of Simon Commission.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which of the following pacts suspended the Civil Disobedience Movement? [SSC MTS 18/10/2024]
solution:
(B) Gandhi-Irwin Pact
Gandhi-Irwin Pact was signed on 5 March 1931, it suspended Civil Disobedience movement and Gandhi agreed to attend second round table conference (1931) at London, it was the only round table conference attended by Gandhi. Rajah-Moonje pact was signed in 1932 by Hindu Mahasabha leaders. Sirima-Gandhi pact was signed between India and Sri Lanka in 1974. Liaquat-Nehru pact was signed in 1950 between India and Pakistan.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which revolutionary organisation was established at Feroz Shah Kotla in New Delhi in 1928? [SSC CHSL 10/07/2024]
solution:
(C) Hindustan Socialist Republican Association
Hindustan Socialist Republican Association (1928) was formed at Feroz Shah Kotla, Delhi by Chandrashekhar Azad, Bhagat Singh, Sukhdev. Bharat Naujawan Sabha was founded by Bhagat Singh in 1926. Swadesh Bandhab Samiti was formed in 1905 by Ashwini Kumar Dutta. Indian Home Rule Society was founded in 1905 by Shyamji Krishna Varma in London.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who has written the book "The Philosophy of the bomb"? [SSC GD 27/02/2024]
solution:
(C) Bhagwati Charan Vohra
Bhagwati Charan Vohra wrote - The Philosophy of the Bomb. He died in an explosion while testing a bomb. Bhagat Singh wrote - Why I Am An Atheist, To Young Political Workers, Letter to Punjab Governor. Satish Chandra Chatterjee wrote Pather Dabi.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which one of the following pairs of books and their authors is not correctly matched? [CAPF 2023]

options.b: The Philosophy of the Bomb: Bhagwati Charan Vohra

solution:
(C) Indian Unrest: Annie Besant
Bandi Jivan - Sachindranath Sanyal, The Philosophy of Bomb - Bhagwati Charan Vohra, Indian Unrest - Valentine Chirol, Desher Katha - Ganesh Deuskar. Valentine Chirol called B G Tilak, the father of Indian Unrest. Annie Besant wrote - The Case for India, India: A nation, New India (newspaper), The Seven Principles of Man.

subject: Modern History
question_text:
Which of the following statements are correct? [CAPF 2024]
1. The Third Round Table Conference met in London in November 1932 without the participation of Congress.
2. The discussions in the Third Round Table Conference led to the passing of the Government of India Act, 1935.
3. The Government of India Act, 1935 did not favor the idea of provincial autonomy.
4. The Governor was not given any power in the Act of 1935 to take over and run the administration of a province.
Select the answer using the code given below.
solution:
(B) 1 and 2
The third Round Table Conference was held in London in Nov 1932, without the participation of INC. It led to the passing of Government of India Act 1935. This act introduced the concept of provincial autonomy. The Governor was given the executive authority for administration of a Province on behalf of the Crown. B R Ambedkar attended all 3 round table conferences. 1st Round Table Conference held in 1930 - 1931. 2nd Round Table Conference held in 1931.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who among the following socialist activists persuaded Gandhiji not to restrict the salt march protest to men alone? [CDS I 2021]
solution:
(A) Kamaladevi Chattopadhyay
Kamaladevi Chattopadhyay persuaded Gandhi for allowing women in Salt Satyagraha and said to Gandhi 'Do not restrict men alone'. She participated in Salt March at Wadala. She was the first woman to be arrested during Salt March in South India and first women to run for legislature in India but lost.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which one of the following statements about the Gandhi-Irwin Pact of 1931 is correct? [CDS I 2021]

options.a: The Government would not release those prisoners who had been non-violent.
options.b: The Government would not release those prisoners who had indulged in the peaceful picketing of liquor and foreign cloth shops.
options.c: The Congress did not agree to the suspension of the Civil Disobedience Movement.
options.d: The Congress agreed to take part in the Second Round Table Conference.

solution:
(D) The Congress agreed to take part in the Second Round Table Conference.
The Gandhi-Irwin pact was signed in 1931. The British government agreed to release political prisoners except those involved in violence and agreed to release those prisoners indulged in peaceful picketing of liquor and foreign cloth shops. The Congress agreed to suspend Civil Disobedience movement and to take part in 2nd round table conference.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who among the following is the author of the book, Bandi Jivan? [CDS I 2023]
solution:
(D) Sachindranath Sanyal
Sachindranath Sanyal wrote - Bandi Jivan. Veer Savarkar wrote - Hindutva, The Indian War of Independence 1857, Letters from Andamans. Aruna Asaf Ali awarded Bharat Ratna in 1997. She wrote - Ideas of a Nation. She hoisted INC flag at Gowalia Tank in Mumbai in 1942.

subject: Modern History
question_text:
Which of the following statements is/are correct? [CDS I 2024]
1. During the Second Round Table Conference held in London in 1931, the British Government refused to accept the basic nationalist demand for freedom.
2. India was granted dominion status in 1932.
3. Viceroy Lord Willingdon adopted a policy of reconciliation with Congress.
4. The Civil Disobedience Movement was started with the breaking of salt laws.
Select the answer using the code given below.
solution:
(C) 1 and 4 only
Second Round Table Conference held in London in 1931, it was attended by Mahatma Gandhi, the British govt refused to accept the Indian demand for Independence. The Civil Disobedience Movement was formally started with the breaking of salt laws on April 6, 1930. India was not granted dominion status in 1932. Viceroy Willingdon is known for his repressive policies towards congress.

subject: Modern History
question_text: At the Second Round Table Conference, who among the following led the FICCI delegation? [CISF AC 2022]
solution:
(D) G.D. Birla and Thakurdas
At the Second Round Table Conference (1931) the Federation of Indian Chambers of Commerce and Industry (FICCI) was represented by G.D. Birla and Thakurdas. FICCI was established in 1927 by GD Birla and Purushottam Thakurdas. J.R.D. Tata is also known as Father of Indian Aviation. He was awarded Bharat Ratna in 1992.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who among the following headed the Indian States Committee formed in the year 1928? [CISF AC 2022]
solution:
(B) Sir Harcourt Butler
The Indian States Committee was formed in 1928, also known as Butler Committee. Headed by Sir Harcourt Butler. It was set up to examine the relationship between the princely states and British Government. Ian Copland wrote India 1885 - 1947: The Unmaking of an Empire.

subject: Modern History
question_text:
Which of the following was/were the founder member(s) of Hindustan Socialist Republican Army established in September, 1928? [CDS I 2022]
1. Bhagat Singh
2. Jatindranath
3. Ajoy Ghosh
4. Phanindranath Ghosh
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
solution:
(A) 1 only
After the Kakori Incident (1925), the Hindustan Republican Association (HRA) met at Firoz Shah Kotla, Delhi in September 1928. Under the leadership of Chandra Shekhar Azad, HRA was changed to Hindustan Socialist Republican Association or Army (HSRA). Bhagat Singh, Sukhdev, Bhagvati Charan Vohra, Bijoy Kumar Sinha etc. were participated in formation of HSRA.

subject: Modern History
question_text:
Consider the following statements about 'Dandi March': [CBI 2023]
1. The march was widely covered by the European and American press.
2. It was the first nationalist activity in which women participated in large numbers.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
solution:
(C) Both 1 and 2
Dandi March was started by Mahatma Gandhi on 12 March 1930. It was protest against the Salt tax monopoly of the British. It was extensively covered by the European and American Press. Women participated in large numbers, led by Sarojini Naidu and Kamaladevi Chattopadhyay.

subject: Modern History
question_text:
Which of the following statements about the resolution passed in the Karachi session of the Indian National Congress with regard to fundamental rights and economic policy is/are correct? [NDA II 2021]
1. It was opposed by Ambalal Sarabhai.
2. Its 20 points included demands for civil liberties and adult suffrage.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
solution:
(B) 2 only
The Karachi resolution was passed in Karachi Session (1931) of INC presided by Sardar Patel. Adopted resolutions on - Elections on the basis of Universal Adult Franchise, freedom of - speech, press, assembly, association were included in the 20 points put forward in the resolution. Ambalal Sarabhai was the president of Ahmedabad Mill owners Association, he supported these resolutions.

subject: Modern History
question_text: The Khudai Khidmatgar (Servants of the God) was organized by: [NDA I 2022]
solution:
(B) Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan
Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan also called Badshah Khan and Frontier Gandhi, organised Khudai Khidmatgars (servants of God) also known as Red-Shirts to support freedom struggle through non-violence. He started the first Pushto political monthly Pukhtoon. He led the Salt Satyagraha at Peshawar. He was the first non-Indian citizen to be awarded with Bharat Ratna in 1987.

subject: Modern History
question_text:
Consider the following historical events: [NDA I 2022]
1. Gandhi-Irwin Pact
2. Second Round Table Conference
3. Peasant Movement in Bardoli
4. Peasant Movement in Kheda
Which one of the following is the correct chronological order (starting from the earliest) of the above events?
solution:
(B) 4-3-1-2
Peasant Movement in Kheda or Kheda Satyagraha was led by Mahatma Gandhi in 1918. Bardoli Satyagraha was led by Sardar Patel in 1928. Gandhi-Irwin pact was signed on 5 March 1931. Congress decided to take part in 2nd Round Table Conference that held in London from sept to dec in 1931.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who among the following gave the famous 'Do or Die' slogan during Quit India Movement? [SSC CGL 21/07/2023]
solution:
(D) Mahatma Gandhi
Quit India Movement was launched on 8 August 1942 by Indian National Congress under Mahatma Gandhi's leadership in Bombay. Mahatma Gandhi gave the slogan of 'Do or Die' during a speech at Gowalia Tank Maidan in Bombay. This movement is also called the August Kranti.

subject: Modern History
question_text: In which of the following years, did Subhas Chandra Bose refer to Mahatma Gandhi as the "Father of the Nation"? [SSC CGL 12/12/2022]
solution:
(B) 1944
Subhas Chandra Bose also known as Netaji was the first person to give title of 'Father of the Nation' to Mahatma Gandhi through Singapore Radio in 1944. Slogans given by Bose - Give me Blood and I shall give you freedom, Jai Hind, and the famous call of 'Dilli Chalo'.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who among the following called Netaji Subhash Chandra Bose a patriot of patriots? [SSC CGL 03/08/2022]
solution:
(C) Mahatma Gandhi
Mahatma Gandhi called Netaji Subhash Chandra Bose as 'Patriot of Patriots'. In 1921, S.C. Bose returned to India quitting the Indian Civil Services. He reorganised Azad Hind Fauj (Free India Legion/Tiger Legion) in 1943 and later merged it with Indian National Army. S.C. Bose was born on 23 Jan 1897, in Cuttack, Odisha. 23 January is celebrated as Parakram Diwas.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who among the following freedom fighters hoisted the Indian flag at the Gowalia Tank Maidan in Mumbai during the Quit India Movement? [SSC CGL 13/04/2022]
solution:
(A) Aruna Asaf Ali
During the Quit India Movement (1942), Aruna Asaf Ali hoisted the Indian flag at Gowalia Tank Maidan (August Kranti Maidan) in Bombay. She is also known as Grand Old Lady of Independent India. She was awarded Bharat Ratna in 1997.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who among the following was the only martyr of the Quit India phase of the freedom movement who was hanged? [SSC CGL 21/04/2022]
solution:
(C) Kushal Konwar
Kushal Konwar was the only martyr during Quit India movement, he was hanged in 1943 in Jorhat Jail (Assam). Potti Sriramulu went on hunger strike and died after 56 days of fasting in 1952 for the formation of Andhra Pradesh for Telugu speaking people. Senapati Bapat started the Mulshi Satyagraha. Kanaiyalal Maneklal Munshi founded Bhartiya Vidhan Bhawan in 1938.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which act was passed by the British Government of India to introduce provincial autonomy? [SSC CHSL 16/03/2023]
solution:
(B) Government of India Act of 1935
The Government of India Act of 1935 was passed after the report of Simon Commission. It introduced provincial autonomy, Abolished dyarchy at provinces and introduced dyarchy at centre. 6 out of 11 provinces were given Bicameral Legislature. The act also proposed the formation of an All India Federation but it was not implemented. J.L. Nehru said "It [GOI Act 1935] was a new charter of bondage".

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which party formed the government in Britain in the year 1945? [SSC CGL 11/09/2024]
solution:
(A) Labour Party
In 1945, the Labour Party led by Clement Attlee formed the Government in Britain defeating Winston Churchill of the Conservative Party. Clement Attlee was elected as Prime Minister of Britain from 1945 - 1951.

subject: Modern History
question_text: In 1936, The Independent Labour Party was founded by ________. [SSC CGL 23/09/2024]
solution:
(C) BR Ambedkar
B.R. Ambedkar founded the Independent Labour Party in 1936 in Bombay to represent the interests of marginalised communities. Later in 1942, he dissolved this party and formed Scheduled Castes Federation (SCF) as its successor in 1942. Ambedkar formed the Republican Party of India in 1956.

subject: Modern History
question_text: In the 1939 Tripuri Congress Presidential Election, Pattabhi Sitaramayya was defeated by: [SSC MTS 13/09/2023]
solution:
(C) Subhash Chandra Bose
In 1939, Congress session held at Tripuri, Jabalpur (MP). Subhas Chandra Bose defeated Pattabhi Sitaramayya for the post of President of Tripuri session but due to Gandhi's protest, Bose resigned and Rajendra Prasad became president in 1939. Bose was the president of Haripur Session (1938) of congress.

subject: Modern History
question_text: In which session of All India Congress Committee was the Quit India Resolution accepted? [SSC CHSL 03/08/2023]
solution:
(D) Bombay
On August 8, 1942, the All India Congress Committee in its session at Gowaliya Tank, Bombay passed Quit India Resolution, moved by J L Nehru in response to Cripps mission. Gandhi gave the calls for 'Do or Die'. On first day all leaders were arrested and Aruna Asaf Ali presided the Congress Working Committee. Quit India movement was not supported by Hindu Mahasabha and Muslim League.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Muslim League observed ________ as 'Direct Action Day'. [SSC MTS 18/07/2022]
solution:
(A) 16 August 1946
On 16 August 1946, Muslim League under Mohammad Ali Jinnah directed Muslims to observe Direct Action Day to push their demand for a separate nation. It resulted in widespread violence and riots. Major riots broke out in Noakhali (Calcutta) known as Calcutta Killings. Gandhi was in Noakhali, when the whole country was celebrating Independence day in 15 Aug 1947.

subject: Modern History
question_text: In ________ the Muslim League had moved a resolution demanding "Independent States" for Muslims in the north-western and eastern areas of the country. [SSC CHSL 25/05/2022]
solution:
(A) 1940
In 1940, Muslim league passed a resolution demanding the creation of Independent States for Muslims in north-western and north-eastern areas. This resolution is known as Lahore Resolution or Pakistan Resolution. It was drafted by Sikandar Hayat Khan and was presented by A.K. Fazlul Haq under the leadership of Mohammad Ali Jinnah.

subject: Modern History
question_text: When did the Drafting Committee publish the first draft of the Indian Constitution? [SSC CGL 17/07/2023]
solution:
(D) February 1948
The Drafting Committee was formed on 29 August 1947. It was a 7 membered committee under the chairmanship of Dr. BR Ambedkar. It submitted the first Draft constitution of India to the President of the assembly in February 1948. The final draft of Constitution was adopted on Nov 26, 1949.

subject: Modern History
question_text: The All India Forward Bloc was founded by ________ in 1939 after resigning from the Congress Presidentship. [SSC CGL 21/07/2023]
solution:
(D) Subhash Chandra Bose
Subhash Chandra Bose was elected president of Tripuri Session of INC in 1939. He resigned as its president and formed All India Forward Block (1939) in Unnao (UP) within the congress. Bose met Hitler under the pseudo name, Orlando Mazzotta. He proclaimed about the formation of Provisional Government of Free India on 21 October 1943, Singapore and formed the Provisional Government of Free India in 1943.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Where was the first All India Kisan Sabha formed? [SSC CGL 21/07/2023]
solution:
(A) Lucknow
All India Kisan Sabha was formed in 1936 under the leadership of Swami Sahajanand Saraswati during Lucknow Session (1936) of Congress presided by Jawaharlal Nehru. It was associated with the Communist Party of India (1925) by MN Roy.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who among the following said that "English education has enslaved us"? [SSC CGL 30/06/2023]
solution:
(B) Mahatma Gandhi
Mahatma Gandhi criticized English education and said 'English education has enslaved us'. Macaulay's Minute on Indian Education in 1835, is credited for English education in India. Wood's Dispatch (1854) is known as the Magna Carta of English education in India.

subject: Modern History
question_text: The Constituent Assembly of India was set up in which year? [SSC GD 22/02/2024]
solution:
(C) 1946
The British PM, Clement Atlee send 3 membered commission of Stafford Cripps, AV Alexander and Prethick Lawrence (Chairman) in 1946. This commission established a Constituent Assembly in 1946 for making of constitution. This assembly first met on 9 Dec 1946 in Delhi. MN Roy was the first to demand for Constituent Assembly in 1934.

subject: Modern History
question_text: When the British rule ended in India in the year 1947 the literacy rate was just ________. [SSC MTS 03/05/2023]
solution:
(D) 12 percent
When the British rule ended in India in 1947 the literacy rate was just 12 percent. According to 2011 census of India, the overall literacy rate is 73 percent and literacy rate of men is 80.9 percent and women is 65.46 percent.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who founded Trinamool Congress in 1998 and became its Chairperson after separating from the Indian National Congress? [SSC CHSL 10/03/2023]
solution:
(D) Mamata Banerjee
Mamata Banerjee founded the All India Trinamool Congress in 1998 after breaking from Indian National Congress. She became the Chief Minister of Bengal in 2011 ending the 34 years left rule and was re-elected in 2016 and 2021. Jayaram Jayalalithaa, also known as 'Amma' or Puratchi Thalaivi, was a Tamil actress and was the CM of Tamil Nadu.

subject: Modern History
question_text: The first President of the All India Trade Union Congress was ________. [SSC CPO 11/11/2022]
solution:
(B) Lala Lajpat Rai
All India Trade Union Congress (AITUC) was founded in 1920 by Lala Lajpat Rai, Joseph Baptista, NM Joshi, Diwan Chamanlal and others in Bombay. Lala Lajpat Rai was its first president. He is also known as Punjab Kesari (Lion of Punjab).

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who among the following freedom fighters of India was assassinated? [SSC MTS 18/07/2022]
solution:
(B) Mahatma Gandhi
On 30 January 1948, Mahatma Gandhi was shot dead by Nathuram Godse at a prayer meeting in Birla Mansion (New Delhi). Jawaharlal Nehru, first Prime Minister of India was also the first PM who died in office in 1964.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who among the following Indian freedom fighters was fondly called Gandhi Buri (Old Lady Gandhi) for her dedication towards Gandhian principles? [SSC CGL 21/04/2022]
solution:
(D) Matangini Hazra
Matangini Hazra was also called Gandhi Buri (Old Lady Gandhi). She was an old peasant widow in Tamluk (Midnapore), was killed in violence in 1942 while holding national flag in hand, when the Sutahata police-station was captured.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which leader gave us the C-R Formula? [SSC CGL 06/03/2020]
solution:
(A) C.Rajagopalachari
C. Rajagopalachari gave us the C-R formula also known as Rajaji formula in 1944. To resolve the deadlock between Indian National Congress and Muslim League. During World War II, it proposed Muslim league to endorse congress's demand of independence and then the Muslim majority province of North-West and North-East India will go for plebiscite.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who among the following was one of the speakers after Jawaharlal Nehru to address the Parliament on the midnight of 15 August 1947? [SSC CPO 23/11/2020]
solution:
(D) Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan
The Constituent Assembly met at midnight of 15 August 1947 under presidency of Rajendra Prasad. There were three main speakers at Central Hall of Parliament, Jawaharlal Nehru, Chaudhary Khaliquzaman and Dr. Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan. Jawaharlal Nehru delivered his famous 'Tryst With Destiny' speech.

subject: Modern History
question_text: The princely state of Hyderabad was brought under the Indian Union in the year ________. [SSC CPO 24/11/2020]
solution:
(C) 1948
Hyderabad State signed standstill agreement with India in 1947. Operation Polo (1948) also known as Police Action was launched against Razakar forces in Sept 1948 to annexe Hyderabad into Indian Union. The last Nizam of Hyderabad was Nizam Osman Ali Khan. Operation Vijay (1961) - to liberate Goa from the control of Portuguese.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who was the chairman of the Economic Programme Committee (EPC) that was formed by the All India Congress Committee in 1947? [SSC CHSL 26/10/2020]
solution:
(B) Jawaharlal Nehru
The All India Congress Committee formed an Economic Programme Committee (EPC) in 1947 under presidency of Jawaharlal Nehru. The National Planning Commission was formed in March 1950. In 2015, the Planning Commission was replaced by NITI Aayog i.e. National Institution for Transforming India.

subject: Modern History
question_text: In which year did Cripps Mission arrive in India? [SSC GD 23/02/2024]
solution:
(C) 1942
Cripps Mission by Stafford Cripps arrived in India in March 1942 during World War II. It proposed: formation of constituent assembly, Indian Union with Dominion Status, acceptance of new Constitution, continued British authority. Gandhi rejected Dominion Status stating it as a 'post dated cheque on a failing bank'.

subject: Modern History
question_text: The 'Quit India Movement' was launched under the leadership of ________. [SSC MTS 13/11/2024]
solution:
(A) Mahatma Gandhi
Quit India Movement was launched on 8 August 1942 by Indian National Congress under Mahatma Gandhi's leadership in Bombay. Mahatma Gandhi gave the slogan of 'Do or Die' during a speech at Gowalia Tank Maidan in Bombay. This movement is also called the August Kranti.

subject: Modern History
question_text:
Consider the following statements: [CAPF 2021]
1. The growth of socialist tendency in the national movement led to the foundation of the Congress Socialist Party in 1934 under the leadership of Acharya Narendra Deva and Jayaprakash Narayan.
2. In 1936, Jawaharlal Nehru urged the Congress to accept socialism as its goal and to bring itself closer to the peasantry and the working class.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
solution:
(C) Both 1 and 2
Congress Socialist Party in 1934 under the leadership of Acharya Narendra Deva and Jayaprakash Narayan, Ram Manohar Lohia, Minoo Masani. All India Kisan Sabha Party was established by Sahjanand Saraswati in Lucknow session 1936. Jawaharlal Nehru presided over the session.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which one of the following statements about 'Princely States' in India is not correct? [CAPF 2022]

options.a: The British Government declared that States were free to join either India or Pakistan or remain independent.
options.b: The decision to join either India or Pakistan or remain independent was left not to the people of Princely States but to the Princely Rulers.
options.c: The Ruler of Travancore first decided to remain independent.
options.d: The State of Travancore finally joined India through a plebiscite.

solution:
(D) The State of Travancore finally joined India through a plebiscite
The state signed the Instrument of Accession and joined the Indian Union on July 30, 1947. The then Diwan was Sir C.P. Ramaswami Iyer. Jammu and Kashmir - Maharaja Hari Singh also signed the Instrument of Accession, on 26 October 1947.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which one of the following statements about the Cripps Mission is not correct? [CDS II 2021]

options.a: It was sent to India by the British Government in March 1942.
options.b: It proposed that the Constitution of India was to be framed by an elected Constituent Assembly of the Indian people.
options.c: Any province which was not prepared to accept the Constitution would be free to retain its constitutional existing at that time. provisions.
options.d: The British Government could enter into a separate constitutional arrangement with any of the acceding provinces.

solution:
(D) The British Government could enter into a separate constitutional arrangement with any of the acceding provinces
Winston Churchill was the Prime Minister of Britain in 1942 when Stafford Cripps was sent to India to secure Indian support during World War II. This event is known as the Cripps Mission. Gandhi famously rejected the proposal, stating that Cripps' offer of Dominion Status after the war was "a post-dated cheque drawn on a failing bank".

subject: Modern History
question_text: In the election held in 1937, in which two provinces was the Indian National Congress not able to emerge as the single largest party? [CDS I 2023]
solution:
(A) Punjab and Sind
In the 1937 provincial elections held under the Government of India Act 1935, the Indian National Congress emerged as the single largest party and formed ministries in most provinces. Congress got majority of 716 out of 1161 seats.

subject: Modern History
question_text:
Consider the following statements: [CDS I 2023]
After forming the Indian National Army, Subhas Chandra Bose
1. Recruited a large number of soldiers from the Indian Prisoners of War in Japanese camp.
2. Introduced a women's detachment named after the Rani of Jhansi.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
solution:
(C) Both 1 and 2
Indian National Army was founded by Mohan Singh in Singapore 1942. Subash Chandra Bose introduced a women regiment named Rani of Jhansi regiment led by Captain Lakshmi Swaminathan.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who among the following is the author of the book 'The Indian Struggle 1920-1942'? [CDS II 2023]
solution:
(B) Subhas Chandra Bose
Subas Chandra Bose, on December 30 1943, became the first person to hoist the Indian national flag on Indian soil. The flag was hoisted in the Port Blair [Andaman & Nicobar Islands], which had been liberated from British control. Bose also renamed the Andaman and Nicobar Islands as "Shaheed" (Martyr) and "Swaraj" (Self-rule).

subject: Modern History
question_text: "...the immediate ending of British rule in India is an urgent necessity..." was a resolution of: [CISF AC 2022]
solution:
(B) Quit India Movement
The Quit India Resolution was adopted in response to the failure of the Cripps Mission (1942). The Individual Satyagraha, a nonviolent protest movement launched by Mahatma Gandhi in 1940 was initiated in response to the August Offer proposed by Viceroy Lord Linlithgow.

subject: Modern History
question_text:
Consider the following events leading to the Indian Independence: [CDS I 2024]
1. Lucknow Pact
2. Return of Subhash Chandra Bose to India
3. Cripps Mission
4. Naval Mutiny in Bombay
Select the chronological sequence of these events (earliest to latest) from the code given below:
solution:
(C) 1, 2, 3, 4
Lucknow Pact (1916) : agreement between the Indian National Congress and the All-India Muslim League. Return of Subhas Chandra Bose to India (1921) / (1934). Cripps Mission (1942). Naval Mutiny in Bombay (1946) : Also known as the Royal Indian Navy Mutiny. The mutiny began at HMIS Talwar, a shore establishment in Bombay.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Which one among the following districts of Eastern India held a referendum in 1947 to decide whether or not to join Pakistan? [NDA I 2023]
solution:
(A) Sylhet
In 1947, a referendum was held in Sylhet District part of Assam Province of British India to decide whether or not to join Pakistan. The majority voted in favour of joining East Pakistan (Present day - Bangladesh).

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who did not accompany Gandhiji during his visit to the NWFP in October, 1938? [CISF AC 2022]
solution:
(C) Jawaharlal Nehru
Sushila Nayar was Gandhi's personal doctor, Amtus Salam was a follower of Gandhi and Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan also known as Frontier Gandhi or Badshah khan, was a prominent leader of North-west frontier province [NWFP].

subject: Modern History
question_text:
Consider the following statements about the Quit India' Resolution of 1942: [CISF AC 2024]
1. Its text was carefully drafted to make it clear that it put India among the countries at war with the Axis Powers and fully aligned with the Allied Powers (United Nations).
2. It called upon Britain to immediately hand over power to an Indian Provisional Government composed of representatives of all sections of the population.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
solution:
(C) Both 1 and 2
The Quit India Resolution was passed by the Congress Working Committee in July 1942 and was formally adopted on 8 August 1942 at the Gowalia Tank Maidan in Bombay. The resolution called for the British to "withdraw from India" and demanded the formation of a provisional government.

subject: Modern History
question_text: With reference to 8th August, 1942 in Indian history, which one of the following statements is correct? [IAS 2021]

options.a: The Quit India Resolution was adopted by the AICC.
options.b: The Viceroy's Executive Council was expanded to include more Indians.
options.c: The Congress ministries resigned in seven provinces.
options.d: Cripps proposed an Indian Union with full Dominion Status once the Second World War was over.

solution:
(A) The Quit India Resolution was adopted by the AICC
The Quit India Resolution was moved by Jawaharlal Nehru, called for an immediate end to British rule in India. In his address, Mahatma Gandhi urged the nation with the stirring call: "Do or Die".

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who among the following is associated with 'Songs from Prison', a translation of ancient Indian religious lyrics in English? [IAS 2021]
solution:
(C) Mohandas Karamchand Gandhi
"Songs from Prison" is a collection of ancient Indian religious lyrics translated into English by Mahatma Gandhi during his imprisonment in Yeravda Jail (Pune) in 1930. In 1931, Gandhi undertook a fast unto death to protest against the Communal Award, which eventually led to the signing of the Poona Pact.

subject: Modern History
question_text:
With reference to the proposals of Cripps Mission, consider the following statements: [IAS 2022]
1. The Constituent Assembly would have members nominated by the Provincial Assemblies as well as the Princely States.
2. Any Province, which is not prepared to accept the new Constitution would have the right to sign a separate agreement with Britain regarding its future status.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
solution:
(B) 2 only
The Cripps Mission proposed that the Constituent Assembly would comprise members elected by the Provincial Assemblies through Proportional representation and members nominated by the rulers of the Princely states.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Freedom fighter Kanaklata Barua was martyred in: [NDA II 2023]
solution:
(B) Quit India Movement
Kanaklata Barua was a young freedom fighter, from Assam who was martyred in 1942 during the Quit India Movement. Aruna Asaf Ali [also known as Grand Old Lady] is a prominent figure in the same movement.

subject: Modern History
question_text:
Which of the following statements about Mahatma Gandhi's anti-untouchability campaign is/are correct? [NDA II 2021]
1. The All India Harijan Sevak Sangh was founded for this purpose.
2. The campaign was to root out untouchability, since it did not enjoy the sanction of the Hindu shastras.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
solution:
(C) Both 1 and 2
In 1932, Mahatma Gandhi founded the All India Anti-Untouchability League, which was later renamed the Harijan Sevak Sangh, to work for the upliftment of the Depressed Classes. Then in 1933, Gandhi launched the weekly publication Harijan to further support this cause.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Where did Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose announce the formation of the Government of Free India in 1943? [NDA II 2022]
solution:
(A) Singapore
The first headquarters of the Indian National Army was established in Singapore. It was initially formed in 1942 by Mohan Singh. In 1943, Subhas Chandra Bose restructured the INA. He was also the first person to address Mahatma Gandhi as the "Father of the Nation".

subject: Modern History
question_text:
Which of the following statements about the Unionist Party is/are correct? [NDA II 2022]
1. It was a political party representing the interests of landholders in Punjab.
2. It was opposed to the idea of the partition of India as India and Pakistan.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
solution:
(A) 1 only
The Unionist Party was established in 1923 by Sir Fazli Husain, Sir Chotu Ram and Sir Sikandar Hayat Khan. It was a political party that represented the interests of landholders in Punjab across communal lines. In 1937 Sikandar Hayat Khan entered into an agreement with Jinnah known as Sikandar-Jinnah Pact, aligning Unionist party with the All India Muslim League.

subject: Modern History
question_text: Who among the following was responsible for demarcating the borders between India and Pakistan during the partition in 1947? [CDS I 2025]
solution:
(A) Cyril Radcliffe
The British appointed Boundary Commission under Cyril Radcliffe to draw the boundaries between India and Pakistan in 1947. The border that separates India and Pakistan is known as Radcliffe line. Durand Commission (1893) was setup to define Durand line between India and Afghanistan. McMahon line is the border between India (Arunachal Pradesh) and China (Tibet) in 1914 by Henry McMahon during Shimla Convention. Balfour Declaration (1917) - support for a Jewish homeland in Palestine.

subject: Modern History
question_text:
Chronologically arrange the following political events related to Indian National Movement beginning from the earliest: [NDA I 2025]
1. Formation of Swaraj Party
2. Communal Award
3. Lucknow Pact
4. Simla Conference
Select the answer using the code given below.
solution:
(A) 3, 1, 2, 4
Lucknow Pact (1916) was signed between Indian National Congress and All India Muslim League during Lucknow Session (1916) of INC presided by Ambika Charan Majumdar. Swaraj Party was formed in 1923 after the Gaya Session of INC in 1922 by C.R. Das and Motilal Nehru. Communal Award was brought by British PM Ramsay Macdonald in 1932, it gave separate electorate to Depressed Classes. Shimla Conference (1945) was attended by leaders of INC, Muslim League, Sikhs to discuss the Wavell Plan under Viceroy Wavell, which proposed to reconstitute Viceroy's Executive Council.
